name: CLAMM Integration to Meteora
description: CLAMMs (Concentrated Liquidity Automated Market Makers), by design, often hold a significant amount of trapped liquidity that isn't actively used for asset swaps. This results in a substantial portion of liquidity going unused, missing out on potential opportunities to generate yields.
possible_solution:
A CLAMM that takes the idle capital not used in the pool and deposits to earn yield unless needed to power a swap. Meteora offers a solution by enabling users and integrated protocols to deposit their funds into the Dynamic Vaults Pool. These deposited funds are then allocated to leading lending protocols such as Marginfi and Solend, generating yields.

AMMs can further enhance their liquidity by channeling their underutilized liquidity into Meteora's Dynamic Vaults. This strategic move can amplify the overall yield for their Liquidity Providers (LPs). The liquidity from CLAMM pools would be directed into the dynamic vaults. With the additional yield, CLAMMs can significantly optimize their pool capital efficiency, thereby reducing the reliance on Liquidity Mining (LM) incentives for sustaining or expanding pool liquidity. 

Noteworthy AMMs in focus include Orca, Raydium, and Lifinity.
resources: \- Meteora Docs - <https://docs.meteora.ag/>

name: Private DeFi
description: Privacy holds paramount significance in the realm of DeFi as it is imperative for institutional participation, wherein trade confidentiality is pivotal. Additionally, privacy is sought by retail traders seeking discreet execution of their trades.
possible_solution:
Unquestionably, DeFi represents the foremost use case for privacy, given its pivotal role in shaping the future of finance. There are several domains within DeFi where privacy can be harnessed:
\- Private Swapping and Staking: Offering the ability to engage in swapping and staking activities while ensuring transaction privacy.
\- Prading on-chain via DEXs: Enabling trading on decentralized exchanges without exposing one's profit and loss data or trading strategies to others.
\- Depositing in a Money Market and LP'ing: Facilitating deposits into money markets and liquidity provision with enhanced privacy.
\- On-chain Dark Pools: Creating on-chain dark pools for private trading.
\- Private Token Vesting: Enabling private token vesting transactions.
\- Specialized Private Wallet: Developing a dedicated private wallet tailored for interactions within the DeFi ecosystem.
resources: \- Light Protocol Documentation - <https://docs.lightprotocol.com>

name: Decentralized Rideshare and Logistics
description:
Building a Web3 version of Uber or Doordash, leveraging crypto-economic protocols. 
For a decentralized Uber, Drivers get control, ownership, and voting rights on the network, while Riders can get cars cheaper and faster through smart price segmentation.
possible_solution:
What does Web3 solve: The Web3 Flywheel 
1\. Token rewards incentivize supply-side participants (Eg. Uber Drivers) to deploy cabs.
2\. As the network’s supply side grows, devs, product builders, and end users are attracted.
3\. Fees generated from end users utilizing a network’s services attract more service providers.
4\. Protocol token models create a relationship between network usage and token price. 

Overall, it helps in the following: 
1\. Superior unit economics: reduces CapE and OpEx 
2\. Embedded financial systems: frictionless P2P payments 
3\. Web3 native: leverages composable tools and apps 
4\. Reduced barriers to entry: crowdsourced hardware, collective ownership via token rewards

name: Chess Puzzle App
description:
People want to get better at chess but struggle with consistency. Services like Chess.com, Chess24, LiChess, Chessable offer a range of courses, training tools, etc with daily streaks and other motivational features. 

Maybe less is more when it comes to discipline.
possible_solution:
An app that offers exactly one chess puzzle per day, of a random difficulty.
\- You get three attempts. It issues your best attempt with a photo of the board, your move, the result, & time taken as a cNFT. 
\- To sign up users just verify their wallet registered with the Seed vault

The app should minimise the number of times a user needs to interact with or approve wallet txn by using concepts like Gum session keys. 
In future versions, the app could customise the puzzle based on your displayed performance (the better you are, the harder the puzzles), offer PvP mode, etc. 
Issuing performance on-chain with the right metadata starts creating an ELO equivalent on-chain which could seed new spontaneous chess clubs, tournaments and more by creating an open & reachable list of chess enthusiasts and their skill level.
resources:
\- Picoplay - <https://picoplay.fun/>
\- Chess puzzle games - <https://github.com/topics/chess-puzzle>
\- Solana Mobile Stack - <https://solanamobile.com/developers> 
\- Compression Docs - <https://docs.metaplex.com/programs/compression/>

An app that offers exactly one chess puzzle per day, of a random difficulty. - You get three attempts. name: Reusable Txn Examples for Signing from Custodial Wallets
description: Users of custodial wallet providers like Fireblocks, often face timeout on conventional transactions. Solving this is essential for the UX of institutional clients who use solutions like Fireblocks.
possible_solution: Create a set of reusable examples for creating transactions that can be signed by custodial services like Fireblocks. Submissions can optionally make use of nonce accounts for multisig situations but can dive deeper into other possible solutions.Another choice would be probably to create the nonce account in a regular transaction and then use it as needed for durable transactions. The creation of a nonce account is unlikely to time out.
resources:
\- Offline Transaction Signing - <https://docs.solana.com/offline-signing>
\- Creating nonce account as a durable transaction - <https://solana.stackexchange.com/questions/4404/creating-nonce-account-as-a-durable-transaction>
\- Fireblocks API - <https://docs.fireblocks.com/api/>

name: Bitcoin Mining Micropayments Pool on Solana
description: Bitcoin mining pools currently have high minimum payout thresholds (typically 0.001 BTC) due to Bitcoin's high transaction fees and Lightning Network limitations. This creates significant barriers for small-scale miners operating devices like Bitaxes (1-8 TH/s hashrate) who contribute fewer shares and rarely reach minimum payout thresholds. As a result, small miners either solo mine with virtually no chance of success or are excluded from pool mining entirely, leading to centralization where only major players can effectively participate in mining pools. This discourages home miners and reduces Bitcoin network decentralization.
possible_solution: Create a Bitcoin mining pool that leverages Solana's low-cost infrastructure and Zeus Network's Bitcoin bridge technology. The solution involves: 1) Forking Bitaxe OS to accept Solana wallet addresses from miners, 2) Building a mining pool on Apollo BTC portal infrastructure, 3) When the pool successfully mines a Bitcoin block, converting the BTC rewards to $zbtc (wrapped Bitcoin on Solana), 4) Distributing proportional rewards as Solana-based micropayments to miners based on their contributed shares, regardless of hashrate size. This eliminates minimum payout thresholds and enables true micropayments for any contribution level, making pool mining accessible to all miners while enhancing Bitcoin DeFi activity on Solana.
resources:
<https://github.com/skot/bitaxe>

<https://zeusnetwork.xyz/developers/orpheus/4-bitcoin-app/>
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Bitcoin mining pools currently have high minimum payout thresholds (typically 0.001 BTC) due to Bitcoin's high transaction fees and Lightning Network limitations. This creates significant barriers for small-scale miners operating devices like Bitaxes (1-8 TH/s hashrate) who contribute fewer shares and rarely reach minimum payout thresholds. As a result, small miners either solo mine with virtually no chance of success or are excluded from pool mining entirely, leading to centralization where only major players can effectively participate in mining pools. This discourages home miners and reduces Bitcoin network decentralization.

Possible Solution:

Create a Bitcoin mining pool that leverages Solana's low-cost infrastructure and Zeus Network's Bitcoin bridge technology. The solution involves: 1) Forking Bitaxe OS to accept Solana wallet addresses from miners, 2) Building a mining pool on Apollo BTC portal infrastructure, 3) When the pool successfully mines a Bitcoin block, converting the BTC rewards to $zbtc (wrapped Bitcoin on Solana), 4) Distributing proportional rewards as Solana-based micropayments to miners based on their contributed shares, regardless of hashrate size. This eliminates minimum payout thresholds and enables true micropayments for any contribution level, making pool mining accessible to all miners while enhancing Bitcoin DeFi activity on Solana.

name: Provenance of Digital Assets
description: The digital art and music industry lacks a comprehensive solution to track and preserve the history of digital assets. Currently, there is no reliable way to authenticate views or listens to digital pieces, and artists and musicians often struggle to establish a clear provenance trail for their work. This absence of transparency and accountability hinders the value and context of digital assets, leaving creators without a means to showcase audience engagement.
possible_solution:
Introducing a platform that employs NFTs to meticulously track and document the history of digital assets. Each time someone views or listens to a piece, a unique NFT is minted, creating an immutable record of interactions. This process addresses provenance concerns and offers several benefits:

This platform benefits artists and musicians by generating a provable trail of engagement with their work. It enhances the value of their creations by showcasing real-time interactions and contextualizing their journey. This solution caters to artists, musicians, collectors, and individuals invested in the history and context of digital assets. Artists gain a stronger connection with their audience, while collectors and enthusiasts enjoy a heightened understanding of the asset's journey.
resources:
\- Underdog Documentation - <https://underdog.readme.io/reference/getting-started-1>
\- Metaplex Docs - <https://docs.metaplex.com/>

name: Aeda Wallet
description:
The idea behind Aeda aims to solve the deep inefficiencies in cross-border money movement, especially in the EU–EECA corridor. Today, sending money across borders is slow, expensive, and complicated. Transfers often involve multiple intermediaries such as correspondent banks, SWIFT, and money transfer operators, which results in high fees, hidden foreign exchange markups, and settlement delays of several days. In the EU–EECA corridor, sanctions and de-risking have further disrupted payment rails, leaving users with limited options, reduced provider coverage, and poor digital access.
For everyday users, this means they often pay €15–20 just to send a €200 remittance, wait several days for settlement, and sometimes still need to visit a bank branch or post office to complete the transaction. Digital solutions that do exist are either one-directional, lack local integration, or require users to navigate complex crypto platforms that less than 5% of the population knows how to use. Even when money arrives digitally, recipients face the additional problem of limited spendability, since merchants in the region rarely accept cross-border or crypto payments, forcing cash-outs with extra fees.
Freelancers, SMEs, and businesses face similar challenges: high fees for cross-border payouts and invoices, long settlement times, chargeback risks, and repetitive compliance checks. The fragmented KYC/AML processes add more friction and slow down access to funds.
In short, people need a way to send and receive money that is instant, affordable, and available 24/7 on a smartphone, while businesses and banks need compliant and modern rails to transact seamlessly across borders. Aeda addresses this problem by replacing intermediary-heavy and outdated rails with stablecoin rails, while delivering the simplicity and familiarity of a banking app. This makes global money movement effortless, affordable, and spendable from day one.
possible_solution:
Our proposed solution is a non-custodial blockchain-powered wallet that uses stablecoin rails to make cross-border money movement instant, effortless, and affordable. Aeda Wallet looks and feels like a traditional banking app but is powered by Web3 infrastructure, allowing users to send, receive, and spend money anywhere in the world using only a smartphone.
The wallet enables peer-to-peer transfers with near-zero fees and instant settlement, removing the dependency on correspondent banks, SWIFT, and costly intermediaries. It also integrates with fiat on/off-ramp providers to ensure funds can be easily deposited or withdrawn in local currencies. For merchants, Aeda provides the ability to accept stablecoin payments directly, opening new opportunities for digital commerce and reducing reliance on outdated card networks.
In addition, Aeda is designed as a scalable platform: beyond retail users, it will expand to serve freelancers, SMEs, and banks through B2B cross-border payments and white-label solutions. By combining regulatory compliance, simple user experience, and blockchain efficiency, Aeda delivers a trusted and future-ready payment platform that bridges traditional finance and Web3.
resources: https://drive.google.com/file/d/15\_J2laLzH4XQj1Dc3WZ0ATKZ4ZMkLcCE/view?usp=drive\_link
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

The idea behind Aeda aims to solve the deep inefficiencies in cross-border money movement, especially in the EU–EECA corridor. Today, sending money across borders is slow, expensive, and complicated. Transfers often involve multiple intermediaries such as correspondent banks, SWIFT, and money transfer operators, which results in high fees, hidden foreign exchange markups, and settlement delays of several days. In the EU–EECA corridor, sanctions and de-risking have further disrupted payment rails, leaving users with limited options, reduced provider coverage, and poor digital access. For everyday users, this means they often pay €15–20 just to send a €200 remittance, wait several days for settlement, and sometimes still need to visit a bank branch or post office to complete the transaction. Digital solutions that do exist are either one-directional, lack local integration, or require users to navigate complex crypto platforms that less than 5% of the population knows how to use. Even when money arrives digitally, recipients face the additional problem of limited spendability, since merchants in the region rarely accept cross-border or crypto payments, forcing cash-outs with extra fees. Freelancers, SMEs, and businesses face similar challenges: high fees for cross-border payouts and invoices, long settlement times, chargeback risks, and repetitive compliance checks. The fragmented KYC/AML processes add more friction and slow down access to funds. In short, people need a way to send and receive money that is instant, affordable, and available 24/7 on a smartphone, while businesses and banks need compliant and modern rails to transact seamlessly across borders. Aeda addresses this problem by replacing intermediary-heavy and outdated rails with stablecoin rails, while delivering the simplicity and familiarity of a banking app. This makes global money movement effortless, affordable, and spendable from day one.

Possible Solution:

Our proposed solution is a non-custodial blockchain-powered wallet that uses stablecoin rails to make cross-border money movement instant, effortless, and affordable. Aeda Wallet looks and feels like a traditional banking app but is powered by Web3 infrastructure, allowing users to send, receive, and spend money anywhere in the world using only a smartphone. The wallet enables peer-to-peer transfers with near-zero fees and instant settlement, removing the dependency on correspondent banks, SWIFT, and costly intermediaries. It also integrates with fiat on/off-ramp providers to ensure funds can be easily deposited or withdrawn in local currencies. For merchants, Aeda provides the ability to accept stablecoin payments directly, opening new opportunities for digital commerce and reducing reliance on outdated card networks. In addition, Aeda is designed as a scalable platform: beyond retail users, it will expand to serve freelancers, SMEs, and banks through B2B cross-border payments and white-label solutions. By combining regulatory compliance, simple user experience, and blockchain efficiency, Aeda delivers a trusted and future-ready payment platform that bridges traditional finance and Web3.

author: Solana Foundation

name: ZK-KYC Gateway for Cross-Chain Compliance
description: Financial institutions and DeFi protocols struggle with **repetitive KYC/AML checks** when users interact across multiple blockchains. Each chain requires redundant verification, exposing sensitive data and increasing compliance costs. Meanwhile, users face privacy risks, and projects lack a standardized, trustless way to share compliance status without compromising user anonymity or fragmenting liquidity.
possible_solution:
A **zero-knowledge proof (ZKP)-based KYC gateway** on Solana that:
1. **Issues reusable ZK-KYC certificates** after initial verification (e.g., proof of residency, accredited investor status).
2. **Allows cross-chain dApps** (e.g., DeFi, NFT platforms) to _cryptographically verify compliance_ without accessing raw user data.
3. **Leverages Solana’s low fees/high speed** for real-time proof generation/validation, with on-chain registries for revoked certificates.
4. **Integrates with major compliance oracles** (e.g., Chainalysis) to auto-flag high-risk addresses while preserving privacy.
**Why Solana?**
- **Throughput**: 65K TPS for instant proof verification.
- **Cost**: <$0.002 per transaction enables micro-verifications.
- **Ecosystem**: Native integrations with Serum, Phantom, and Firedancer for scalability.
resources:
- **Docs**: [Solana ZK Toolkit](https://github.com/solana-labs/solana-zksnarks), [Light Protocol](https://lightprotocol.com/) (ZK on Solana).
- **Dev Tools**: Anchor framework, Solana Pay for fiat onboarding.
- **Standards**: Study [Circle’s Verite](https://verite.org/) (private KYC) and [Ondo’s KYC solution](https://ondo.foundation/).
- **Tutorial**: [Building ZK Circuits with Solana](https://soldev.app/course/zero-knowledge-proofs).
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Financial institutions and DeFi protocols struggle with repetitive KYC/AML checks when users interact across multiple blockchains. Each chain requires redundant verification, exposing sensitive data and increasing compliance costs. Meanwhile, users face privacy risks, and projects lack a standardized, trustless way to share compliance status without compromising user anonymity or fragmenting liquidity.

Possible Solution:

A zero-knowledge proof (ZKP)-based KYC gateway on Solana that: 1. Issues reusable ZK-KYC certificates after initial verification (e.g., proof of residency, accredited investor status). 2. Allows cross-chain dApps (e.g., DeFi, NFT platforms) to cryptographically verify compliance without accessing raw user data. 3. Leverages Solana’s low fees/high speed for real-time proof generation/validation, with on-chain registries for revoked certificates. 4. Integrates with major compliance oracles (e.g., Chainalysis) to auto-flag high-risk addresses while preserving privacy. Why Solana? - Throughput: 65K TPS for instant proof verification. - Cost: <$0.002 per transaction enables micro-verifications. - Ecosystem: Native integrations with Serum, Phantom, and Firedancer for scalability.

- Docs: Solana ZK Toolkit, Light Protocol (ZK on Solana). - Dev Tools: Anchor framework, Solana Pay for fiat onboarding. - Standards: Study Circle’s Verite (private KYC) and Ondo’s KYC solution. - Tutorial: Building ZK Circuits with Solana.

name: DAOs as Risk-Pooling Vehicles
description:
There are certain professions with high risk high reward. For e.g: Pro gamers, White hat hackers, Youtube/TikTok creators, Musicians, Story writers, Drug developers

A DAO of gamers/hackers/creators/musicians/etc could be a perfect vehicle for these professionals to share risks while maintaining the same expected reward. It’s no coincidence that the first generation of DAOs are investment DAOs. Entrepreneurship and investments are high risk high reward activities.
possible_solution:
These DAOs would serve three primary functions:
\- Curation: they scout for and admit high potential members.
\- Risk sharing: they invest in new members in exchange for future financial upside. Members optionally share each other’s upside.
\- Support: they provide support to each other, such as marketing and distribution.

name: ColdChain: Decentralized Cold‑Chain Logistics Network on Solana
description: Southeast Asia’s food & vaccine supply chains suffer massive spoilage and waste due to opaque, centralized tracking-up to 40% of perishables are lost en route. Traditional cold‑chain logistics providers lack real‑time visibility; small farmers and clinics can’t afford expensive IoT infrastructure. This leads to food insecurity, lost revenue, and health risks from spoiled vaccines or medicines.
possible_solution:
Build **ColdChain**, a DePIN‑powered mesh of low‑cost, solar‑powered temperature & humidity sensors that automatically record environmental data on Solana.
1. **Plug‑and‑Play IoT Nodes**
    - Each node is a GPS‑enabled, tamper‑resistant sensor device that streams readings every minute.
    - Data packets are compressed & batched on‑device, then posted on‑chain via Solana’s low‑fee micro‑transactions.
2. **Token Incentives for Data Relay**
    - $CCLOG tokens reward node operators for uptime & relayed data.
    - Bonus staking multipliers for historically stable contributors, encouraging long‑term reliability.
3. **Immutable Cold‑Chain Ledger**
    - Every shipment’s environmental history is stored on Solana as a compressed NFT (cNFT) tied to the pallet ID.
    - Downstream recipients (wholesalers, retailers, clinics) scan the cNFT to verify that temperature never exceeded safe thresholds.
4. **Automated Smart Escrow & Insurance**
    - Integrate with on‑chain escrow: if a spoilage event is detected, smart contracts trigger token‑backed micro‑insurance payouts to affected farmers or distributors.
    - Parametric triggers based on deviation from acceptable ranges, no manual claims.
5. **Open‑API & Dashboard**
    - Public REST & WebSocket APIs for partners to integrate ColdChain data into their ERP/WMS.
    - A user dashboard for real‑time monitoring, alerts, and analytics – all verifiable on the blockchain.
resources:
Solana Sensor SDK & cNFTs: https://docs.solana.com/developing/programming-model/cnfts
Anchor Framework for on‑chain programs: <https://www.anchor-lang.com/>
Solana Micro‑Payment Batching: <https://github.com/solana-labs/solana-program-library/tree/master/token-2022>
Example IoT firmware repo: <https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_CircuitPython_SHT31D>
Temperature‑triggered parametric insurance model: https://docs.chainlink.com/temperature-feeds
DePIN data dashboards: <https://analytics.topledger.xyz/solana>
Sample front‑end & dashboard boilerplate: <https://github.com/solana-labs/example-frontend>

This leads to food insecurity, lost revenue, and health risks from spoiled vaccines or medicines.

Authors of different books could subsidize with $MYBOOK governance tokens, which would let you input into the contents of the book or what happens with it. Or would just collect royalty on all book sales

Speculation element would come from wagering on book’s success

name: Yield Trading Protocol
description:
It is easy for users on Solana to express their market views on asset prices by simply buying/selling an asset. Yet there is currently no way to express market views on yield (e.g. whether staking yield will go up/down).

A Pendle equivalent on Solana would enable the trading of yield and tap into what is a $500T market in TradFi.
possible_solution:
@pendle\_fi is a yield-trading protocol on Ethereum. Yield-bearing tokens (such as staked ETH) are split into their principal and yield components. Their special purpose AMM then enables the tokenized yield to be traded efficiently.

Through Pendle, users can execute various yield-management strategies such as:
\- Fixed yield (e.g. earn fixed yield on staked ETH)
\- Long yield (e.g. bet on staked ETH yield going up by purchasing more yield)
\- Use staked ETH to provide liquidity to yield traders

There have been attempts to build similar products on Solana before, but they all relied on order books and thus struggled to gain meaningful liquidity and adoption.

The key to Pendle's success is that it uses an AMM so that LPs don't have to strategize about market making.

Building a Pendle equivalent on Solana would enable people to express market views that they are currently unable to, and thus it would tap into a wholly new source of trading demand.

Some examples of yield that can be traded:
\- Liquid staked SOL
\- @Lifinity\_io revenue
\- @MeteoraAG pool yield
\- @Kamino\_Finance kTokens
\- @solendprotocol cTokens
resources: \- Pendle Docs: <https://docs.pendle.finance/>

name: Private onchain messaging
description: By default, on-chain messages are made public, allowing all messages sent to the Solana network to be accessible by anyone. This significantly diminishes the value of on-chain peer-to-peer messaging.
possible_solution:
A potential solution is to develop a Zk-based communication system, which involves encrypted wallet-to-wallet communication.

Think of it as a concept similar to Protonmail, but entirely integrated within Solana's framework, eliminating the need for off-chain storage. This approach is uniquely feasible on Solana due to its advantages such as cheaper fees and account compression.

name: DeFi Research and Risk DAO on Solana
description:
From $10 Billion in TVL to $200 Million in TVL, multiple protocols like Friktion, Everlend, and Nazare shut down, serious liquidity crunch - it's evident that Solana DeFi has been severely hit hard by the FTX crash. There's a void in Solana DeFi post-SBF/Alameda's exit, as they were one of the major contributors to Solana DeFi. 

1\. Unlike other blockchains, Solana lacks a leader project in DeFi, as no project has a dominant voice (For example - Ethereum has Uniswap and Aave)
2\. Unlike blockchains like NEAR, Solana Foundation doesn't have a DeFi think tank to help navigate existing DeFi teams and nurture new DeFi projects. DeFi sharp minds don't have a platform to discuss ideas, do deep research, and get reviews from the community.
3\. BD/Partnerships is right now centralized with Solana Foundation, and with the thinner foundation thesis, Solana BD will have a limited chance to grow. There's a need to decentralize the chain's BD initiatives.

Solana DeFi is critical to attracting capital and making the ecosystem thrive.
possible_solution:
Formulation of a DAO solely focussed on DeFi. Think, Superteam but for DeFi

What will the DAO do?
1\. Research: Think of unique financial use cases possible only on Solana and publish them for the wider community to glorify Solana DeFi.
2\. Accelerate: Help early-stage DeFi teams and nurture them in the initial stages, helping them with liquidity, capital, and BD connection.
3\. Risk Management: They can provide research and risk analysis for DeFi protocols. It can also publish public Risk Analysis Dashboards with key metrics for the Solana ecosystem and offer paid research, risk assessment frameworks, and risk rating services to DeFi projects.
4\. Consulting and Services: It can also become a degen talent layer for DeFi protocols and provide services to earn revenue.
5\. BD: The DAO will also take care of matchmaking in Ecosystems, helping DeFi teams bag Web2 (FinTech) partnerships, bringing institutions, Market Makers, and TradFi on DeFi.

Impact: Became a hub for all bright Solana DeFi minds, Bringing partnerships and Nurture Projects

In case you are interested in building or supporting this initiative. DM me at @yashhsm on Twitter/Telegram - happy to take this forward.

\- Risk DAO - <https://riskdao.org/>
\- Why fork SuperteamDAO? - <https://superteam.substack.com/p/celebrating-forking-within-daos>

name: X-to-Earn Ecosystem of Applications
description: StepN and similar apps have demonstrated the potential of incentivization to drive user growth in Web3 mobile apps and X-to-Earn - unlike traditional apps such as Google Maps or survey forms, which don't incentivize user participation despite benefiting from it. The lack of an incentive layer in such mainstream products may have broader implications.
possible_solution:
There can be a wide range of applications possible:
\- Data-to-earn:
    \- Anonymize & share on-device collected data and monetize by selling it to companies for tokens. Surveys can also be tokenized. Users can also provide user-generated content, photos, etc., to AI learning models for rewards.
\- Work-to-earn: 
    \- Small micro-tasks like “sending 20 personalized emails” can be put up, allowing users to earn for their time and effort.
\- Location-to-earn: 
    \- If a user can perform a valuable task in a given location, use the phone's GPS location to ensure the user is in the correct location to receive a reward. Hivemapper is a good example.
\- Review-to-earn: 
    \- Think Yelp, Google reviews, etc., but users are incentivized to provide reviews via earning tokens.
‍
These applications can be standalone Apps on mobile or be an xNFT on the Backpack wallet, enabling the creation of an ecosystem of apps.
resources:
\- Solana Mobile Stack - <https://solanamobile.com/developers>
\- Solana DAO Tooling Landscape Deep Dive - <https://insitesh.medium.com/solana-dao-tooling-ecosystem-challenges-opportunities-33f6a1cab32c>

description: Energy Infra is unpredictable due to the intermittent nature of sources such as wind and solar, batteries can help mitigate this issue by storing excess energy for later use. The effectiveness of batteries increases as more are connected to a public energy network.

author: Jon Wong

name: Autorebalancer for LP pools that helps route capital autonomously from crowded LP pools to uncrowded LP pools
description:
Liquidity providers (LP's) currently face a highly complex decision-making process when seeking to optimally rebalance their LP positions. It requires an LP to account for a complex set of factors, including but not limited to:
- _variance of yields and their composition;_
- _different fee and reward systems;_
- _offsetting various costs such as gas, slippage, and trading fees;_
- _different AMM models._

Differentiating between AMM models warrants highlighting, as its complexity is an often neglected core challenge of manual liquidity provision. 

Additional factors complicating the process include the significant amount of time LPs must spend researching opportunities and interacting with multiple protocols, making the process time-consuming and cumbersome. 

Even without considering these problems, the high costs of rebalancing and compounding often lead to underperformance and can prevent LPs from participating altogether.

If we can solve this problem, liquidity provisioning for correlated assets will become more efficient and can even compete with prop AMM's by undercutting them.
possible_solution:
Build an auto rebalancer vault protocol where: 
- Users deposit assets into an vault, which represents a set of potential deployment destinations + rebalancing logic between them. 
- From there, the protocol auto rebalances capital by monitoring the underlying pools

If solved you will be be able to tokenize these vaults into yield bearing tokens (like autoSOL represents SOL + yield coming from multiple LP destinations) and these tokens will start to reflect the standard LP'ing yield rate in Solana DeFi. 

Standard LPing yield rate can then be put as collateral into other protocols like Exponent and Kamino which will further increase the liquidity depth. 

This is enable new LST's, new stablecoins and new bitcoin, ethereum wrappers to get instant liquidity by plugging into the protocol. Think of this autopool as growth as a service for these new assets.
resources: Just see DEX pools of correlated assets on all DEX's on Solana and you will be able to com

Standard LPing yield rate can then be put as collateral into other protocols like Exponent and Kamino which will further increase the liquidity depth. author: Prastut Kumar

name: NFT Standard using Token Extensions
description: The current NFT standard implementations are controlled by Metaplex, which was highly debated last year as they are a monopoly in this case. Token Extensions enable anyone to spin off their own NFT standard, while still retaining composability across wallets and exchanges.
possible_solution:
The NFT standard can have features like:
1\. NFT Royalty — Transfer fees enable NFT creators to embed royalties directly into the tokens, making royalty enforcement feasible.
2\. Native Modular Token Metadata –– Developers can now add, update, and remove custom fields as necessary, customizing the metadata to meet the unique needs of their projects. With token extensions, this metadata is carried with the token, ensuring portability across all applications for seamless interoperability. For standard token mints—name, symbol, and URL can be easily added directly to the mint account, greatly simplifying the process for indexers and protocols to interact with your token.

WEN — a cat-themed memecoin that emerged during Jupiter's LFG launchpad testing — has developed an entirely new NFT standard leveraging TEs, known as WNS. This NFT standard employs the metadata pointer, group, and member pointers, along with mint closure extensions, with transfer hooks expected to be added soon to enforce on-chain royalties. It is already gaining significant traction with Asset Dash, one of the largest portfolio trackers, launching its 10k collection!

More token extensions can be mixed and matched to allow a higher feature set of tokens.
resources:
1\. A Primer on Solana's Token Extensions: The Superteam Blog
2\. Token Extensions Docs
3\. Wen New Standard by Wen

Problem to Solve:

The current NFT standard implementations are controlled by Metaplex, which was highly debated last year as they are a monopoly in this case. Token Extensions enable anyone to spin off their own NFT standard, while still retaining composability across wallets and exchanges.

Possible Solution:

The NFT standard can have features like: 1. NFT Royalty — Transfer fees enable NFT creators to embed royalties directly into the tokens, making royalty enforcement feasible. 2. Native Modular Token Metadata –– Developers can now add, update, and remove custom fields as necessary, customizing the metadata to meet the unique needs of their projects. With token extensions, this metadata is carried with the token, ensuring portability across all applications for seamless interoperability. For standard token mints—name, symbol, and URL can be easily added directly to the mint account, greatly simplifying the process for indexers and protocols to interact with your token. WEN — a cat-themed memecoin that emerged during Jupiter's LFG launchpad testing — has developed an entirely new NFT standard leveraging TEs, known as WNS. This NFT standard employs the metadata pointer, group, and member pointers, along with mint closure extensions, with transfer hooks expected to be added soon to enforce on-chain royalties. It is already gaining significant traction with Asset Dash, one of the largest portfolio trackers, launching its 10k collection! More token extensions can be mixed and matched to allow a higher feature set of tokens.

1. A Primer on Solana's Token Extensions: The Superteam Blog 2. Token Extensions Docs 3. Wen New Standard by Wen
author: Yash Agarwal

name: Decentralized Marketplaces for Collectibles
description:
Collecting is big business. Today, over one-third of Americans self-identify as collectors, and, as of 2024, the market size for collectibles is now nearly [$500 billion dollars](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/collectibles-market-2024-estimated-492-103200953.html). Collectibles are a booming industry—and blockchains are coming for them.

For flip-trading to be maximally efficient, collectibles marketplaces need to have:
1. instant settlement, 
2. physical custody, and
3. authentication

None of the leading collectibles marketplaces today, such as [Bring a Trailer](https://bringatrailer.com/), [StockX](https://stockx.com/), and [Chrono24](https://www.chrono24.com/), offer all three. Cash settlement is not an option; instead, physical settlement is the default—with settlement times usually measured in days or weeks. For bigger collectibles, such as cars, physical storage also quickly becomes an issue (where do you put 20 cars while you flip them?). For smaller collectibles, often traded via vertical marketplaces like Facebook Groups, fraud is a persistent challenge. 

All of these factors make trading collectibles on today’s marketplaces highly inefficient.
possible_solution:
We see a massive opportunity to create a new marketplace design, purpose-built for collectibles traders, called “Blockchain-Enabled Collectibles Marketplaces” (BECMs). These marketplaces offer instant trading via cash settlement, reduce settlement time from weeks to seconds using stablecoins, and use NFTs as digital representations of physical assets held by a trusted custodian or authenticator.

BECMs have the potential to reshape the billion-dollar collectibles market because they make it possible to: 1) unify markets and increase liquidity (vs. current fragmented, dark markets), 2) eliminate the need for personal physical storage, which encourages more trading, 3) increase trust by providing authentication, and 4) financialize collecting behavior by facilitating borrowing where borrowing wasn’t possible before. We believe the result of these efficiencies will be materially TAM expansionary for collectibles markets as a whole as more traders, liquidity, inventory, and marketplaces come online.
resources:
1. [Full Essay by Multicoin Capital](https://multicoin.capital/2024/08/22/blockchain-enabled-collectibles-marketplaces/)
2. [State of RWAs on Solana by Yash Agarwal](https://yashhsm.medium.com/state-of-real-world-assets-on-solana-the-opportunities-23ebff9a50c9)

For smaller collectibles, often traded via vertical marketplaces like Facebook Groups, fraud is a persistent challenge. All of these factors make trading collectibles on today’s marketplaces highly inefficient.

name: CrowdLens - A Community-Powered Content Oracle for Creators and AI teams
description:
Creators often struggle with choosing the best version of their content — be it thumbnails, captions, or poster designs. Traditional A/B testing is either inaccessible, slow, or lacks diverse human input. Meanwhile, AI-generated suggestions lack real-world context or creative resonance.
For AI teams and data curation projects, there’s also no scalable, quality-controlled system for collecting human judgments on subjective content. Crowd-sourced validation is either noisy or gamed.
In both cases, there's no open, incentivized protocol that allows **real humans to validate creative content**, reward useful input, and help both creators and models improve their performance.
possible_solution:
CrowdLens is a DePIN-powered decentralized voting oracle for subjective content.
- Creators upload multiple variations (e.g., 4-5 thumbnails for a YouTube video).
- Validators — real humans — vote on the most effective version.
- Votes are recorded on-chain, and validators are rewarded for high-quality, consensus-aligned votes.
- A reputation system ensures quality input, and slashing discourages spam.
- Over time, an **AI layer (Lens AI)** learns from the vote data and gives generative suggestions to improve content.
- This creates a **feedback flywheel**: human votes train AI → AI helps creators → better content → more voting.
We’re already actively building the MVP on Solana, including wallet login, vote submission, reward logic, and validator dashboard.
resources:
- GitHub: <https://github.com/Pratikkale26/CrowdLens>
- Dev Tools: Solana (Anchor), Phantom, Compressed NFTs, monorepo, Next.js
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Creators often struggle with choosing the best version of their content — be it thumbnails, captions, or poster designs. Traditional A/B testing is either inaccessible, slow, or lacks diverse human input. Meanwhile, AI-generated suggestions lack real-world context or creative resonance. For AI teams and data curation projects, there’s also no scalable, quality-controlled system for collecting human judgments on subjective content. Crowd-sourced validation is either noisy or gamed. In both cases, there's no open, incentivized protocol that allows real humans to validate creative content, reward useful input, and help both creators and models improve their performance.

Possible Solution:

CrowdLens is a DePIN-powered decentralized voting oracle for subjective content. - Creators upload multiple variations (e.g., 4-5 thumbnails for a YouTube video). - Validators — real humans — vote on the most effective version. - Votes are recorded on-chain, and validators are rewarded for high-quality, consensus-aligned votes. - A reputation system ensures quality input, and slashing discourages spam. - Over time, an AI layer (Lens AI) learns from the vote data and gives generative suggestions to improve content. - This creates a feedback flywheel: human votes train AI → AI helps creators → better content → more voting. We’re already actively building the MVP on Solana, including wallet login, vote submission, reward logic, and validator dashboard.

- GitHub: https://github.com/Pratikkale26/CrowdLens - Dev Tools: Solana (Anchor), Phantom, Compressed NFTs, monorepo, Next.js

name: Fixed Income Products
description: Fixed Income represents a significant opportunity, given that the global fixed-income market is valued at approximately $130 trillion. On Ethereum, ERC-4626 is a popular standard for yield-bearing vaults, managing over $10 billion in assets. A similar standard could also be developed on Solana using token extensions.
possible_solution:
Build a derivative token standard or a SaaS tool to enable anyone to deploy their interest-bearing tokens and vaults. Interest-earning Tokens (a token extension) enable Embedding Interest Rates Directly into the Token UI.

The interest-bearing mint extension enables projects to emulate real-world, interest-bearing financial instruments like bonds. Traditionally, most yield-bearing instruments rely on rebasing — distributing accrued yields in the form of additional tokens to reflect gains. This extension allows for the inherent accrual of yield or transparent and automatic rewards without necessitating a rebase. Teams can programmatically set an interest rate and retrieve the token balance, inclusive of interest, at any point. Interest accrues continuously, based on the network timestamp — the value can be updated as often as once per block.

More token extensions can be mixed and matched to allow a higher feature set to tokens.
resources:
1\. A Primer on Solana's Token Extensions: The Superteam Blog
2\. Token Extensions Docs

Problem to Solve:

Fixed Income represents a significant opportunity, given that the global fixed-income market is valued at approximately $130 trillion. On Ethereum, ERC-4626 is a popular standard for yield-bearing vaults, managing over $10 billion in assets. A similar standard could also be developed on Solana using token extensions.

Possible Solution:

Build a derivative token standard or a SaaS tool to enable anyone to deploy their interest-bearing tokens and vaults. Interest-earning Tokens (a token extension) enable Embedding Interest Rates Directly into the Token UI. name: Solana Loot Protocol: Open & Composable In-Game Asset Economy
description:
Game developers lose 30-50% of revenue to platform fees (Steam/Apple) and face fractured asset ecosystems. Players suffer from:\*
- 🎮 **Trapped assets**: $50B+ of in-game items languish in closed ecosystems
- 💸 **Zero royalties**: Creators earn nothing from secondary sales
- 🔒 **No cross-game utility**: Fortnite skins can’t be used in Call of Duty
- 📉 **Opaque drop rates**: Lootbox odds are hidden (illegal in some regions)
possible_solution:
A **decentralized loot protocol on Solana** enabling:
1. **SPL Token Wrapping**: Convert any in-game item (Unity/Unreal) into a compressed NFT (cNFT) in 1 click
2. **Royalty Engine**: Enforce 5-15% automatic royalties for devs on resales
3. **Provably Fair Drops**: Verifiable on-chain odds for lootboxes using Solana’s RNG
4. **Cross-Game SDK**: Let players use CS:GO skins in Solana RPGs via dynamic metadata
**Why Solana?**
- ✅ **cNFTs**: Store 100M items at <$0.001 each (vs. $10+ on Ethereum)
- ⚡ **Real-Time Trading**: 400ms block times for live gameplay integration
- 🎮 **Unity/Unreal Plugins**: Native support via Solana GameStack
resources:
- [Solana cNFT Docs](https://docs.solanalabs.com/compressed-nfts)
- [GameStack SDK](https://github.com/solana-labs/game-stack)
- [Royalty Standard (SPL-22)](https://spl.solana.com/token-2022)
- Must-Read: [a16z’s "Can’t Be Evil" Licenses](https://a16zcrypto.com/nft-licenses/)
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Game developers lose 30-50% of revenue to platform fees (Steam/Apple) and face fractured asset ecosystems. Players suffer from:* - 🎮 Trapped assets: $50B+ of in-game items languish in closed ecosystems - 💸 Zero royalties: Creators earn nothing from secondary sales - 🔒 No cross-game utility: Fortnite skins can’t be used in Call of Duty - 📉 Opaque drop rates: Lootbox odds are hidden (illegal in some regions)

Possible Solution:

A decentralized loot protocol on Solana enabling: 1. SPL Token Wrapping: Convert any in-game item (Unity/Unreal) into a compressed NFT (cNFT) in 1 click 2. Royalty Engine: Enforce 5-15% automatic royalties for devs on resales 3. Provably Fair Drops: Verifiable on-chain odds for lootboxes using Solana’s RNG 4. Cross-Game SDK: Let players use CS:GO skins in Solana RPGs via dynamic metadata Why Solana? - ✅ cNFTs: Store 100M items at <$0.001 each (vs. $10+ on Ethereum) - ⚡ Real-Time Trading: 400ms block times for live gameplay integration - 🎮 Unity/Unreal Plugins: Native support via Solana GameStack

- Solana cNFT Docs - GameStack SDK - Royalty Standard (SPL-22) - Must-Read: a16z’s "Can’t Be Evil" Licenses

name: Web3 Table Interface
description: Many people think in tables. A table is great way to interact with the world because many problems are data problems and it’s easy to think about data in a table.
possible_solution:
Excel first popularized tables to the point where some started calling it a “programming language”. Airtable took this further by basically becoming a table-formed backend for web2 businesses.

Web3Table would take it a stop further by adding web3 native primitives, namely reading from and writing to a blockchain.

Eg:
\- you’d pull in real-time data for 10 coins for the past 10 hours
\- you’d do a sum on the column and a conditional buy
\- = IF ( SUM ( A1:A10 ) > 123 , BUY XYZ , SELL XYZ )

Added bonuses:
1\. can pull in offchain in addition to onchain data
2\. can export pretty reports like Weights and Biases
3\. everything real time, never have to press refresh on anything
resources:
1\. Flipside Crypto
2\. Bubblemaps

name: Fundraising through LSTs
description: Only two viable models right now are raising from VCs or bootstrapping.
possible_solution:
A lot more projects should use LSTs as a way to crowdfund their ambitions, be it indie gamedev, animation, or a new energy drink. 10,000 SOL in your LST is ~10,000 USDC per month, enough to fund a good team. 

The utility can vary e.g. offer early beta access if you hold 10 gameSOL. Risk-free for stakers, no rug possible.

An LST is not just staked SOL. It's a way to direct a stream of future staking yields to someone without putting your capital at risk. this will 10x the way we collaborate and create value in the world.

name: Nontine - Tools for Pools
description:
\- duration/continuity based on-chain risk pools for fun and profit (and utility)
\- let individuals form and fund capital pools with ongoing premiums. the capital pools will invest/stake the premiums for yield and pay out premiums based on the prorated share of the interest in the pool.
\- as members fail to either 1) pay their premiums and/or 2) sign the contract at some interval (show proof of engagement ) their interest in the pool gets retired and everyone else’s goes up proportionally
possible_solution:
Nontine will give operators/managers tooling to setup and customize their pools across a bunch of levers
\- Inflow period: how long do members need to pay premiums? forever?
\- Outflow period: when do you start paying out dividends?
\- Investment: what does the pooled capital get invested in or does it just sit static?
\- Signature type: is it a signature or do you pay in every time
\- Premium: how much? does it stay flat, increase, decrease? is it one-time or continuous?
\- Fee: what fee does the manager charge on
\- the entire website should just be one path from link wallet, set rules, create pool
resources: This idea was originally shared on Colosseum. Find more inspiration, team mates, resources here.
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

- duration/continuity based on-chain risk pools for fun and profit (and utility) - let individuals form and fund capital pools with ongoing premiums. the capital pools will invest/stake the premiums for yield and pay out premiums based on the prorated share of the interest in the pool. - as members fail to either 1) pay their premiums and/or 2) sign the contract at some interval (show proof of engagement ) their interest in the pool gets retired and everyone else’s goes up proportionally

Possible Solution:

Nontine will give operators/managers tooling to setup and customize their pools across a bunch of levers - Inflow period: how long do members need to pay premiums? forever? - Outflow period: when do you start paying out dividends? - Investment: what does the pooled capital get invested in or does it just sit static? - Signature type: is it a signature or do you pay in every time - Premium: how much? does it stay flat, increase, decrease? is it one-time or continuous? - Fee: what fee does the manager charge on - the entire website should just be one path from link wallet, set rules, create pool

name: Improving Data Querying for Solana
description: Improving access and querying capabilities for data on Solana is necessary. Incorrect data has caused unwarranted FUD in the past and there's a need for a tool to ensure access to accurate information.
possible_solution:
\- A web-based tool through which any user can query all data on the Solana blockchain by using SQL queries. Pre-populated databases with parsed data from Solana blocks. This can all be built on Redash just like Dune.
\- Any user would be able to write a query: free users would have to open source their queries so others could work and paid users would have the ability to write queries privately
\- Automatic hourly updates on the most viewed queries and charts: Paid users could hook an API to any publicly available query and get results
\- Dashboarding functionality: Curated queries shared in a visualized form. The team would write several popularly requested queries to launch this product to expedite the kick-off
resources:
\- Redash Docs - <https://redash.io/help/open-source/setup>
\- Step Finance Docs - <https://docs.step.finance/>

name: DeFi-powered Consumer Loans
description:
Enable users to borrow uncollateralized loans using DeFi as the capital source while a local FinTech/Bank acts as an underwriter.

Problems:
1\.  DeFi is too over-collateralized, where one has to put > 100% of their loan amount as the collateral, making it unviable for real use cases, leading to only speculative use cases.\n
2\.  On-chain reputation and credit scores might solve this in the future, but not in the near term.
possible_solution:
A potential solution would be to:
1\. Accept Capital and Liquidity from DeFi, where primary and secondary marketplaces of loans can be created. 
2\. A FinTech does collateral and profile underwriting and KYC to ensure legal compliance and enforceability and acts as a Frontend.
resources:
\- Huma Finance - <https://huma.finance/>
\- GoldFinch - <https://goldfinch.finance/>

author: Yash Agarwal

name: Privacy-Powered Tokenized Opinion Discovery
description: Traditional opinion markets often fail to reveal true sentiment because people are influenced by social pressure, fear of judgment, or bias. Without anonymity and aligned incentives, participants may not share their honest beliefs. This skews results and prevents accurate understanding of collective opinion in politics, markets, or culture.
possible_solution: A technical solution is to implement onchain opinion markets (preferrably using tokens on a bonding curve to reward early believers) and for privacy to maintain fairness. Users anonymously commit opinions by buying tokens, with privacy ensuring validity without revealing identity. Differential privacy and threshold cryptography secure aggregate results through encryption. Incentive design redistributes rewards by rugging the inaccurate answer liquidity pool and rewarding users who opined correctly.
resources:
Essay: <https://papers.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2018/hash/b2dc449578a4744a1684d3b0ea933702-Abstract.html>

A good understanding on privacy can be found here: https://discuss.jup.ag/t/enabling-private-swaps-on-jupiter/39500

name: Yearly Census Dashboard
description: A network state, like any other state, needs a record of its citizens. A traditional census is a record of people living in the borders of a particular society. A census of a network state is a record of its citizens living across the world. An online dashboard is needed to be an accurate record of its citizens, their earnings (GDP) and much more.
possible_solution:
Every year (or quarter, or month), members of a network state can pledge their continued allegiance to a said startup society by participating in census. This is a way for the society to signal its power. The census displays a dashboard consisting of the most important health metrics of a startup society. This could include, networth of individuals (crypto & fiat), country of residence, donations to philanthropy, revenue from businesses they’ve started. Maybe even do a token for Census.
All of the off chain census data can be fetched using Reclaim Protocol. This would require understanding of societies, philosophy and technology to be able to identify what metrics actually matter. Some signals are mentioned in the book, however practical implementation would require quick experimentation.

name: Onchain MM program on Phoenix
description: Market making is market neutral strategy, aimed at infusing liquidity to order books. Automated market making strategies are important to ensure order books are thick and execution prices for even big orders are close to a fair price, impact cost & volatility is thus lower.
possible_solution:
Phoenix is the latest and leading on-chain order book on Solana, which has already facilitated over $250 million in trading volume. It also holds the distinction of being the first crankless limit order book DEX. Building on-chain Market Making program that places orders based on an oracle price. The bots should be profitable and should have the following features:
\- It should be easily configurable by anyone, plug and play.
\- It should place both ask and bid orders around some mid-price.
\- The spread should be configurable.
\- It should automatically rebalance and be able to maintain 24/7 liquidity.

name: Project Walkthroughs
description: There is often a lot of documentation and answers available for specific questions or aspects of a project, but it is rare to find a comprehensive document that explains the entire project. This is because projects can be complex and multifaceted, making it difficult to capture all of the information in one place. In open source projects, different people may have different perspectives on the project, which can make it difficult to create a single document that covers everything. Additionally, it can be time-consuming and challenging to create a comprehensive document while also working on building the project.
possible_solution: Project walkthroughs can be useful for onboarding new developers to an ecosystem and helping existing developers understand the source code of a large, complex open source project. They typically provide a step-by-step overview of the main components of the project and how they work together to build the project. Maintainers can also use walkthroughs to explain how they are maintaining the source code, any modifications or upgrades they have made, and any other important information that users should know in order to use the project correctly and effectively. In addition, walkthroughs may include helpful resources such as tutorials, links to relevant documentation, and example code that can help new developers get up to speed quickly.
resources: \- Kubernetes Source Code Tour by kubectl: <https://www.linux.com/news/tour-kubernetes-source-code-part-one-kubectl-api-server/>
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

There is often a lot of documentation and answers available for specific questions or aspects of a project, but it is rare to find a comprehensive document that explains the entire project. This is because projects can be complex and multifaceted, making it difficult to capture all of the information in one place. In open source projects, different people may have different perspectives on the project, which can make it difficult to create a single document that covers everything. Additionally, it can be time-consuming and challenging to create a comprehensive document while also working on building the project.

Possible Solution:

Project walkthroughs can be useful for onboarding new developers to an ecosystem and helping existing developers understand the source code of a large, complex open source project. They typically provide a step-by-step overview of the main components of the project and how they work together to build the project. Maintainers can also use walkthroughs to explain how they are maintaining the source code, any modifications or upgrades they have made, and any other important information that users should know in order to use the project correctly and effectively. In addition, walkthroughs may include helpful resources such as tutorials, links to relevant documentation, and example code that can help new developers get up to speed quickly.

name: Habit Tracker
description: People want to get better (exercise more, drink less, save more, eat better, etc)  but need help staying on-track
possible_solution:
1\. An app that lets you start an accountability pool with friends (e.g. invite by wallets address or email id to a pool you created)
2\. The creator sets a task and deadline (e.g. I will walk an avg of 5k steps/day for 7 days)
3\. At the end of the deadline, 3 outcomes are possible
    a. All participants confirm that the task was completed, and the creator of the pool gets their money back
    b. All participants confirm that the task was not completed and the creator loses their stake to others in the pool 
    c. Consensus is not reached and the tokens remain locked for 90 days after which they are burned or donated

name: Onchain Luxury Brands
description: For these user segments, blockchains can fundamentally be two things. First, they can be factories for digitally native goods. Image-based NFTs (eg, PFPs and generative art), language-based NFTs (eg, novels), or audio-based NFTs (eg, music) are all examples of digitally native goods. They offer creators and brands a new way to monetize their brand and artistic prowess. For buyers, these NFTs are speculative collectibles, and that's an under-appreciated carrot for the early adopters. But the creator/brand could also decide to offer benefits such as exclusive access and royalty.
possible_solution: Second, they can be databases of redeemable receipts for physical goods. For example, a pair of Nike sneakers can come with an embedded physical chip that's digitally linked to an NFT. Some call this "phygital". Others call this "digital twin". You can even consider this as an "RWA" if that's helpful. An onchain representation of physical merchandise offers the creator/brand a new channel to engage their customers, the customers strong assurances against counterfeiting, and the asset itself the potential to take advantage existing DeFi/NFTFi infrastructure such as lending.
resources: 1\. TipLink Docs
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

For these user segments, blockchains can fundamentally be two things. First, they can be factories for digitally native goods. Image-based NFTs (eg, PFPs and generative art), language-based NFTs (eg, novels), or audio-based NFTs (eg, music) are all examples of digitally native goods. They offer creators and brands a new way to monetize their brand and artistic prowess. For buyers, these NFTs are speculative collectibles, and that's an under-appreciated carrot for the early adopters. But the creator/brand could also decide to offer benefits such as exclusive access and royalty.

Possible Solution:

Second, they can be databases of redeemable receipts for physical goods. For example, a pair of Nike sneakers can come with an embedded physical chip that's digitally linked to an NFT. Some call this "phygital". Others call this "digital twin". You can even consider this as an "RWA" if that's helpful. An onchain representation of physical merchandise offers the creator/brand a new channel to engage their customers, the customers strong assurances against counterfeiting, and the asset itself the potential to take advantage existing DeFi/NFTFi infrastructure such as lending.

name: Gleanai: A Fun, Open-Source Onboarding Engine for Millions on Solana
description:
Most blockchain ecosystems struggle to attract and retain mainstream users because onboarding is often complex, boring, or high-friction. Telegram-based projects like Notcoin, Hamster Kombat, Dogs, and Major proved that simple, game-like tasks can bring millions of active users with minimal marketing spend.

However, Solana lacks a unified, open-source platform to replicate that success:

★ Existing Solana onboarding experiences are fragmented and developer-focused, not user-friendly.

★ There’s no engaging “play-to-earn” style flow combining NFT minting, staking, and token claiming directly inside a familiar platform like Telegram.

★ Without such a solution, Solana misses out on millions of potential users and transactions.
possible_solution:
Gleanai is an open-source, gamified Telegram integration designed to onboard millions of new users to Solana. Key features:

★ Mini-Games & Tasks on Telegram → Simple, fun challenges that encourage users to create wallets, mint NFTs, and stake them.

★ NFT Minting & Staking on Solana → Users mint and stake NFTs to unlock token claims and level up, driving on-chain activity.

★ Reward Distribution via Smart Contracts → Automatic token claims ensure transparency and fairness.

★ Fully Open-Source Architecture → Anyone can audit, fork, or extend Gleanai to build their own campaigns.

★ Massive Scalability → Designed to handle millions of Telegram-based transactions without central bottlenecks.

By combining the viral mechanics of successful Telegram projects with Solana’s fast and low-cost blockchain, Gleanai will make Solana the most fun and frictionless network for newcomers.
resources:
- [Solana Developer Docs](https://solana.com/developers)
- [Anchor Framework  for smart contract development](https://www.anchor-lang.com/)
- [Metaplex  for NFT minting tools](https://www.metaplex.com/)
- [Telegram Bot API  for bot integrations](https://core.telegram.org/bots/api)
- [Open-source repo](https://gleanai.github.io/) (to be published on GitHub once initial prototype is ready)
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Most blockchain ecosystems struggle to attract and retain mainstream users because onboarding is often complex, boring, or high-friction. Telegram-based projects like Notcoin, Hamster Kombat, Dogs, and Major proved that simple, game-like tasks can bring millions of active users with minimal marketing spend. However, Solana lacks a unified, open-source platform to replicate that success: ★ Existing Solana onboarding experiences are fragmented and developer-focused, not user-friendly. ★ There’s no engaging “play-to-earn” style flow combining NFT minting, staking, and token claiming directly inside a familiar platform like Telegram. ★ Without such a solution, Solana misses out on millions of potential users and transactions.

Possible Solution:

Gleanai is an open-source, gamified Telegram integration designed to onboard millions of new users to Solana. Key features: ★ Mini-Games & Tasks on Telegram → Simple, fun challenges that encourage users to create wallets, mint NFTs, and stake them. ★ NFT Minting & Staking on Solana → Users mint and stake NFTs to unlock token claims and level up, driving on-chain activity. ★ Reward Distribution via Smart Contracts → Automatic token claims ensure transparency and fairness. ★ Fully Open-Source Architecture → Anyone can audit, fork, or extend Gleanai to build their own campaigns. ★ Massive Scalability → Designed to handle millions of Telegram-based transactions without central bottlenecks. By combining the viral mechanics of successful Telegram projects with Solana’s fast and low-cost blockchain, Gleanai will make Solana the most fun and frictionless network for newcomers.

- Solana Developer Docs - Anchor Framework for smart contract development - Metaplex for NFT minting tools - Telegram Bot API for bot integrations - Open-source repo (to be published on GitHub once initial prototype is ready)

and blockchains are inherently composable.

connect wallet is powerful. for instance,  you can use your asset like SOL everywhere inside solana ecosystem by just connecting wallet.

similarly, if this context (or memory) exists on-chain, different llm platforms can quickly gain context of a user and have personalised experience. ofc, the context should exist as encryped (maybe zk-proofs) and can be as simple as an NFT.

most interesting would be: trading of context. 

think like how one can monetise/liscence their context (while retaining custody).

i personally think IP will be highly commoditised (think how image gen made ghibli IP irrelevant overnight). instead context will be priced higher than even IP in the age of superintelligence

name: MaybePay
description: When you go to buy something on the Internet these days, you are almost always going to be offered a choice — pay now in full, or finance the purchase with Affirm style 'after pay' (pay a bit every month for a long time) - After pay is fine / I get why some people take out micro-loans, but it is fully commoditized.
possible_solution:
With MaybePay, when you check out you get a button that offers you a third choice.... 99% of the time you can have your shopping cart for free, but 1% of the time you pay 101x (leaving some margin for the business).
So, if you go for 'maybe pay'.. almost always your purchase costs $0... and people LOVE $... but every once in a while you pay a lot!
I think people would love this - they would use it all the time, buy more stuff, and generally have quite a bit of fun (it isn't gambling, it is a form of shopping entertainment... of course :)!

Crypto Solution to Enforcing No Backsies!
Of course, the issue with this idea is that many not so honorable people might try to reverse their credit card charges when they hit that 1%.... And that kinda breaks the whole thing!...
That is where crypto can help. It would be pretty easy with crypto to have folks 'stake' on the purchase - transfer stable-coin in to a 'pending' state and then either return the coin (99% of the time) or sweep it 1% of a time.
resources: This idea was originally shared on Colosseum. Find more inspiration, team mates, resources here.

Possible Solution:

With MaybePay, when you check out you get a button that offers you a third choice.... 99% of the time you can have your shopping cart for free, but 1% of the time you pay 101x (leaving some margin for the business). So, if you go for 'maybe pay'.. almost always your purchase costs $0... and people LOVE $... but every once in a while you pay a lot! I think people would love this - they would use it all the time, buy more stuff, and generally have quite a bit of fun (it isn't gambling, it is a form of shopping entertainment... of course :)! Crypto Solution to Enforcing No Backsies! Of course, the issue with this idea is that many not so honorable people might try to reverse their credit card charges when they hit that 1%.... And that kinda breaks the whole thing!... That is where crypto can help. It would be pretty easy with crypto to have folks 'stake' on the purchase - transfer stable-coin in to a 'pending' state and then either return the coin (99% of the time) or sweep it 1% of a time.

name: Crypto Healthcare
description:
One obvious thing that Crypto can be used to fix, is misaligned incentives. 
Healthcare suffers from the typical principal-agent problem - the apparatus (pharma, insurance, clinics) is incentivized to sell you stuff that costs more but isn’t necessarily better for you, while obviously what you want is better outcomes (at lower cost ideally)d
possible_solution:
1\. Both you and your practitioner get some amount of $HEALTH tokens
2\. You both stake into a pot
3\. As you see them and they recommend treatment, your health changes
4\. If outcomes are positive (or as positive as they can be given any genetics / inherent disease), your practitioner earns more of the pot over time
5\. If they’re negative, you get more of the pot and they get “slashed”

name: Crypto Loyalty Program
description: Companies today compete fiercely for to increase their retention and customer loyalty.
possible_solution:
Instead of charging customers in $, you offer them a choice: pay full price in $ or pay a discounted price in $MYJOINTTOKEN

Why would they want that?
1\. Anyone holding $MYJOINTTOKEN is now more likely to go to that restaurant to eat, than another
2\. $MYJOINTTOKEN is basically free advertising. Every time someone fires up their wallet they get reminded about the awesome restaurant they’ve not visited for some time
3\. Targeting: wallets holding $MYJOINTTOKEN are your superfans. You can now airdrop them discounts, special offers, and other things they rightfully deserve vs a single visit customer.
4\. Finally, if $MYJOINTTOKEN does well, they now have a treasury they can use for airdrops and incentives (yes physical spaces incentives)

name: AI Evals
description: We need better ways to evaluate and compare AI models, particularly as agents become more common. Evals will inform the common framework and language with which we compare AI models, but there will not be one “correct answer”, so a plurality of eval suites and approaches is best.
possible_solution:
- Since evals by neutral third-parties are a public good, and because evals can be prohibitively expensive to run, the use of crypto primitives for eval crowdsourcing, incentivization, and verification should play a significant role in the growing field.
- Context: AI evaluations can be thought of as tests. As with human tests such as the SAT or Myers-Briggs, different facets of an AI model can be tested:
    - How intelligent is the model?
    - How capable is the model at performing various tasks (e.g., coding) autonomously?
    - How much of an extra boost, or uplift, in performance does a person get from using the model for a certain task?
    - How biased is the model, in either direction? What ‘beliefs,’ if any, does it seem to have?
    - Does the model exhibit any goals or drives?
    - How easy is it to convince the model to do nefarious things?
To support this process, it is necessary to make scoring, logging, and visualizing outputs extremely easy. Evals are a critical component of the AI development loop. Due to the inherent complexity of AI problems, defining them precisely is often impossible. Instead, engineers need to measure correctness using context-specific data and metrics, then rapidly experiment with various models, prompts, fine-tuning, and other techniques to achieve desired results.
resources:
1. Example companies (existing and potential): LMSYS, Braintrust, Galileo, Zeno, Parea, AtlasFM, Haize Labs
2. Further reading:
    - [One vs. Many: Comprehending Accurate Information from Multiple Erroneous and Inconsistent AI Generations](https://capital.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6f895fcc6df9ca0df180735e3&id=9ccfe0a933&e=5c51df9638)
    - [We Need to Consider Disagreement in Evaluation](https://capital.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6f895fcc6df9ca0df180735e3&id=a93d6f2c24&e=5c51df9638)
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

We need better ways to evaluate and compare AI models, particularly as agents become more common. Evals will inform the common framework and language with which we compare AI models, but there will not be one “correct answer”, so a plurality of eval suites and approaches is best.

Possible Solution:

- Since evals by neutral third-parties are a public good, and because evals can be prohibitively expensive to run, the use of crypto primitives for eval crowdsourcing, incentivization, and verification should play a significant role in the growing field. - Context: AI evaluations can be thought of as tests. As with human tests such as the SAT or Myers-Briggs, different facets of an AI model can be tested: - How intelligent is the model? - How capable is the model at performing various tasks (e.g., coding) autonomously? - How much of an extra boost, or uplift, in performance does a person get from using the model for a certain task? - How biased is the model, in either direction? What ‘beliefs,’ if any, does it seem to have? - Does the model exhibit any goals or drives? - How easy is it to convince the model to do nefarious things? To support this process, it is necessary to make scoring, logging, and visualizing outputs extremely easy. Evals are a critical component of the AI development loop. Due to the inherent complexity of AI problems, defining them precisely is often impossible. Instead, engineers need to measure correctness using context-specific data and metrics, then rapidly experiment with various models, prompts, fine-tuning, and other techniques to achieve desired results.

1. Example companies (existing and potential): LMSYS, Braintrust, Galileo, Zeno, Parea, AtlasFM, Haize Labs 2. Further reading: - One vs. Many: Comprehending Accurate Information from Multiple Erroneous and Inconsistent AI Generations - We Need to Consider Disagreement in Evaluation

Allowing developers to create mini-games where users can burn & earn BONK will be a definitive driver of both developer and user activity on Solana. For e.g., Bonkmas was a Christmas themed weeklong festival where ecosystem projects like Jito, Exchange Art, Solana mobile, MarginFi, MixMob, MoonBoi, GooseFX, DualFinance, etc. offered rewards to thousands of users who signed up and used BONK within their products.

If developers & projects can create Blinks where users can try out their products with BONK and earn rewards, they will be incentivised to build out a presence there.
resources:
1\. Blinks Docs
2\. Spinova - A BONK Slot Machine Game by Avneesh from Superteam India, powered by Frames
