author: Aditya Shetty

name: Social trust-based P2P onramp
description:
Most new users struggle with the first step of entering Web3 and Solana: obtaining their first small amount of crypto. Today, the default onramp requires conventional fiat-to-crypto providers who impose KYC/AML checks, bank integrations, and regulatory hurdles. While necessary for compliance, this process introduces significant friction, especially for users who:
- Only want to experiment with small trial amounts (e.g., $5–10).
- Lack access to compliant onramp providers (because of geography or banking exclusions).
- Abandon onboarding entirely when faced with document uploads, approval delays, or opaque fees.
This creates a paradox: _to understand the value of Solana, you need to experiment with it firsthand_—but to do so, you must clear hurdles designed for high-value transactions.
As a result, adoption stalls at the very start, with potential new users either turning away or relying on less secure, informal workarounds.
possible_solution:
## Social P2P Micro-Onramp for Solana
The core idea is to provide a seamless way for current Solana users to onboard friends or trusted contacts by sending a minimal, prepackaged “starter kit” (e.g., $5 USDC + SOL for transaction fees) to their new wallet, all within a safe, guided, and compliant flow.
## 1. One-Click Embedded Wallet Creation
- New users are issued an embedded wallet upon invitation from an existing user, with no manual setup required—just a link received via email, DM, or QR code for guided onboarding.
## 2. Social Trust Invitation and Funding
- The sender initiates an "onramp" action in their wallet/app, selecting a trusted contact and sending them a small starter bundle.
- The transaction is capped for regulatory safety and flagged as P2P, not commerce. Both parties sign a simple attestation that no goods/services are being exchanged, minimizing AML/KYC obligations for trial-sized transfers.
- Social graph data and reputation signals can be used for fraud checks before funds are released.
## 3. Guided First Actions and Education
- Upon wallet activation, the recipient is shown an interactive tutorial on Solana basics and safe practices with their funded wallet.
- The UI highlights micro-transactions they can try and optionally includes a referral bonus for further inviting others.
## 4. Gradual Compliance Steps
- For trial-sized, trusted onramp flows, full KYC is deferred; higher-value transfers or repeat activity can trigger a “progressive KYC” process if users want to increase transfer limits.
- Pattern monitors and smart attestation ensure regulatory compliance is maintained at scale.
## 5. Return or Forward Mechanism
- Recipients are given the option (and instruction) to return unused funds, or to “pay it forward” by inviting and funding another first-timer, propagating the network effect in a sustainable loop.
resources: https://perena.org/resources
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Most new users struggle with the first step of entering Web3 and Solana: obtaining their first small amount of crypto. Today, the default onramp requires conventional fiat-to-crypto providers who impose KYC/AML checks, bank integrations, and regulatory hurdles. While necessary for compliance, this process introduces significant friction, especially for users who: - Only want to experiment with small trial amounts (e.g., $5–10). - Lack access to compliant onramp providers (because of geography or banking exclusions). - Abandon onboarding entirely when faced with document uploads, approval delays, or opaque fees. This creates a paradox: to understand the value of Solana, you need to experiment with it firsthand—but to do so, you must clear hurdles designed for high-value transactions. As a result, adoption stalls at the very start, with potential new users either turning away or relying on less secure, informal workarounds.

Possible Solution:

Social P2P Micro-Onramp for Solana The core idea is to provide a seamless way for current Solana users to onboard friends or trusted contacts by sending a minimal, prepackaged “starter kit” (e.g., $5 USDC + SOL for transaction fees) to their new wallet, all within a safe, guided, and compliant flow. 1. One-Click Embedded Wallet Creation - New users are issued an embedded wallet upon invitation from an existing user, with no manual setup required—just a link received via email, DM, or QR code for guided onboarding. 2. Social Trust Invitation and Funding - The sender initiates an "onramp" action in their wallet/app, selecting a trusted contact and sending them a small starter bundle. - The transaction is capped for regulatory safety and flagged as P2P, not commerce. Both parties sign a simple attestation that no goods/services are being exchanged, minimizing AML/KYC obligations for trial-sized transfers. - Social graph data and reputation signals can be used for fraud checks before funds are released. 3. Guided First Actions and Education - Upon wallet activation, the recipient is shown an interactive tutorial on Solana basics and safe practices with their funded wallet. - The UI highlights micro-transactions they can try and optionally includes a referral bonus for further inviting others. 4. Gradual Compliance Steps - For trial-sized, trusted onramp flows, full KYC is deferred; higher-value transfers or repeat activity can trigger a “progressive KYC” process if users want to increase transfer limits. - Pattern monitors and smart attestation ensure regulatory compliance is maintained at scale. 5. Return or Forward Mechanism - Recipients are given the option (and instruction) to return unused funds, or to “pay it forward” by inviting and funding another first-timer, propagating the network effect in a sustainable loop.

name: TradWi API Abstraction
description:
Every telco will launch an "API strategy" over the next few years, if they haven't already.  McKinsey estimates $100b+ annual revenue from telco APIs over the next 5-7 years.

But there's one huge issue: telcos suck at building APIs. There is not a developer-focused bone in their entire organization
possible_solution: A brave entrepreneur can aggregate these emerging telco APIs into a single, global, interoperable telecom API, with developer-friendly documentation / monitoring / etc. Effectively, we're talking about building the next-gen [Twilio](https://www.twilio.com/en-us).
resources:
1. [What it will take for telcos to unlock value from network APIs: by McKinsey](https://www.mckinsey.com/industries/technology-media-and-telecommunications/our-insights/what-it-will-take-for-telcos-to-unlock-value-from-network-apis)
2. [AT&T, Verizon, Dish and others hunt for common network APIs](https://www.lightreading.com/oss-bss-cx/what-s-the-story-at-t-verizon-dish-and-others-hunt-for-common-network-apis)
3. [State of Solana DePIN (2024) by Yash Agarwal](https://blog.superteam.fun/p/state-of-solana-depin-2024)

Problem to Solve:

Every telco will launch an "API strategy" over the next few years, if they haven't already. McKinsey estimates $100b+ annual revenue from telco APIs over the next 5-7 years. But there's one huge issue: telcos suck at building APIs. There is not a developer-focused bone in their entire organization

Possible Solution:

A brave entrepreneur can aggregate these emerging telco APIs into a single, global, interoperable telecom API, with developer-friendly documentation / monitoring / etc. Effectively, we're talking about building the next-gen Twilio.

name: Crypto Stipend
description: Some time ago there was a wave of fintech startups trying to fund peoples’ degrees and then get a % of their earnings for some number of years to pay down the loan / make extra for the lender.
possible_solution:
There are smart people around the world without access to traditional education finance and they would benefit from having a decentralized source of financing.

It might go something like this:
1\. X releases token $SELF
2\. X’s profile is public so you can see what they’ve done before and the quality of their work
3\. $SELF can be purchased on a (probably not very steep) bonding curve, so early funders are rewarded
4\. Money can be spent on education only (probably sent directly to university of choice)
5\. A % of each payment could be matched by the government if the degree they chose is useful / in short supply (medical professionals, engineers)
6\. When they start working a % of their salary is used to cover the loan + extra so lender is rewarded
resources: 1\. Full Essay

Problem to Solve:

Some time ago there was a wave of fintech startups trying to fund peoples’ degrees and then get a % of their earnings for some number of years to pay down the loan / make extra for the lender.

Possible Solution:

There are smart people around the world without access to traditional education finance and they would benefit from having a decentralized source of financing. It might go something like this: 1. X releases token $SELF 2. X’s profile is public so you can see what they’ve done before and the quality of their work 3. $SELF can be purchased on a (probably not very steep) bonding curve, so early funders are rewarded 4. Money can be spent on education only (probably sent directly to university of choice) 5. A % of each payment could be matched by the government if the degree they chose is useful / in short supply (medical professionals, engineers) 6. When they start working a % of their salary is used to cover the loan + extra so lender is rewarded

name: Neobanks for Developing Nations
description: Neobanks were all the rage 2-3 years ago, but few focused on developing nations where demand for stablecoins and yield is disproportionately strong.
possible_solution:
We are excited about the idea of offering yield on top of stablecoins to consumers in developing nations. Although in certain parts of Latam it's fairly crowded, we still believe opportunities exist across the globe.

A further tailwind is the proliferation of onchain RWAs, such as US treasuries, which enable uncorrelated and often higher yields than crypto-native yields from Aave and Compound. These RWAs make high-quality assets from developed nations easily accessible to developing nations.
resources: 1\. Circle Developer Docs

author: FP Lee

name: Stablecoins for Real World Payments
description:
Stablecoins are the first and by far the largest non-speculative use case of crypto.

According to a Brevan Howard Digital report, "In 2022, stablecoins settled over $11T onchain, dwarfing the volume processed by PayPal ($1.4T), surpassing the payment volume of Visa, and reaching 14% of the volume settled by ACH." It always baffles us that people think crypto still hasn't found a killer use case beyond trading.
possible_solution: We don’t believe crypto payment startups should compete with the likes of Venmo or Revolut. Instead, they should identify and focus on underserved user segments such as traditionally underbanked industries (often taboo industries) or cross-border payment - think stablecoin payment appplications for Latam, Africa, the Middle East, South Asia, and Southeast Asia, where fiat debasement and censorship are more prevalent.
resources:
1\. The Relentless Rise of Stablecoins: By Brewan Howard
2\. Circle Developer Docs

name: Structured Products using Token Extensions
description: Structured Products are big in TradFI as they enable specific investor needs with a customized product mix. Replicating that on-chain, particularly with Real World Assets is a big opportunity.
possible_solution: Obligate, an RWA debt player has built a cool prototype for structured product issuances on Solana. They use transfer hooks to do compliance checks, but most importantly keep track of coupon payments using our custom snapshot-transfer-hook. The prototype shows the ability to reduce issuance and price fixing time frames from days to milliseconds and to handle the entire life cycle in a fully automated and transparent fashion.
resources:
1\. A Primer on Solana's Token Extensions: The Superteam Blog
2\. Token Extensions Docs
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Structured Products are big in TradFI as they enable specific investor needs with a customized product mix. Replicating that on-chain, particularly with Real World Assets is a big opportunity.

Possible Solution:

Obligate, an RWA debt player has built a cool prototype for structured product issuances on Solana. They use transfer hooks to do compliance checks, but most importantly keep track of coupon payments using our custom snapshot-transfer-hook. The prototype shows the ability to reduce issuance and price fixing time frames from days to milliseconds and to handle the entire life cycle in a fully automated and transparent fashion.

1. A Primer on Solana's Token Extensions: The Superteam Blog 2. Token Extensions Docs
author: Yash Agarwal

name: New order types like Stop Loss for Phoenix
description: Advanced orders such as Stop Loss, encompassing Stop Loss Market Orders, Stop Loss Limit Orders, Trailing Stop Loss orders, and more, play a pivotal role for traders operating on order books. Phoenix, the latest and foremost on-chain order book on Solana, has already facilitated trading volume exceeding $250 million. However, the absence of advanced orders like Stop Loss on Phoenix hinders the ability to execute trades automatically when a security's price hits a predefined level known as the stop price.
possible_solution: Enhance Phoenix's order book functionality by introducing new order types like Stop Loss. Develop a user-friendly interface (UI) or widget that empowers users to place Stop Loss orders. This entails providing details such as the desired USD amount or asset quantity for buying or selling, in addition to a trigger price.
resources:
\- Phoenix Github Repository - <https://github.com/Ellipsis-Labs/phoenix-v1>
\- Phoenix Website and Documentation - <https://www.phoenix.trade/>

name: Brand Vouchers as cNFTs
description: Onboarding the masses to Web3 presents a significant challenge. DRiP has been addressing this issue by distributing millions of compressed NFTs for free. The emergence of compressed NFTs on Solana has tackled the cost barrier, enabling the creation of 1 million NFTs with just 5 SOL. However, establishing and disseminating coupons and vouchers as NFTs proves challenging for smaller brands.
possible_solution: Introduce a loyalty tool, an airdrop application designed for brands to dispense coupons and vouchers as compressed NFTs to carefully curated Solana addresses. This solution has the potential to enhance customer engagement and retention for these brands.

name: LLM x prediction markets
description:
prediction markets are information markets.
ai is really good at aggregation information (eg. chatgpt)

ai will tend to reduce information asymmetry.
ai agents will have an inherent edge in prediction markets (and will make prediction markets more efficient - not solve, ofc)
possible_solution:
for eg. via llm-based market making, which quote prices (or odds) and market make orders based on the real-time information aggregated. ofc, it'll be hugely probabilistic (and hence good directional risks)

further, ai will help retail at better:
\- new markets discovery (recommends best ones)
\- research and decision making (and we already rely on chatgpt/grok for all info/opinions) - and it can give rise to new trading modalities. eg. bet on anything directly from chat interface.

you'll think, if everyone uses ai, where's the edge?

well, there are 5+ foundational models (and 30+ major fine-tuned models) and every model has their own opinions/taste and it's probabilistic (it will come up with random opinions every time) - this makes it inherently interesting.

btw, [@grok](https://x.com/grok)
 x kalshi/polymarket is a good early indicator!

1. Mockups: <https://x.com/0xsrinivasa/status/1962247946101198857>
2. OG Tweet: <https://x.com/yashhsm/status/1970029953019252933>

Problem to Solve:

prediction markets are information markets. ai is really good at aggregation information (eg. chatgpt) ai will tend to reduce information asymmetry. ai agents will have an inherent edge in prediction markets (and will make prediction markets more efficient - not solve, ofc)

Possible Solution:

for eg. via llm-based market making, which quote prices (or odds) and market make orders based on the real-time information aggregated. ofc, it'll be hugely probabilistic (and hence good directional risks) further, ai will help retail at better: - new markets discovery (recommends best ones) - research and decision making (and we already rely on chatgpt/grok for all info/opinions) - and it can give rise to new trading modalities. eg. bet on anything directly from chat interface. you'll think, if everyone uses ai, where's the edge? well, there are 5+ foundational models (and 30+ major fine-tuned models) and every model has their own opinions/taste and it's probabilistic (it will come up with random opinions every time) - this makes it inherently interesting. btw, @grok x kalshi/polymarket is a good early indicator!

author: Yash Agarwal

name: US Tokenised Stocks on Solana
description:
Retail Investors around the globe want to invest in US stocks or other capital markets but are constrained by low access and high forex costs of ~3-4%, which eat away a significant portion of their returns.

Tokenized Stocks on Blockchain are a great alternative. However, they are right now:
1) Only focussed on Ethereum-ecosystems, no robust protocols on Solana
2) Synthetic stock was a great feature in FTX, but with its crash, there's a huge void in the market

Synthetify is an existing protocol on Solana but has failed to gain significant traction and is now almost dead with daily volume < $1000 post FTX crash.
possible_solution:
A Synthetic protocol on Solana, where it tracks the price of US Stocks via Pyth/Switchboard oracle. A synthetic AMM pool can be created, or orderbooks like OpenBook (Serum) can be used.

How will it work:
1) User buys the tokenized stock, let's say AAPL, as per the real-world price tracked by Pyth Oracle. This will be transferred from an existing user.
2) User can also mint a tokenized stock by simply depositing the equivalent amount of USDC and redeeming likewise.
3) The stocks can either be fully backed by equivalent stocks, partnering with a US-based custodian, or hedged via equivalent futures to avoid any risk.

The key benefit of bringing it on-chain would be: increased accessibility, as anyone worldwide can trade these assets without relying on clunky international payments and long onboarding processes. Other synthetic assets like Bonds, Currencies, and commodities can also be bought later. There's a significant demand for this product from a FinTech BD perspective.

DM me at @yashhsm on Twitter/Telegram - I would be happy to guide you throughout and make connections if anyone is interested in building.
resources:
\- Synthetix - <https://synthetix.io/>
\- Tokenized Stocks - <https://www.sofi.com/learn/content/tokenized-stocks/>

Problem to Solve:

Retail Investors around the globe want to invest in US stocks or other capital markets but are constrained by low access and high forex costs of ~3-4%, which eat away a significant portion of their returns. Tokenized Stocks on Blockchain are a great alternative. However, they are right now: 1) Only focussed on Ethereum-ecosystems, no robust protocols on Solana 2) Synthetic stock was a great feature in FTX, but with its crash, there's a huge void in the market Synthetify is an existing protocol on Solana but has failed to gain significant traction and is now almost dead with daily volume < $1000 post FTX crash.

Possible Solution:

A Synthetic protocol on Solana, where it tracks the price of US Stocks via Pyth/Switchboard oracle. A synthetic AMM pool can be created, or orderbooks like OpenBook (Serum) can be used. How will it work: 1) User buys the tokenized stock, let's say AAPL, as per the real-world price tracked by Pyth Oracle. This will be transferred from an existing user. 2) User can also mint a tokenized stock by simply depositing the equivalent amount of USDC and redeeming likewise. 3) The stocks can either be fully backed by equivalent stocks, partnering with a US-based custodian, or hedged via equivalent futures to avoid any risk. The key benefit of bringing it on-chain would be: increased accessibility, as anyone worldwide can trade these assets without relying on clunky international payments and long onboarding processes. Other synthetic assets like Bonds, Currencies, and commodities can also be bought later. There's a significant demand for this product from a FinTech BD perspective. name: Solana Build
description:
Rust is a programming language that is known for its performance, safety, and concurrency. However, it is also considered to be a difficult language to learn, especially for developers who are new to programming.
There is a lot of documentation available on building on Solana, but this can be overwhelming for new developers who are just starting their development journey. To make the onboarding process easier and more intuitive, there is a need for more resources and support -- this could include tutorials, sample code, and other learning materials that are specifically designed to make the learning process more enjoyable.
possible_solution:
A no-code platform which can be used for educating the new devs with the fundamentals. As well as create prototypes without coding and understand how the interactions work with each block.
It can be the teaching tool for hands-on learning. Helps visual learners about the workings of solana with a visual editor. 
Provide a drag-and-drop interface for prototyping Smart Contracts and dApps, making it easy to understand how the components of a dApp interact and behave. 
The tool can explain basics of cryptography, rust & solana ecosystem including -
\- Encryption 
\- Digital signatures 
\- Signing transactions,
\- Smart contracts, and
\- More basics
resources: \- Eth.Build - <https://sandbox.eth.build/>
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Rust is a programming language that is known for its performance, safety, and concurrency. However, it is also considered to be a difficult language to learn, especially for developers who are new to programming. There is a lot of documentation available on building on Solana, but this can be overwhelming for new developers who are just starting their development journey. To make the onboarding process easier and more intuitive, there is a need for more resources and support -- this could include tutorials, sample code, and other learning materials that are specifically designed to make the learning process more enjoyable.

Possible Solution:

A no-code platform which can be used for educating the new devs with the fundamentals. As well as create prototypes without coding and understand how the interactions work with each block. It can be the teaching tool for hands-on learning. Helps visual learners about the workings of solana with a visual editor.  Provide a drag-and-drop interface for prototyping Smart Contracts and dApps, making it easy to understand how the components of a dApp interact and behave.  The tool can explain basics of cryptography, rust & solana ecosystem including - - Encryption  - Digital signatures  - Signing transactions, - Smart contracts, and - More basics

- Eth.Build - https://sandbox.eth.build/

**Problems:**
- DeFi markets suffer from significant volatility, causing uncertainty and increased risk for users and investors.
- Users currently lack efficient tools to accurately predict market movements, leading to financial losses or missed opportunities.
- Existing solutions often rely on outdated or incomplete data, lacking predictive capabilities needed for complex DeFi dynamics.
possible_solution:

- Use deep neural networks and predictive analytics to forecast token price movements, market volatility, and optimal investment strategies.

Utilizing Chainlink’s proven oracle framework ensures reliable and secure data integration, crucial for accurate and trustworthy market predictions.
- **Numerai (https://numer.ai/)**: Numerai leverages artificial intelligence and crowdsourced machine learning models to predict financial markets. Studying Numerai's methodology and platform helps in developing robust AI models and understanding how predictive analytics can be effectively applied to blockchain and decentralized finance.

author: Superteam

name: Stablecoin Launchpad
description:
Enable users to launch omni-chain tokens, such as stablecoins and meme-coins, across multiple chains.

This way, a token can increase its Total Addressable Market (TAM) of all chains while launching on one chain.

For any token, be it a project governance/utility or memecoin or stablecoins, they are constrained by the chains they are deployed on natively.

This limits their TAM, both from capital and user perspective.
possible_solution:
One could launch stablecoins on a chain like Solana, backed by assets such as tokenized treasuries.

Then, with Wormhole, launch a wrapped version on 20+ other chains, ensuring interoperability with the main chain — via the Hub and Spoke Model.
resources:
1\. Superteam Blog
2\. Wormhole Docs
3\. Circle Docs
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Enable users to launch omni-chain tokens, such as stablecoins and meme-coins, across multiple chains. This way, a token can increase its Total Addressable Market (TAM) of all chains while launching on one chain. For any token, be it a project governance/utility or memecoin or stablecoins, they are constrained by the chains they are deployed on natively. This limits their TAM, both from capital and user perspective.

Possible Solution:

One could launch stablecoins on a chain like Solana, backed by assets such as tokenized treasuries. Then, with Wormhole, launch a wrapped version on 20+ other chains, ensuring interoperability with the main chain — via the Hub and Spoke Model.

1. Superteam Blog 2. Wormhole Docs 3. Circle Docs
author: Yash Agarwal

name: Archive
description:
**Problem Statement:**
Archive is a decentralized publishing platform that aims to solve several critical issues in the current publishing ecosystem:
1. **Centralization and Gatekeeping:** Traditional publishing houses control which authors get published, often prioritizing commercial viability over creativity or originality. This leaves many talented writers without a platform or forces them to compromise their vision.
2. **Loss of Ownership and Control:** Authors typically sign contracts that transfer significant rights over their work to publishers. As a result, creators lose control over distribution, adaptations, pricing, and access to their own intellectual property.
3. **Lack of Permanent Preservation:** Works published through traditional channels are subject to publisher archives, print runs, or digital obsolescence. There is no guarantee that the content will remain accessible, intact, or unaltered over time.
4. **Limited Monetization for Authors:** Revenue streams are often restricted by intermediaries—publishers, distributors, and platforms take a large share of earnings, leaving authors with a small fraction of the value they generate.
5. **Opacity and Trust Issues:** Centralized platforms can manipulate visibility, rankings, and access to content, creating unfair advantages or censorship without transparency.
**How Archive Solves These Problems:**
- **Decentralized Control:** Authors publish their works (NFBs – Not-Fading Books) directly on the platform, removing gatekeepers and enabling creative freedom.
- **Ownership & Security:** Each work is encrypted and stored across distributed nodes, guaranteeing that only the author controls access, modifications, and rights.
- **Permanent Preservation:** NFBs are designed to be immutable, tamper-proof, and persist indefinitely, ensuring the longevity of intellectual work.
- **Direct Monetization:** Authors can set pricing, subscription access, or reward models directly, without intermediaries taking unfair cuts.
- **Transparency:** Platform rules, publishing, and distribution are open and verifiable, preventing manipulation or censorship.
In short, Archive democratizes publishing by giving authors full creative freedom, secure ownership, and a sustainable ecosystem for their works, while ensuring permanence and transparency in a way that no centralized publisher can provide.
possible_solution:
**Proposed Solution:**
Archive is a decentralized publishing platform built on a distributed network where each NFB (Not-Fading Book) is encrypted, stored, and verified across multiple nodes. The system ensures that:
1. **Direct Author Control:** Authors can create, publish, and manage their works without intermediaries. All access rights, monetization rules, and distribution are set by the author.
2. **Immutable & Secure Storage:** Each NFB is encrypted and replicated across the network. Any changes to a work are recorded, and the original version remains tamper-proof and verifiable.
3. **Transparent Verification:** Readers, authors, or auditors can programmatically verify the authenticity, ownership, and integrity of any NFB using cryptographic proofs.
4. **Decentralized Access & Monetization:** Authors can define who can access their works (public, private, paid, subscription-based), with all transactions and access records transparently logged on the network.
5. **Self-Sustaining Ecosystem:** By removing traditional gatekeepers and enabling direct interaction between authors and readers, Archive creates a fully autonomous publishing ecosystem that scales with community participation.
**In short:** Archive transforms publishing into a decentralized, secure, and transparent network where authors retain full ownership, readers gain trust in authenticity, and the permanence of works is guaranteed.
resources: https://librarium-dwua8jrtd-sources-projects-b9494b02.vercel.app/

name: Decentralized Scraping Hub
description: AI builders are constantly looking for new sources of data.
possible_solution:
Scraping allows them to extract large quantities of data by mimicking humans to an extent where both defense and offense make 100M$+ in ARR, the current wave of AI is only going to push this further. 

Companies like ScrapingHub(Zyte), Bright Data go through several hoops to evade bots and maintain a proxy network with hundreds of thousands of individual IP addresses.

The prompt here is to leverage token incentives to build up a supply of millions of individual IP addresses that you can use as a proxy network to scrape data for AI and redistribute the revenue generated as rewards to the users who shared their bandwidth.
resources:
1\. Blockmesh lets you earn rewards for your idle bandwidth
2\. HolaVPN: They are a free VPN with 6m+ users, they make money by using your bandwidth for scraping and in return you get a slow internet and a basic VPN.
3\. Grass by Wynd Network gets users paid for their unused internet
4\. AI x Web3, by AllianceDAO

author: Volt Capital

name: Solana NFT Storage Optimizer
description: Compressed NFTs are the next big thing as NFTs are becoming increasingly popular in the Soalna ecosystem. However, the developers need to optimize their storage solution for performance, scalability, and data integrity, which requires a deep understanding of different data storage solutions and their trade-offs.
possible_solution:
The Solana NFT Storage Optimizer tool provides several proposed solutions to help Solana developers optimize their Merkle tree storage for NFTs. This can look like:
1\. Parameter Optimization: The tool recommends optimal values for the depth, buffer size, and canopy height parameters based on the number of NFTs to be stored. Developers can use these recommendations to optimize their Merkle tree storage for better performance, storage efficiency, and security.
2\. Rent Calculation: The tool estimates the rent required for the Merkle tree based on the chosen parameters. This feature helps developers estimate the storage, computational overhead, and bandwidth requirements of their Merkle tree, enabling them to choose the most cost-effective storage solution.
3\. Trade-Offs Between Different Storage Options: The tool provides trade-offs between different storage options, enabling developers to make informed decisions on how to optimize their storage based on their specific needs. Developers can choose between higher security or better performance, depending on their priorities. For instance, if a project prioritizes security over performance, they can choose a higher depth and a lower canopy height. Alternatively, if they prioritize performance over security, they can choose a lower depth and a higher canopy height.
4\. Alternative data storage options: it should also provide options like partitioning, splitting, etc., for the Merkle tree to optimize performance and storage usage. You can also think of providing hybrid solutions that combine different data storage solutions to achieve better performance, storage efficiency, and security. For example, a hybrid solution can use Merkle trees for secure storage and linear data storage for efficient access.

author: Jon Wong

name: Idle Treasury deposited to Meteora Vaults
description: DAOs and projects on Solana often possess substantial treasuries that remain idle due to the inability to invest in high-risk assets.
possible_solution:
Meteora presents a remedy by enabling users and integrated protocols to deposit their funds into the Dynamic Vaults Pool. These funds are subsequently directed to established lending protocols like Marginfi and Solend, generating attractive yields.

For these idle treasuries, Meteora's Dynamic Vaults offer a viable option. By depositing their assets here, treasuries can accrue yields in a secure and optimized manner. This approach introduces new use cases, enabling temporarily idle capital to be deployed for yield generation and later withdrawn for practical use.
resources: \- Meteora Docs - <https://docs.meteora.ag/>

name: AI Web Crawlers
description: Whether we like it or not, scraping the data on the public web is how the current era of AI has gained an edge.
possible_solution:
As we go down this path, it is important to recognize that not everyone is going to be happy with their data being scrapped. The current web infrastructure addresses this by adding a robots.txt to the website as a guide book for any crawlers or bots to follow, mostly a polite request not a legal expectation, robots.txt is mostly ignored. 

 Seeing that the number of AI agents in the wild are only going up, it is time to reinvent robots.txt for the modern web.

New generation of decentralized scraping hubs and crawlers can negotiate via smart contracts for their use of the data, ideally by depositing some collateral.
resources: 1\.  Arweave's UDL to license the data

name: Privacy focused stablecoin on Solana
description:
The transactions on a public blockchain are visible to anyone, it can be difficult to maintain privacy with respect to financial matters. This can be a problem for individuals or organizations that want to keep their financial information private, as it may be vulnerable to being viewed by outsiders. It can also be a problem for businesses that want to maintain the privacy of their financial data in order to protect their competitive advantage.

A few considerations:
1\. How secure the solution is so that individuals and institutions can trust that?
2\. Is it reliable enough to not disclose the recipients and amounts while maintaining privacy?
3\. Is it easy to use? (Most of the time privacy comes with lots of complication and extra effort on the users' end, making privacy a game for the elite class)
possible_solution:
A privacy-focused stablecoin which leverages Solana's two key points: Scalability and speed to provide secure and easy-to-use Zero Knowledge transfers. 

Some of the potential use cases include but are not limited to:
\- A public donation platform that maintains donor anonymity.
\- A grant funding platform/fundraising platform that maintains public privacy.
\- Peer-to-peer payment splitting.
\- Private business payments to make payments to suppliers, contractors, or other partners without revealing the details of the transaction to the public.
\- Secure online marketplaces allowing buyers and sellers to transact securely and privately.
resources:
\- zkSNARKS & Circuits - <https://docs.zkbob.com/implementation/zksnarks-and-circuits>
\- Zcash - <https://learn.bybit.com/altcoins/what-is-zcash-zec/>
\- What are ZK-Proofs good for - <https://archive.devcon.org/archive/watch/6/scalability-is-boring-privacy-is-dead-zk-proofs-what-are-they-good-for/?tab=YouTube>
\- zkBob - <https://docs.zkbob.com/zkbob-overview/basic-concepts>
\- Light Protocol - <https://lightprotocol.com/>
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

The transactions on a public blockchain are visible to anyone, it can be difficult to maintain privacy with respect to financial matters. This can be a problem for individuals or organizations that want to keep their financial information private, as it may be vulnerable to being viewed by outsiders. It can also be a problem for businesses that want to maintain the privacy of their financial data in order to protect their competitive advantage. A few considerations: 1. How secure the solution is so that individuals and institutions can trust that? 2. Is it reliable enough to not disclose the recipients and amounts while maintaining privacy? 3. Is it easy to use? (Most of the time privacy comes with lots of complication and extra effort on the users' end, making privacy a game for the elite class)

Possible Solution:

A privacy-focused stablecoin which leverages Solana's two key points: Scalability and speed to provide secure and easy-to-use Zero Knowledge transfers. Some of the potential use cases include but are not limited to: - A public donation platform that maintains donor anonymity. - A grant funding platform/fundraising platform that maintains public privacy. - Peer-to-peer payment splitting. - Private business payments to make payments to suppliers, contractors, or other partners without revealing the details of the transaction to the public. - Secure online marketplaces allowing buyers and sellers to transact securely and privately.

author: DeCharge

name: The End of Seed Phrases
description:
Seed phrases are a deterrent for onboarding new users. When a new user makes a move into the web3 world the 1st interaction is with a wallet and to set up their wallet they come across the seed phrase. Not all the users are comfortable in taking the matter into their own hands and keep the seed phrase safely.
Moreover it is a single point of failure as there is no recovery mechanism for a lost seed phrase.
possible_solution:
The possible solution is to eliminate the need for seed phrases in a wallet. This can be achieved with alternatives such as:
\- Social recovery
\- Email auth
\- 2FA
\- Solutions like MPC wallets
resources: \- MPC Wallets Introduction - <https://www.alchemy.com/overviews/mpc-wallet>

name: Mint web2 data as cNFTs
description: Currently, there exists a lack of on-chain customer data. However, a substantial opportunity presents itself: the potential to transition web2 data onto the blockchain, enabling its composability and utilizing this data to construct user profiles directly on-chain.
possible_solution:
A viable solution could involve implementing "Login with" functionality for prominent social profiles such as Twitter, Reddit, and Github. This integration would facilitate the automatic minting of web2 activities as compressed NFTs. 

These activities could encompass a variety of actions, including tweets, subreddit interactions, and project engagements. Consequently, these compressed NFTs would unlock access to products that harness web2 data by merely scrutinizing the compressed NFTs retained by the user. 

This solution is particularly suited for consumer applications seeking user context and individuals desiring to aggregate their interests as compressed NFTs.
resources:
\- TipLink Wallet Adapter - <https://tiplink.io/products/tiplink-wallet-adapter>
\- Underdog Documentation -  <https://underdog.readme.io/reference/getting-started-1>
\- Compression Docs - <https://docs.metaplex.com/programs/compression/>
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Currently, there exists a lack of on-chain customer data. However, a substantial opportunity presents itself: the potential to transition web2 data onto the blockchain, enabling its composability and utilizing this data to construct user profiles directly on-chain.

Possible Solution:

A viable solution could involve implementing "Login with" functionality for prominent social profiles such as Twitter, Reddit, and Github. This integration would facilitate the automatic minting of web2 activities as compressed NFTs. These activities could encompass a variety of actions, including tweets, subreddit interactions, and project engagements. Consequently, these compressed NFTs would unlock access to products that harness web2 data by merely scrutinizing the compressed NFTs retained by the user. This solution is particularly suited for consumer applications seeking user context and individuals desiring to aggregate their interests as compressed NFTs.

name: Shiller Slashing
description:
People want to shill their bags, duh. The problem is that some have no moral issues with doing that and immediately turning around and dumping on their followers.

To date there’s been no consequences for the pump and dumper, and the space seems to have a memory of a goldfish letting the same people do it time and again.
possible_solution:
Create a platform on which calls are made → followers ape into the call → you track their P&L and see if they got ruined with the call.

The trick is the caller has to stake assets to get distribution on the platform. The more they stake, the higher distribution they get (aka more people see their calls and can cape into them).

If their followers make money, their staked assets earn yield. If their followers lose money, they get slashed and assets are moved to followers.

This creates aligned incentives for both sides:
1\. callers - now have to make good calls, and if they do they make MORE money than had they just posted on Twitter
2\. followers - now can see who’s actually a good trader and only follow them / ape into their calls
resources: 1\. Full Essay with GTM and Biz Model Ideas
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

People want to shill their bags, duh. The problem is that some have no moral issues with doing that and immediately turning around and dumping on their followers. To date there’s been no consequences for the pump and dumper, and the space seems to have a memory of a goldfish letting the same people do it time and again.

Possible Solution:

Create a platform on which calls are made → followers ape into the call → you track their P&L and see if they got ruined with the call. The trick is the caller has to stake assets to get distribution on the platform. The more they stake, the higher distribution they get (aka more people see their calls and can cape into them). If their followers make money, their staked assets earn yield. If their followers lose money, they get slashed and assets are moved to followers. This creates aligned incentives for both sides: 1. callers - now have to make good calls, and if they do they make MORE money than had they just posted on Twitter 2. followers - now can see who’s actually a good trader and only follow them / ape into their calls

name: Truth Chains
description: Social networks are often controlled by centralised entities. Truth is often lost in bias and interpretation instead of optimising for facts and positive discourse. There is a medium needed that becomes a source of objective truth, minimising any bias in a trustless and verifiable manner.
possible_solution:
A network for news that is not controlled by any entity. Instead, anyone can publish facts on chain by providing the fact, its source and proof of authenticity. All facts are recorded on chain. Numerous AI models can compete to present this data in a human readable fashion. For example, the same basketball match may be interpretted differently by the fans of either teams. Similarly, political actions may have multiple interpretations. The truth chain should be a record of all factual information and AI models on top should churn out interesting articles using these onchain facts.

This product has two parts to it : the storage of data itself while proving the authenticity using Reclaim Protocol, and building the first AI model that utilizes the on chain facts.

name: Fully Onchain Gig Economy
description: Current gig discovery platforms suffer from unreliable hires, fake profiles, high cross-border payment fees, etc.
possible_solution:
Build a generalised fully onchain platform for all forms of digital labor. 

It’s the best shot at using:
- stablecoins
- distributed social graphs for id verification,
- and smart contracts for payments

The primary differentiator here would rather ironically be off-ramps/on-ramps in emerging markets because while stablecoins are great to send money you can’t pay yet with it. So users will aggregate wherever the liquidity for off-ramping and tax filing is easiest. 

Additional nuance is, such a product will inevitably have to be user owned over time. An employee owned cooperative of sorts. A global DAO. 

Initially, it may have only low-value, highly commoditised players but as capital supply increases (something in abundance in stables), it will attract more sophisticated forms of talent.

3. [@bountycaster](https://x.com/bountycaster) for posting work
4. [@coordinape](https://x.com/coordinape) for peer-assessed value creation

a shift from navigation-based to intent-based usage could give rise to pay-per-conversion models, where agents (performing tasks for users) are rewarded for opting into ads or promotional actions in exchange for small payments.

name: Milestone Grant Manager
description:
Milestone Grant Management is a tricky problem in Web3 where many L1s and prominent protocols are providing grants with few ways to ensure grant recipients deliver on their grant promises. Very commonly, protocol owners say that they give the grant and either:
1\. Nothing is delivered, or
2\. Something is delivered but it is vastly different or inferior to what was promised
possible_solution: Using Milestone Grant Manager, the grant can be streamed or progressively unlocked in steps as the owner of the grant confirms that milestones have been met.

name: crvUSD
description: It is important to have the option to hold USD in a decentralized stablecoin (as opposed to centralized options such as USDC and USDT), but none have gained meaningful adoption on Solana so far.
possible_solution:
crvUSD is a collateralized-debt-position (CDP) stablecoin pegged to the US dollar.

Its main difference from other CDP protocols is in how liquidations work, or to put it differently in how the protocol manages your collateral. While other CDP protocols only convert your collateral in one direction (i.e. into the asset you borrowed), crvUSD ensures that your position is converted to the more valuable asset as you near certain price points. The benefit of this is that borrowers don't end up in the unfortunate situation sometimes faced with traditional CDPs where the price moves enough to liquidate you but then bounces right back while you are left holding the less valuable asset.

The unique liquidation model of crvUSD – which is friendlier to the borrower – could significantly increase adoption of decentralized stablecoins.
resources:
\- How does crvUSD work?: <https://curve.substack.com/p/crvusd-faq>
\- crvUSD overview: <https://docs.curve.fi/crvUSD/overview/>

name: Sports betting exchange
description: Global sports betting is a multi-trillion dollar industry. However, this industry is highly fragmented with multiple players providing poor products at high margins, all driven by low or fragmented liquidity across the gambling markets.
possible_solution:
One solution is building decentralized sports betting exchange on top of the Monaco Protocol. The Monaco protocol is open sourced and provides for pooled liquidity, allowing new exchanges to tap into the entire liquidity pool of the protocol on day one. This allows the exchange to offer markets with high liquidity from the start, giving users the best possible experience, and improves the liquidity and experience for other exchanges built on the Monaco Protocol.

The Monaco Protocol streamlines the wagering process, helps provide greater liquidity for participants, reduce spreads, and offer lower prices. It lowers the take rates of institutions and middlemen and reduces settlement times, increases transparency, and improves capital efficiency -- all leading to a better user experience in the world of sports betting!
resources:
\- Monaco Protocol - <https://github.com/MonacoProtocol>
\- Monaco Protocol Docs - <https://docs.monacoprotocol.xyz/>

name: Lending Orderbook
description:
In DeFi lending markets, we use a pool system where there is 1 borrow APY and the supply APY is a function of the borrow APY. 

This creates issues where future borrowers after you spike the borrow APY and increase the cost of borrowing for ALL users, or leads to capital being under utilized as users arent willing to borrow, leading to very low capital efficiency in lending markets.
possible_solution:
Ideally, we have an orderbook or a system where lenders and borrowers can specify the interest rate they want to borrow and supply at, and an orderbook handles the matching between users.

Alternatively, lenders can use a pool and borrowers can specify the rates they will borrow at on an orderbook, and borrows are executed overtime at different rates. For example, 100 USDC in the pool, 80 USDC can be borrowed at 4%, and 10 USDC borrowed at 3.8%, leading to higher yields for all lenders and optimized borrow costs for borrowers.
resources:
\- First look into Solend and traditional lending markets - <https://save.finance>
\- Look into Serum V4 (AOB) as a starting point - <https://github.com/openbook-dex/program>

name: Liquidating an NFT to any token
description: Solana-based NFTs are primarily denominated in SOL. However, many traders and collectors might prefer to interact with USDC and USDT. Allowing NFTs to be liquidated in any token, particularly stablecoins like USDC, can significantly simplify matters.
possible_solution:
Introduce an NFT liquidation platform that enables users to instantly exchange any SOL-based NFT for any SPL token at the currently available best price.

This platform will leverage two key services:
\- Marketplace aggregators like Tensor, which facilitate the sale of NFTs at the optimal prices (in SOL).
\- Swap aggregators like Jupiter, which convert SOL to any token at the best available prices. Both Jupiter’s pricing and swap APIs will be utilized in this process.

The user workflow is as follows: A user clicks on the 'swap' option, connects their SOL wallet, selects the NFT they wish to swap, and designates the desired token for exchange/receipt. Subsequently, they await the aggregator to identify the most advantageous route, choose the option that best suits their preferences, and then execute the swap.
resources:
\- Jupiter APIs - <https://station.jup.ag/docs/apis>
\- Tensor Docs - <https://docs.tensor.trade/>

name: Tokenized Ride-Sharing Reputation Layer (TRRL) on Solana
description:
Ride-sharing platforms like Uber, Lyft, and Grab rely on closed, centralized systems for managing driver and passenger reputations. This creates multiple problems:
1. **Lack of Data Portability:** Drivers cannot carry their reputation or history to new platforms, limiting competition and worker empowerment.
2. **Platform Bias & Censorship:** Ratings can be manipulated or removed without transparency, affecting drivers' livelihoods.
3. **No Proof of Contribution:** There’s no verifiable on-chain proof of service history, customer satisfaction, or value added.
4. **Southeast Asia Specific:** In SEA, where gig economies are rapidly expanding, many drivers work across platforms and informal networks. There’s no unified system to track or reward long-term performance or community trust.
possible_solution:
**A tokenized, on-chain reputation layer for ride-sharing drivers and passengers built on Solana.**
- **Driver NFT ID**: Each driver gets a cNFT or tokenized badge tied to their wallet (Soulbound optional), tracking:
    - Completed rides (via oracle or partner integration)
    - Ratings from verified users
    - Tips received (on-chain)
    - Response rate & punctuality (optional via GPS metadata)
- **Reputation Score**: An aggregated, transparent, and permissionless score visible to any dApp or ride-hailing platform integrating the protocol.
- **User Side**: Riders can also accrue badges for good behavior (no-shows, tipping, consistent usage), building an ecosystem of trust on both sides.
- **Token Incentives**:
    - Bonuses for verified drivers with high scores
    - Reputation-based staking to access premium jobs
    - Optional loyalty rewards via $USDC or governance token
- **Designed for Integration**: Can be integrated by new DePIN ride-sharing apps, or eventually interoperable with Web2 incumbents through APIs or off-chain data bridges.
resources:
Solana cNFT Docs: https://docs.solana.com/developing/programming-model/cnfts
DePIN Analytics: <https://analytics.topledger.xyz>
Bee Maps (Solana mapping DePIN): <https://beemaps.io>
NATIX (Geo Data DePIN): <https://www.natix.network/>
Helium Mobile (Wireless DePIN case study): https://www.helium.com/mobile
Backpack xNFT: <https://www.backpack.app/>
Real-time dApp integration ideas: https://solana.com/developers
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Ride-sharing platforms like Uber, Lyft, and Grab rely on closed, centralized systems for managing driver and passenger reputations. This creates multiple problems: 1. Lack of Data Portability: Drivers cannot carry their reputation or history to new platforms, limiting competition and worker empowerment. 2. Platform Bias & Censorship: Ratings can be manipulated or removed without transparency, affecting drivers' livelihoods. 3. No Proof of Contribution: There’s no verifiable on-chain proof of service history, customer satisfaction, or value added. 4. Southeast Asia Specific: In SEA, where gig economies are rapidly expanding, many drivers work across platforms and informal networks. There’s no unified system to track or reward long-term performance or community trust.

Possible Solution:

A tokenized, on-chain reputation layer for ride-sharing drivers and passengers built on Solana. - Driver NFT ID: Each driver gets a cNFT or tokenized badge tied to their wallet (Soulbound optional), tracking: - Completed rides (via oracle or partner integration) - Ratings from verified users - Tips received (on-chain) - Response rate & punctuality (optional via GPS metadata) - Reputation Score: An aggregated, transparent, and permissionless score visible to any dApp or ride-hailing platform integrating the protocol. - User Side: Riders can also accrue badges for good behavior (no-shows, tipping, consistent usage), building an ecosystem of trust on both sides. - Token Incentives: - Bonuses for verified drivers with high scores - Reputation-based staking to access premium jobs - Optional loyalty rewards via $USDC or governance token - Designed for Integration: Can be integrated by new DePIN ride-sharing apps, or eventually interoperable with Web2 incumbents through APIs or off-chain data bridges.

name: Transaction Guards
description: Currently, wallets are vulnerable to instant draining in case their private key gets compromised.
possible_solution:
A potential solution could be adding the following features to the wallets:
\- Withdrawal limits
\- Daily spending limits
\- Whitelisted addresses
Additionally, Transaction Guards could be integrated into protocols, analyzing a user's transaction history and halting high-risk transactions (e.g., a user usually withdraws amounts ranging from $500 to $1000 but in this case the transaction amount is 20 times larger). In such cases, a 2FA confirmation might be required to ensure that it's the actual user initiating the action, rather than a malicious actor.
resources: \- Wallet Guard: <https://www.walletguard.app/>

name: Creator Apps using Blinks
description:
400K+ unique users collect 2-3 million pieces of content from ~50 creators via DRiP every week. This includes art, memes, podcasts, games, music, sports moments, puzzles, etc. 

DRiP is well beyond the stage of “mass free airdrops” because each time you collect from a creator, it costs units of an in-game currency and over a million DRiP accounts have been purged to fight bots and farming. 

DRiP is well positioned to onboard the next millions of users and thousands of creators, but by their own admission they aren’t best placed to built niche vertical content consumption experiences; i.e., the UI of the DRiP vault (and upcoming app) will be largely similar whether you’re reading a comic, listening to music or enjoying some art. 

However, creators can build custom products and services for their collectors because the underlying content is composable and owned by each user (as an NFT). While in theory these can be separate apps, the ability for creators to embed these experiences within a social feed makes it much easier for them to get distribution.
possible_solution:
For example,
1\. Vault Music can build a player for their 44K active DRiP subs so that they enjoy the music they’ve collected on DRiP within a social feed, and potentially buy more. 
2\. Andrew Mason can take custom photography orders & ship IRL frames to their 96k active DRiP subs.
3\. The wave can build a player for their 39K active DRiP subs to not only listen to their podcasts but also receive airdrops and perks from the featured brands and founders. 

Today, DRiP creators complement their channels by engaging with their audience on Twitter – in fact it is one of the things that Vibhu looks for as he onboards new creators. Allowing them to build a new revenue stream from their most loyal fans will motivate them to prioritise building a following on Twitter.
resources: 1\. Blinks Docs

Possible Solution:

For example, 1. Vault Music can build a player for their 44K active DRiP subs so that they enjoy the music they’ve collected on DRiP within a social feed, and potentially buy more.  2. Andrew Mason can take custom photography orders & ship IRL frames to their 96k active DRiP subs. 3. The wave can build a player for their 39K active DRiP subs to not only listen to their podcasts but also receive airdrops and perks from the featured brands and founders.  Today, DRiP creators complement their channels by engaging with their audience on Twitter – in fact it is one of the things that Vibhu looks for as he onboards new creators. name: Crypto-Powered Virtual Powered Plant
description:
The US Department of Energy [estimates](https://liftoff.energy.gov/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/LIFTOFF_DOE_VVP_10062023_v4.pdf) that between 2023 and 2030 ~140 GW worth of non-clean electricity will be retired. During the same period, electrification (e.g., proliferation of electric vehicles) will add ~60 GW of peak demand. Together this will create a ~200 GW peak supply-demand gap. Note that this DOE report, released in 2023, likely underestimated the increase in demand, as the full extent of additional electricity demand from AI data centers was not yet well understood at the time. The report also acknowledges that if the US was to go 100% clean energy, the gap would be even greater because solar and wind energy is inherently unstable.

How do we bridge this gap?
possible_solution:
One way is to build more power plants and to expand the grid, which, in order to supply 200 GW, could very well cost hundreds of billions of dollars. The other way is to deploy VPPs. 

A VPP aggregates and coordinates small-scale energy resources—such as thermostats, electric vehicles, home appliances, batteries, and solar panels—across thousands of homes and businesses. These aggregated resources can be flexibly managed to meet the grid's needs. For example, when the grid is overloaded, the VPP can automatically shift the timing of EV charging, or even dispatch energy from EVs back to the grid. This coordinated effort allows participants not only to contribute to grid stability but also to receive financial rewards for their role in preventing blackouts.

One helpful way to view VPPs is that they are marketplaces for energy. The supply side is households and their energy devices. The demand side is the grid. Like any marketplaces, VPPs have a network effect, and they need to reach a certain critical mass before they can become useful. And crypto is particularly good at bootstrapping the critical mass and ultimately accelerating the network effect.
resources:
1. [Full essay by Qiao Wang](https://x.com/QwQiao/status/1830961236151873902)
2. [State of Solana DePIN (2024) by Yash Agarwal](https://blog.superteam.fun/p/state-of-solana-depin-2024)
3. [Srcful](https://twitter.com/srcful): The First-ever Blockchain Powered Power Plant

Problem to Solve:

The US Department of Energy estimates that between 2023 and 2030 ~140 GW worth of non-clean electricity will be retired. During the same period, electrification (e.g., proliferation of electric vehicles) will add ~60 GW of peak demand. Together this will create a ~200 GW peak supply-demand gap. Note that this DOE report, released in 2023, likely underestimated the increase in demand, as the full extent of additional electricity demand from AI data centers was not yet well understood at the time. The report also acknowledges that if the US was to go 100% clean energy, the gap would be even greater because solar and wind energy is inherently unstable. How do we bridge this gap?

Possible Solution:

One way is to build more power plants and to expand the grid, which, in order to supply 200 GW, could very well cost hundreds of billions of dollars. The other way is to deploy VPPs. A VPP aggregates and coordinates small-scale energy resources—such as thermostats, electric vehicles, home appliances, batteries, and solar panels—across thousands of homes and businesses. These aggregated resources can be flexibly managed to meet the grid's needs. For example, when the grid is overloaded, the VPP can automatically shift the timing of EV charging, or even dispatch energy from EVs back to the grid. This coordinated effort allows participants not only to contribute to grid stability but also to receive financial rewards for their role in preventing blackouts. One helpful way to view VPPs is that they are marketplaces for energy. The supply side is households and their energy devices. The demand side is the grid. Like any marketplaces, VPPs have a network effect, and they need to reach a certain critical mass before they can become useful. And crypto is particularly good at bootstrapping the critical mass and ultimately accelerating the network effect.

1. Full essay by Qiao Wang 2. State of Solana DePIN (2024) by Yash Agarwal 3. Srcful: The First-ever Blockchain Powered Power Plant

name: TWAP program on Phoenix
description: Phoenix is the latest and leading on-chain order book on Solana, which has already facilitated over $250 million in trading volume. It also holds the distinction of being the first crankless limit order book DEX. The TWAP (Time-Weighted Average Price) program plays a critical role in any order book by enabling the execution of large orders over a defined timeframe, aiming to achieve an average price that closely aligns with the prevailing market price during that period. Developing a TWAP program for Phoenix will significantly aid in mitigating market impact and reducing slippage when trading substantial asset quantities.
possible_solution:
Proposed solution for implementing a TWAP program on the Phoenix Order book:
\- Construct an algorithm based on a sample TWAP algorithm, customized to suit the unique features of Phoenix.
\- Create a user interface (UI) or widget that empowers users to place TWAP orders. This entails inputting details such as the desired USD amount or asset quantity for buying or selling, alongside a designated time interval (period field) for execution.
resources:
\- Phoenix Github Repository - <https://github.com/Ellipsis-Labs/phoenix-v1>
\- Sample TWAP Program for DEX Implementation -<https://github.com/orbs-network/twap>
\- Phoenix Website and Documentation - <https://www.phoenix.trade/>

name: 1-click Mint-compressed NFTs
description: Existing NFT minting solutions are overly complex and prohibitively expensive, making them inaccessible for retail users interested in NFTs. The advent of compressed NFTs on Solana addresses the cost aspect, enabling the minting of 1 million NFTs for just 5 SOL. This breakthrough has the potential to open doors for a diverse array of retail engagement use-cases, including marketplaces, events, e-commerce, restaurants, and more.
possible_solution:
Solutions that facilitate one-click minting of compressed NFTs hold the key to unlocking broader accessibility. TipLink, a lightweight wallet designed for effortless digital asset transfers through simple links, presents an ideal candidate to drive these developments. The potential solution could harness TipLink to construct web-based applications encompassing the following scenarios:
\- Claiming Movie NFTs: Offering movie-themed NFTs after viewing films or attending events like Coachella.
\- Restaurant Loyalty Programs: Implementing loyalty programs within restaurants, fostering customer engagement and retention.
\- Customer Acquisition Tool: Deploying NFTs as a customer acquisition tool for platforms like Airbnb.

The user should be able to claim NFTs with a single click, without the need for any wallet (enabled through TipLink). This streamlined process aims to provide users with an experience akin to using any other conventional web2 application.
resources:

\- TipLink Documentation - <https://docs.tiplink.io/>
\- Underdog Documentation - <https://underdog.readme.io/reference/getting-started-1>
\- Loyalty Program Case Study - <https://superteam.substack.com/p/turbocharging-airbnbs-loyalty-program>

name: LST Options Vaults on Solana
description: LSTs like JitoSOL and mSOL are underutilized in Solana DeFi. Users earn passive yield, but there’s no way to amplify returns or hedge risk without unstaking or complex strategies. Meanwhile, options markets on Solana are still shallow and lack real utility-driven demand.
possible_solution:
**LST Options Vaults** let users deposit LSTs into automated covered call or put-selling strategies.
- Earn **extra yield** from options premiums on top of staking rewards
- Choose risk levels (strike %, expiry, etc.) via a simple UI
- LPs get vault shares, and protocols like PsyOptions or Zeta can plug in for execution
- Helps bootstrap **real demand** for Solana-native options markets

LSTs like JitoSOL and mSOL are underutilized in Solana DeFi. Users earn passive yield, but there’s no way to amplify returns or hedge risk without unstaking or complex strategies. Meanwhile, options markets on Solana are still shallow and lack real utility-driven demand.

name: Crypto Credit Card
description: Let users spend their crypto like fiat.
possible_solution:
Card connects directly to your wallet. The main things you get are:
1\. automatic yield from lending protocols on all your holdings
2\. easy withdrawals/deposits from/to your bank
3\. portfolio management within the same app: lets you ape into majors and rebalance (this is not a trading product so long tail of shitcoins not important)
4\. stupid banks can’t tell you when you can / can’t withdraw your money — the whole point of crypto. I hate the Orwelian banking system we have today.
Bonus:
1\. Security controls if you want to allow only some amount of spend/only certain vendors
2\. Advanced derivative / vault strategies for yield

If you’re successful enough, the endgame could be becoming a bank yourself.
resources:
1\. Full Essay with GTM and Biz Model Ideas
2\. KAST Finance
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Let users spend their crypto like fiat.

Possible Solution:

Card connects directly to your wallet. The main things you get are: 1. automatic yield from lending protocols on all your holdings 2. easy withdrawals/deposits from/to your bank 3. portfolio management within the same app: lets you ape into majors and rebalance (this is not a trading product so long tail of shitcoins not important) 4. stupid banks can’t tell you when you can / can’t withdraw your money — the whole point of crypto. I hate the Orwelian banking system we have today. Bonus: 1. Security controls if you want to allow only some amount of spend/only certain vendors 2. Advanced derivative / vault strategies for yield If you’re successful enough, the endgame could be becoming a bank yourself.

1. Full Essay with GTM and Biz Model Ideas 2. KAST Finance

This unlocks both a new audience and a seamless trial experience that can drive growth for both Blinks & the Griffy App.

name: crypto powered economic co-ordination networks for ai agents:
description:
crypto is powerful in co-ordinating capital and incentives. DePIN is a good example, where crypto is already being used for capital bootstrapping and co-ordination for decentralised compute (eg [@akashnet\_](https://x.com/@akashnet_))

[bittensor](https://bittensor.com/) (or tao ecosystem) is a good glimpse of financial engineering of the ai value chain (training and inference )and has created a $4b ecosystem (though it's mostly bloated valuations). 

in 2025, i personally think most of training/inference plays are almost done and we're moving more towards the tooling stack (i.e ai agents) or post-training.
possible_solution:
as [@balajis](https://x.com/@balajis) has [been advocating](https://balajis.com/p/ai-is-polytheistic-not-monotheistic): ai verifiability will become critical problem for crypto to solve. 

imagine proof-of-stake networks like solana, but for specific use-cases like agents trust marketplace, identity, memory, and so on - which solves for both composability and trust.

[Perle Labs](https://x.com/PerleLabs) is a good example of using crypto co-ordination networks for data.

further, crypto is an excellent capital bootstrapping mechanism for new ai communities and cults to form (like cyberpunk movement).

2. similar projects: gokite.ai, bittensor, and most depin projects.

name: RWA Standard using Token Extensions
description: The Solana Ecosystem lacks a unified, widely accepted standard for tokenizing RWAs. Just as standards like JPEG and PDF formats standardized files across different devices and facilitated the sharing of content on the internet, a standardized RWA standard can help boost RWAs for Solana. Think of it this way: Just as Metaplex made it easy to create NFTs, a standardized RWA token can simplify the process of creating tokenized RWAs.
possible_solution:
Create a RWA standard leveraging token extensions like:
1\. Transfer hooks for checking whether an address is whitelisted or not.
2\. Interest-bearing tokens
3\. Transfer fees
And more. The token extensions are created with RWAs in mind.

A SaaS tool can be created which enables institutions to deploy RWAs.

More token extensions can be mixed and matched to allow a higher feature set of tokens.
resources:
1\. The State of Solana RWAs by Yash Agarwal (2023)
2\. A Primer on Solana's Token Extensions: The Superteam Blog
3\. Token Extensions Docs
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

The Solana Ecosystem lacks a unified, widely accepted standard for tokenizing RWAs. Just as standards like JPEG and PDF formats standardized files across different devices and facilitated the sharing of content on the internet, a standardized RWA standard can help boost RWAs for Solana. Think of it this way: Just as Metaplex made it easy to create NFTs, a standardized RWA token can simplify the process of creating tokenized RWAs.

Possible Solution:

Create a RWA standard leveraging token extensions like: 1. Transfer hooks for checking whether an address is whitelisted or not. 2. Interest-bearing tokens 3. Transfer fees And more. The token extensions are created with RWAs in mind. A SaaS tool can be created which enables institutions to deploy RWAs. More token extensions can be mixed and matched to allow a higher feature set of tokens.

1. The State of Solana RWAs by Yash Agarwal (2023) 2. A Primer on Solana's Token Extensions: The Superteam Blog 3. Token Extensions Docs

description: When making payments to a merchant, users may possess USDC, while the merchant exclusively accepts USDT. This discrepancy creates the inconvenience of having to convert these tokens on a swapping platform before ultimately completing the payment for the purchase.

This integration would enable users to directly swap their USDC for the desired USDT or any other preferred token. Consequently, this approach streamlines the payment process, eliminating the need to navigate away from the merchant's page and ensuring smooth token swapping, all powered by Jupiter's technology.

name: Flatcoins
description: Currently, stablecoins tend to be excessively centralized, whereas decentralized currencies like BTC and ETH lack stability. A superior substitute for fiat-backed stablecoins is necessary – one that is not only inflation-resistant but also functions as an improved form of currency.
possible_solution: Flatcoins are like stablecoins, but instead of being tied to assets like the US dollar, they're linked to the cost of living. This keeps their purchasing power steady. A form of money that resists inflation by monitoring the Consumer Price Index (CPI) and can be backed by a diversified selection of real-world assets. Several ongoing projects are already exploring this concept, including Nuon, Ampleforth/Spot, and ISC.
resources:
\- ISC Money - <https://www.isc.money>
\- Flatcoin Explainer - <https://blockworks.co/news/nuon-flatcoin-targets-inflation>
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Currently, stablecoins tend to be excessively centralized, whereas decentralized currencies like BTC and ETH lack stability. A superior substitute for fiat-backed stablecoins is necessary – one that is not only inflation-resistant but also functions as an improved form of currency.

Possible Solution:
