author: Yash Agarwal

name: Open-Grants Protocol for Solana
description:
The biggest challenge in the current process of grants and hackathons is, it’s way too CENTRALISED! There are pros and cons to it. The biggest pro is, faster turnaround time and hence, faster decision-making. For instance, Superteam Instagrants are disbursed within just ~48 hours, if approved. Implying, the Solana foundation or any central organization responsible for grants is better suited for shortlisting and judging deserving projects.

However, the lack of a decentralized grants program also leads to more closed-source programs, as the projects are not incentivized enough to open-source their programs with very low community participation in them.
possible_solution:
The Open Grants protocol will be similar to Openbook [fork of Project Serum], but for matching grants instead of matching trades. This can be entirely on-chain i.e Solana and will be accompanied by an SDK and a reference open-source frontend implementation for easy integrations.

A few components of the Open Grants architecture:

\- Frontends/UIs:
    \- There can be as many as Frontends possible and they can compete against each other to attract more sponsors, funders, project creators, and so on. 
    \- They will be independent to decide their own fees, filtering mechanisms, and so on. 
    \- However, since all of the funds and data are aggregated into a single protocol, the liquidity will be concentrated and will avoid any fragmentation of grants - making it truly composable as well.
\- Protocol:
    \- Once collected by the frontends, all the metadata and funds of the various stakeholders like Projects, communities, and Sponsors finally get aggregated into the protocol. 
    \- All the logic and calculations of Quadratic Funding, Funds disbursal, and Milestones can be embedded in the protocol itself. 
    \- The changes can be governed by the community, which can even be a DAO and for an obvious reason, all this will be open-source.
\- Governance:
    \- Similar to Gitcoin, a governance token would make sense to decentralize the community into a DAO. 
    \- However, a token isn’t necessary and it can be very well governed by a set of trusted multi-sigs, who can make decisions and signs on behalf of the community. 
    \- For instance, few members can be appointed from Solana Foundation or any major community voices can be elected to be part of the multi-sig group.

More details here: <https://litmosis.substack.com/p/understanding-gitcoin-and-envisioning-for-solana>
resources:
\- Cubik (Quadratic Funding Platform on Solana) by Irffan and Dhruvraj: <https://www.cubik.so/>
\- Stockpile (crowdfunding platform on Solana) by 0xSavant - <https://www.stockpile.pro/>
\- Read the entire idea in this essay, written by us to get few concrete models around it - <https://open.substack.com/pub/litmosis/p/understanding-gitcoin-and-envisioning-for-solana>
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

The biggest challenge in the current process of grants and hackathons is, it’s way too CENTRALISED! There are pros and cons to it. The biggest pro is, faster turnaround time and hence, faster decision-making. For instance, Superteam Instagrants are disbursed within just ~48 hours, if approved. Implying, the Solana foundation or any central organization responsible for grants is better suited for shortlisting and judging deserving projects. However, the lack of a decentralized grants program also leads to more closed-source programs, as the projects are not incentivized enough to open-source their programs with very low community participation in them.

Possible Solution:

The Open Grants protocol will be similar to Openbook [fork of Project Serum], but for matching grants instead of matching trades. This can be entirely on-chain i.e Solana and will be accompanied by an SDK and a reference open-source frontend implementation for easy integrations. A few components of the Open Grants architecture: - Frontends/UIs: - There can be as many as Frontends possible and they can compete against each other to attract more sponsors, funders, project creators, and so on. - They will be independent to decide their own fees, filtering mechanisms, and so on. - However, since all of the funds and data are aggregated into a single protocol, the liquidity will be concentrated and will avoid any fragmentation of grants - making it truly composable as well. - Protocol: - Once collected by the frontends, all the metadata and funds of the various stakeholders like Projects, communities, and Sponsors finally get aggregated into the protocol. - All the logic and calculations of Quadratic Funding, Funds disbursal, and Milestones can be embedded in the protocol itself. - The changes can be governed by the community, which can even be a DAO and for an obvious reason, all this will be open-source. - Governance: - Similar to Gitcoin, a governance token would make sense to decentralize the community into a DAO. - However, a token isn’t necessary and it can be very well governed by a set of trusted multi-sigs, who can make decisions and signs on behalf of the community. - For instance, few members can be appointed from Solana Foundation or any major community voices can be elected to be part of the multi-sig group. More details here: https://litmosis.substack.com/p/understanding-gitcoin-and-envisioning-for-solana

- Cubik (Quadratic Funding Platform on Solana) by Irffan and Dhruvraj: https://www.cubik.so/ - Stockpile (crowdfunding platform on Solana) by 0xSavant - https://www.stockpile.pro/ - Read the entire idea in this essay, written by us to get few concrete models around it - https://open.substack.com/pub/litmosis/p/understanding-gitcoin-and-envisioning-for-solana

name: Proof-of-Location Aggregator
description:
DePIN location proofs are like to DEX order quotes: from users' point of view, tapping into one DePIN for location proofs (or DEX for trades) is far less valuable than aggregating across multiple DePINs/DEXs.

In DeFi, this led to the rise of aggregators like 1inch/Paraswap. In DePIN, it's a wide open opportunity.
possible_solution:
A proof-of-location aggregator can serve many types of use cases. Depending on the requirements for precision/latency/cost, the aggregator can ping different types of geo-positioning networks (GPS/RTK/BLE/LoRA) or a combination of multiple networks for redundancy

Challenges:
- integrating with multiple DePINs
- managing DAO politics & negotiations
- dealing with privacy concerns (real & perceived)
resources: 1. [State of Solana DePIN (2024) by Yash Agarwal](https://blog.superteam.fun/p/state-of-solana-depin-2024)
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

DePIN location proofs are like to DEX order quotes: from users' point of view, tapping into one DePIN for location proofs (or DEX for trades) is far less valuable than aggregating across multiple DePINs/DEXs. In DeFi, this led to the rise of aggregators like 1inch/Paraswap. In DePIN, it's a wide open opportunity.

Possible Solution:

Depending on the requirements for precision/latency/cost, the aggregator can ping different types of geo-positioning networks (GPS/RTK/BLE/LoRA) or a combination of multiple networks for redundancy Challenges: - integrating with multiple DePINs - managing DAO politics & negotiations - dealing with privacy concerns (real & perceived)

name: Crypto Bounties using Blinks
description:
Superteam Earn is the most comprehensive hub for earning opportunities on Solana. Over 30K users across the world have signed up to access 350+ earning opportunities and made over $100K in earnings. 
Recently, the most popular bounty format is a twitter thread that projects use to generate organic awareness in the ecosystem.
possible_solution:
A project can expect anywhere between 30 - 80 submissions for a total payout of $500 - $1000. What makes this more effective than running ads is that each thread goes out from a unique handle with its own voice to its own audience.
The ideal Superteam Earn experience on Blinks will allows users to,
1\. discover open bounties within their X feed
2\. write their thread and have their submissions tracked against their X account
3\. get instant payouts within the app when the winners are declared
This creates a new and integrated distributed channel for Earn & helps onboard productive users to Blinks and Solana.
resources:

2\. Bountycaster (built on Farcaster)

author: Aditya Shetty

name: Crypto + Immigration
description: It’s really hard to figure out who to let into the country and who not to. The best systems to date have probably been those similar to Canada’s points systems, but those are hard to design (look at UK failing after leaving the EU).
possible_solution:
One interesting way would be to let citizens “back” candidates with crypto.

Could go something like this:
1\. government issues $TALENT coin to citizens, some amount per year
2\. you stake $TALENT on individuals you believe will be accretive to your country’s economy
3\. if they accumulate enough $TALENT, they can immigrate and work
4\. if they create economic value (salary, capital), a portion of that goes back to $TALENT holders that backed them
5\. the earlier you backed someone, the higher % of their econ value you earn (bonding curve)
6\. if your previous $TALENT allocations were successful, next year government gives you more (since you clearly know what you’re doing) — and vice versa, if your previous $TALENT allocations were bad, next year government gives you less (slashes you)

The result is an immigration system built entirely on referrals from existing citizens. They back who they want to see immigrate into the country. There is no need for artificial points schemes or for government officials to ever become “experts” in what the country needs and how to find it.

The best part is, the system is entirely fluid and self adjusting. Need more engineers? Stake $TALENT on engineers. Need doctors? Stake on those med school grads from other countries.
resources: 1\. Full Essay with GTM and Biz Model Ideas
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

It’s really hard to figure out who to let into the country and who not to. The best systems to date have probably been those similar to Canada’s points systems, but those are hard to design (look at UK failing after leaving the EU).

Possible Solution:

One interesting way would be to let citizens “back” candidates with crypto. Could go something like this: 1. government issues $TALENT coin to citizens, some amount per year 2. you stake $TALENT on individuals you believe will be accretive to your country’s economy 3. if they accumulate enough $TALENT, they can immigrate and work 4. if they create economic value (salary, capital), a portion of that goes back to $TALENT holders that backed them 5. the earlier you backed someone, the higher % of their econ value you earn (bonding curve) 6. if your previous $TALENT allocations were successful, next year government gives you more (since you clearly know what you’re doing) — and vice versa, if your previous $TALENT allocations were bad, next year government gives you less (slashes you) The result is an immigration system built entirely on referrals from existing citizens. They back who they want to see immigrate into the country. There is no need for artificial points schemes or for government officials to ever become “experts” in what the country needs and how to find it. The best part is, the system is entirely fluid and self adjusting. Need more engineers? Stake $TALENT on engineers. Need doctors? Stake on those med school grads from other countries.

name: Decentralized Blockchain-Based Ride-Hailing System
description: Current ride-hailing services are centralized, leading to issues like lack of transparency, unfair commission fees, and disputes over driver or rider reviews. Additionally, in regions with limited banking facilities, paying via traditional methods is challenging. This system aims to create a transparent, fraud-resistant, and decentralized platform where drivers and riders interact directly, with secure payments and immutable ratings.
possible_solution: Implement a blockchain-based ride-hailing platform that employs smart contracts to manage ride requests, payments, and ratings. Drivers register their vehicles and identities on the blockchain, verified via community consensus. Riders request rides via a decentralized app, which locks crypto payments upfront. GPS oracles verify pickup/drop-off, and smart contracts release payments upon ride completion, ensuring trustless transactions without intermediaries.
resources:
- Blockchain platforms 
- Decentralized identity verification tools.
- GPS oracles for real-time ride verification.
- Development tools for building decentralized apps (dApps).
- Cryptocurrency wallets for transactions.
article_content:

Current ride-hailing services are centralized, leading to issues like lack of transparency, unfair commission fees, and disputes over driver or rider reviews. Additionally, in regions with limited banking facilities, paying via traditional methods is challenging. This system aims to create a transparent, fraud-resistant, and decentralized platform where drivers and riders interact directly, with secure payments and immutable ratings.

Possible Solution:

Implement a blockchain-based ride-hailing platform that employs smart contracts to manage ride requests, payments, and ratings. Drivers register their vehicles and identities on the blockchain, verified via community consensus. Riders request rides via a decentralized app, which locks crypto payments upfront. GPS oracles verify pickup/drop-off, and smart contracts release payments upon ride completion, ensuring trustless transactions without intermediaries.

author: Benjamin Richard

name: Buy Now Pay Later on Solana Pay
description:
Enable consumers to pay on credit lines (BNPL) while paying through Solana Pay. BNPL is a $100 Billion+ market, giving a huge market opportunity to tap into, and it also requires bootstrapping the merchant network, just like Solana Pay!

There aren't enough user and merchant motivation to use Solana Pay; BNPL can change that.
possible_solution:
\- Decide a credit limit based on different tiers:
    \- Good on-chain history
    \- KYC’d
    \- FinTech guarantee
    \- The credit increases as more data points are collected
\- [Initially, the money is lent via DeFi Lending pools, which earn as the yield from merchant fees]
\- Keep paying via Solana Pay till they reach the credit limit and billing at the end of the month.
\- Payment via “Permissioned Stablecoins” to restrict P2P transfers and enable payments only on the whitelisted merchant's network.
\- Merchants should be registered on the BNPL network and can be:
    \- POS
    \- E-commerce
\- They can pay 1% as BNPL increases the consumer spending capacity, hence sales
resources:
\- Solana Pay Docs - <https://docs.solanapay.com/>
\- BNPL Pay - <https://bnplpay.io/>
\- Atpay - <https://atpay.io/>

Enable consumers to pay on credit lines (BNPL) while paying through Solana Pay. BNPL is a $100 Billion+ market, giving a huge market opportunity to tap into, and it also requires bootstrapping the merchant network, just like Solana Pay! There aren't enough user and merchant motivation to use Solana Pay; BNPL can change that.

Possible Solution:

- Decide a credit limit based on different tiers: - Good on-chain history - KYC’d - FinTech guarantee - The credit increases as more data points are collected - [Initially, the money is lent via DeFi Lending pools, which earn as the yield from merchant fees] - Keep paying via Solana Pay till they reach the credit limit and billing at the end of the month. - Payment via “Permissioned Stablecoins” to restrict P2P transfers and enable payments only on the whitelisted merchant's network. - Merchants should be registered on the BNPL network and can be: - POS - E-commerce - They can pay 1% as BNPL increases the consumer spending capacity, hence sales

name: Social Media Embed for Solana NFTs with Ownership Display
description: There is a growing demand for easy ways to showcase Solana NFTs on social media platforms, along with information about the current owner's Twitter handle. Existing solutions do not provide a simple URL that generates a summary card with a large image and ownership information, making it difficult for users to share and display their NFTs on social media.
possible_solution: A social media embed service for Solana NFTs that generates a summary card with a large image and the owner's Twitter handle. Users can input a mint address to create a dead-simple URL, such as [showoff.me/:mint-address](http://showoff.me/:mint-address), which automatically generates the embed with ownership information. The platform leverages the Cardinal Twitter integration to display the correct owner's Twitter handle. It should makes it easy for NFT owners to share and showcase their assets on social media platforms, enhancing their online presence and promoting NFT adoption.
resources: N/A

Problem to Solve:

There is a growing demand for easy ways to showcase Solana NFTs on social media platforms, along with information about the current owner's Twitter handle. Users can input a mint address to create a dead-simple URL, such as showoff.me/:mint-address, which automatically generates the embed with ownership information. name: NFT-based Gaming SuperApp
description: With many Web3 games coming up, gamers would want a single platform to access all games. In Web2, platforms like Miniclip or poki aggregator have various mini-games under one umbrella. A similar platform for Web3 will be needed in the near future.
possible_solution:
A gaming super-app where gamers can discover, access, and play an array of casual web3 games from a single app. Think of it as the early days of ‘Facebook Gaming,’ where people used to log in to Facebook to play those casual games!
To make it a unified experience, the following features can be incorporated:
1\. A single currency to be used in all apps in the marketplace/super-app, which automatically gets converted to the respective in-game currency in the backend. 
2\. Cross-chain experience: Aggregating games across multiple chains, like Polygon and Avalanche, to abstract the experience for a Solana user via cross-chain messaging frameworks like Wormhole
3\. Recommendation Engine: Discover the hottest games, which are optimised as per incentivisation or better gameplay
4\. Integrated On/Off-ramps
5\. Cross-usage of gaming assets in the form of NFTs across all gaming apps in the ecosystem leveraging the power of composability.
resources:
\- Solana Mobile Stack - <https://solanamobile.com/developers>
\- Solana Gaming Developers Kit - <https://solana.com/developers/gaming>

name: Cross-chain Yield Aggregator
description:
Enabling users to discover the best yields across multiple chains and invest from their preferred chain in universal assets like USDC.

A 'Universal Yield Marketplace' can be created, which would search for the best yields across chains.
possible_solution:
Earning yield across chains is currently a fragmented process, which involves searching, analyzing, bridging, swapping, depositing, claiming, and withdrawing, which is both laborious and complex.

For instance: Deposit of USDC on Solana → USDC is bridged to Arbitrum & deposited into Pendle\_fi → User receives a tokenized yield position back on Solana.

Another adjacent idea to explore in yield aggregation: Cross-Chain (Re)Staking or Cross-Chain Restaking, where one can stake their ETH (or wrapped ETH) or wrapped SOL from any chain.

Imagine being able to stake or restake your WETH on Solana and earn yields or farm points!
resources:
1\. Superteam Blog
2\. Wormhole Docs
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Enabling users to discover the best yields across multiple chains and invest from their preferred chain in universal assets like USDC. A 'Universal Yield Marketplace' can be created, which would search for the best yields across chains.

Possible Solution:

Earning yield across chains is currently a fragmented process, which involves searching, analyzing, bridging, swapping, depositing, claiming, and withdrawing, which is both laborious and complex. For instance: Deposit of USDC on Solana → USDC is bridged to Arbitrum & deposited into Pendle_fi → User receives a tokenized yield position back on Solana. Another adjacent idea to explore in yield aggregation: Cross-Chain (Re)Staking or Cross-Chain Restaking, where one can stake their ETH (or wrapped ETH) or wrapped SOL from any chain. Imagine being able to stake or restake your WETH on Solana and earn yields or farm points!

author: Yash Agarwal

name: Curve v2
description: Constant product pools (x\*y=k) are inefficient, while CLMMs require LPs to manually determine ranges in which to provide liquidity and adjust position as prices change. Curve v2 pools enable lazy liquidity provision even while concentrating liquidity.
possible_solution:
Curve is most well-known for its stableswap AMM, but its v2 pools enable concentrated liquidity for non-pegged assets. This creates 5~10 times greater liquidity than the constant product AMM. A major difference from Uni v3 style CLMMs is that LPs can remain passive; there is no need to adjust your positions, as the pool makes adjustments automatically.

This would be an extremely useful DeFi primitive to have on Solana, as it is more efficient than constant product pools, it is more suited to retail LPs than CLMMs are, and it can support any pair of assets.
resources:
\- Curve v2 pools and their levers: <https://curve.substack.com/i/136740473/curve-v-pools-and-their-levers>
\- Curve Docs: <https://docs.curve.fi/references/whitepapers/cryptoswap/>
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Constant product pools (x*y=k) are inefficient, while CLMMs require LPs to manually determine ranges in which to provide liquidity and adjust position as prices change. Curve v2 pools enable lazy liquidity provision even while concentrating liquidity.

Possible Solution:

Curve is most well-known for its stableswap AMM, but its v2 pools enable concentrated liquidity for non-pegged assets. This creates 5~10 times greater liquidity than the constant product AMM. A major difference from Uni v3 style CLMMs is that LPs can remain passive; there is no need to adjust your positions, as the pool makes adjustments automatically. This would be an extremely useful DeFi primitive to have on Solana, as it is more efficient than constant product pools, it is more suited to retail LPs than CLMMs are, and it can support any pair of assets.

name: Tipping Links on Solana
description: Businesses, Freelancers, Creators, and DAOs have to send over their wallet addresses to another party and check manually, whether they have received payment or not. Further, these payments are not tied to any particular invoice, making tracking transactions and accounting difficult.
possible_solution:
A No-code solution for anyone to create a payment request link, and enable them to receive one-time payments from their clients or users.
User Flow: Create a link -> Share the link -> Get Paid

Must-have Features:
\- Custom, expirable, and revokable payment links
\- Custom stripe/Shopify-style brandings like Color and Profile picture
\- Tracking Dashboard with metrics
\- Email notifications on successful payments
\- Invoicing Page with the payment button

Moonshot Features: 
‍
\- Off-ramp to Bank Account Integration
\- Automatic swapping using Jup.ag APIs
\- Receive cross-chain payments from 17+ chains using wormhole
\- Escrow accounts for storing funds

\- Stripe Payment Links - <https://stripe.com/en-in/payments/payment-links>

name: Cross-chain Money Markets
description:
Enabling users to deposit assets as collateral on Chain A & borrow assets on Chain B. This would act as a 'Universal Liquidity Protocol.'

For instance, a Solana user has SOL and wants to explore Sui. They will have to either:
1\. Swap SOL for USDC on Solana <-> Bridge <-> Swap USDC for SUI on Sui
2\. Lend SOL on Solana protocol <-> Bridge <-> Borrow SUI on Sui protocol
possible_solution: One can create an allowing user to deposit SOL on Solana & borrow SUI on Sui as per collateral ratio. This enables them to leverage assets cross-chain!

2\. Circle's CCTP
3\. Wormhole's Cross-Chain Messaging Layer
4\. Superteam Blog
5\. Wormhole Docs

name: Cursor/Perplexity for Solana
description:
we believe crypto x ai chat apps — or “chatgpt for crypto” — were too early. the technology simply wasn’t ready. models struggled with tool calling and understanding crypto-specific context. there were a few strong teams experimenting, but overall things were constrained by model limitations and their ability to execute actions.

today, the landscape is shifting. chat apps are finally becoming agentic. deep research was the first glimpse, and now chatgpt agents are showing what’s possible. models like claude sonnet 4 and kimi k2 are already impressively strong at tool calling.
possible_solution:
in our view, every new version of foundation models (chatgpt, claude, gemini, llama) will increasingly focus on agentic actions. the latest chatgpt release is already far better at both agentic workflows and generative ui.

while simple actions like swaps may not be best suited for chat interfaces, complex workflows such as trading strategies are a clear killer use case.
we’re especially bullish on mcp as a tool-calling standard — it makes tools composable with apps and interoperable with each other.

our conviction: the future of agents will be built on mcp servers, or networks of them. these servers will either:
- fetch and read data (e.g., prices),
- take actions (e.g., swaps),
- or run prompts directly.
resources:
1. SendAI OSS MCP: <https://github.com/sendaifun/solana-mcp>
2. Solana Agent Kit by SendAI: <https://github.com/sendaifun/solana-agent-kit>

name: Fully On-Chain Prediction Market with Meteora DBC-Based Instant Settlement
description:
Current prediction markets rely on off-chain orderbook mechanisms because they lack efficient on-chain price discovery. This creates several issues:
1. **Settlement delays**: Traditional prediction markets require waiting for events to resolve before participants can claim winnings
2. **Liquidity fragmentation**: Off-chain orderbooks separate liquidity from the blockchain's native composability
3. **Capital inefficiency**: Users must lock capital until event resolution, reducing overall market efficiency
4. **Trust assumptions**: Off-chain components introduce centralization risks and require users to trust intermediaries
The core challenge is that existing on-chain prediction markets haven't leveraged modern DeFi primitives to create truly efficient, instant-settling mechanisms that can operate entirely on-chain.
possible_solution:
A fully on-chain prediction market where YES and NO tokens are issued on top of a Dynamic Bonding Curve (DBC), enabling instant settlements without orderbooks.
**Key mechanism:**
- Instead of traditional orderbooks, use a two-sided bonding curve (y = x²) that moves up and down based on YES/NO demand
- Bets are placed by buying into the curve, which automatically adjusts pricing based on market sentiment
- The curve allows liquidity providers to earn yield per trade after results are published
- Tokens are burnt when the event resolves, and USDC is distributed to winners immediately
- No need for gradual selling - instant settlement upon event resolution
resources:
<https://docs.polymarket.com/polymarket-learn/get-started/what-is-polymarket>
<https://docs.meteora.ag/overview/products/dbc/what-is-dbc>
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Current prediction markets rely on off-chain orderbook mechanisms because they lack efficient on-chain price discovery. This creates several issues: 1. Settlement delays: Traditional prediction markets require waiting for events to resolve before participants can claim winnings 2. Liquidity fragmentation: Off-chain orderbooks separate liquidity from the blockchain's native composability 3. Capital inefficiency: Users must lock capital until event resolution, reducing overall market efficiency 4. Trust assumptions: Off-chain components introduce centralization risks and require users to trust intermediaries The core challenge is that existing on-chain prediction markets haven't leveraged modern DeFi primitives to create truly efficient, instant-settling mechanisms that can operate entirely on-chain.

Possible Solution:

A fully on-chain prediction market where YES and NO tokens are issued on top of a Dynamic Bonding Curve (DBC), enabling instant settlements without orderbooks. Key mechanism: - Instead of traditional orderbooks, use a two-sided bonding curve (y = x²) that moves up and down based on YES/NO demand - Bets are placed by buying into the curve, which automatically adjusts pricing based on market sentiment - The curve allows liquidity providers to earn yield per trade after results are published - Tokens are burnt when the event resolves, and USDC is distributed to winners immediately - No need for gradual selling - instant settlement upon event resolution

https://docs.polymarket.com/polymarket-learn/get-started/what-is-polymarket https://docs.meteora.ag/overview/products/dbc/what-is-dbc
author: Harsh Ghodkar

name: Fully Onchain Games
description:
On-chain games are those where the entirety of the game state and game logic (not just in-game assets likes currencies and NFTs.) is written to the blockchain.

Consider Tic-Tac-Toe. The concept of turns, a 3x3 board, players and a sequence of three must be unchangeably documented as the rules. The history of player moves must also be recorded. On the other hand, the colors of the board and pieces, the shapes of the pieces (X and O can become Y and Z for all we care), the animations, the sounds, etc. can all stay on the client-side.
possible_solution:
Putting all states and logic onchain unlocks all sorts of novel behaviors:
\- Smart contract-based players (as opposed to human players) that play the game. One of the earliest instantiations of this is actually MEV in DeFi. Trading is the game and MEV bots are smart contract-based players. Notice that in this case the smart contract-based players not only compete with each other, but also compete with human players.
\- Game mods built by third-party developers without fear of getting deplatformed. Mods are the lifeblood of Web2 games, and many generational games started out as mods (eg, Dota 2, Counter-Strike, and PUPG). However mod devs are at the mercy of the original game studio due to the permissioned nature of Web2 games. In fact many have gotten either de-platformed or would never have started modding for fear of getting deplatformed.
Eventually, the game studio can become a game publisher. If Web2 history is any indication, it’s highly likely that the first successful onchain game engine comes from a successful onchain game studio. Think Unreal (the engine) and Epic Games (the studio). By the same token, the first successful crypto game publisher will also likely come from a successful crypto game studio. Think Steam (the publisher) and Valve (the studio).
resources:
1\. Solana Gaming Resources by MagicBlock
2\. Gameshift by Solana Labs
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

On-chain games are those where the entirety of the game state and game logic (not just in-game assets likes currencies and NFTs.) is written to the blockchain. Consider Tic-Tac-Toe. The concept of turns, a 3x3 board, players and a sequence of three must be unchangeably documented as the rules. The history of player moves must also be recorded. On the other hand, the colors of the board and pieces, the shapes of the pieces (X and O can become Y and Z for all we care), the animations, the sounds, etc. can all stay on the client-side.

Possible Solution:

Putting all states and logic onchain unlocks all sorts of novel behaviors: - Smart contract-based players (as opposed to human players) that play the game. One of the earliest instantiations of this is actually MEV in DeFi. Trading is the game and MEV bots are smart contract-based players. Notice that in this case the smart contract-based players not only compete with each other, but also compete with human players. - Game mods built by third-party developers without fear of getting deplatformed. Mods are the lifeblood of Web2 games, and many generational games started out as mods (eg, Dota 2, Counter-Strike, and PUPG). However mod devs are at the mercy of the original game studio due to the permissioned nature of Web2 games. In fact many have gotten either de-platformed or would never have started modding for fear of getting deplatformed. Eventually, the game studio can become a game publisher. If Web2 history is any indication, it’s highly likely that the first successful onchain game engine comes from a successful onchain game studio. Think Unreal (the engine) and Epic Games (the studio). By the same token, the first successful crypto game publisher will also likely come from a successful crypto game studio. Think Steam (the publisher) and Valve (the studio).

name: Sports betting analytics
description: Sports betting is growing rapidly in the web3 world, yet there is still no practical solution to collecting & analyzing data and trends in the space. This data would open a whole new world to bettors, allowing them to bet with more confidence by betting on or against trends, certain high profile bettors, or other factors.
possible_solution: A data and analytics platform which easily extracts betting data & trends from the blockchain and displays them in an easy-to-manipulate interface. This platform could include: tools to identify target wallets with a high propensity to win certain wagers (ie. smart money betters), tools to send instantaneous alerts of high-profile wallet movements or ‘whale’ movements to subscribers, or tools to track your own personal profit/loss, winning %, trends, etc so users can make more informed wagers going forward.
resources:
\- Monaco Protocol - <https://github.com/MonacoProtocol>
\- Monaco Protocol Docs - <https://docs.monacoprotocol.xyz/>

name: Wellness Crypto Apps
description:
We’re seeing increased demand for health diagnostics, quantified self devices, and a more sophisticated understanding of biomarkers + therapeutics for longevity. But there is a bottleneck. The current issue for training next-generation models is the lack of accessible, high-quality health data. 

The goal has long been to develop domain-specific models—such as those for healthcare —but these require high-quality datasets that often do not exist or are siloed, making them unusable.
possible_solution:
Presently, foundational models are trained on the entirety of the internet, but much of this data is gobbledygook, leading to model degradation as machines train on machine-generated data. 

We believe there is an opportunity to solve some of the above via real financial incentives and high-quality preventative health services which can create a compelling ecosystem.

name: Scan and Pay Integration for Merchants
description:
The lack of a seamless POS solution for merchants is hindering the adoption of Solana Pay. The following are their needs:
\- No extra efforts for integration and should have a smooth learning curve
\- Should fit well into their existing workflows
\- Shouldn’t require any additional setup and settle in Fiat, if needed
possible_solution:
\- Ability to generate static as well as dynamic QRs.
\- Merchants can have static (permanent) QRs in their shops without requiring any devices
‍
\- The POS machine can also have a Dynamic QR, which automatically fills in the amount to be paid by the user. This Dynamic QR can be generated by the merchant by just entering the amount, similar to a credit card POS.
‍
\- An Android SDK can be developed to enable any Android device (including Saga) to become a Solana Pay POS or can be integrated into existing payment aggregator apps.
\- Accept Credit Cards and other Fiat payments methods as well for a complete solution to merchants (Can integrate existing FinTech APIs like Stripe)
\- Off-ramping of USDC to Bank Account (Can use existing APIs developed by Wyre, Circle etc)
resources: \- Solana Pay - <https://solanapay.com/>

The lack of a seamless POS solution for merchants is hindering the adoption of Solana Pay. Possible Solution:

This Dynamic QR can be generated by the merchant by just entering the amount, similar to a credit card POS. ‍ - An Android SDK can be developed to enable any Android device (including Saga) to become a Solana Pay POS or can be integrated into existing payment aggregator apps. - Accept Credit Cards and other Fiat payments methods as well for a complete solution to merchants (Can integrate existing FinTech APIs like Stripe) - Off-ramping of USDC to Bank Account (Can use existing APIs developed by Wyre, Circle etc)

author: Solana Mobile

name: Bribe Aggregator
description:
Marinade Finance and Solblaze enable holders of their LSTs (mSOL & bSOL) and governance token (MNDE & soon BLZE) to direct stake to validators. Validators are incentivized to bribe token holders to direct stake to them so they can earn more staking yield.

However, there is no central location where validators can broadcast their bribes and token holders can accept them. The result is an opaque, fragmented market. 

A bribe aggregator would make coordinating activity between bribers and voters much more convenient and lead to greater market efficiency.
possible_solution:
@VotiumProtocol on Ethereum aggregates bribes for veCRV votes and automatically delegates token holders' voting power so as to maximize the incentives received.

Creating an equivalent for LST protocols on Solana would enable users to simply deposit governance tokens, have their votes optimally delegated, and see the APR they get for it. Validators could see the current bribe landscape and easily determine whether bribing will on net increase their validator rewards.
resources: \- Votium: <https://docs.votium.app/>
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Marinade Finance and Solblaze enable holders of their LSTs (mSOL & bSOL) and governance token (MNDE & soon BLZE) to direct stake to validators. Validators are incentivized to bribe token holders to direct stake to them so they can earn more staking yield. However, there is no central location where validators can broadcast their bribes and token holders can accept them. The result is an opaque, fragmented market. A bribe aggregator would make coordinating activity between bribers and voters much more convenient and lead to greater market efficiency.

Possible Solution:

@VotiumProtocol on Ethereum aggregates bribes for veCRV votes and automatically delegates token holders' voting power so as to maximize the incentives received. Creating an equivalent for LST protocols on Solana would enable users to simply deposit governance tokens, have their votes optimally delegated, and see the APR they get for it. Validators could see the current bribe landscape and easily determine whether bribing will on net increase their validator rewards.

- Votium: https://docs.votium.app/

name: NioVerify: Multi-Chain Scam & Rug Pull Detection Platform
description:
Millions of crypto users across multiple blockchains are exposed daily to scams, rug pulls, and phishing attacks, resulting in over $2 billion in annual losses globally. Existing solutions are fragmented, chain-specific, and often inaccessible to everyday users. Most new participants lack the technical knowledge to assess token contract risks, verify project legitimacy, or detect malicious phishing sites before engaging.
**NioVerify solves this problem by:**
1. Providing a **multi-chain scam, rug pull, and phishing detection platform** where users can input any token address or website URL across major blockchains (Ethereum, Solana, BNB Chain, Polygon, and more) and receive an instant, clear risk assessment.
2. Combining **automated security checks** (contract code analysis, liquidity lock status, top holder distribution, blacklist integration) with **community reporting and verification bounties**, incentivising rapid identification of scams.
3. Offering **real-time alerts via Telegram and Discord bots** to keep users, traders, and communities safe proactively rather than reactively.
4. Integrating **educational modules** to teach users how to spot scams, rug pulls, and phishing threats themselves, improving Web3 literacy and reducing vulnerability long-term.
The problem NioVerify addresses is the **lack of a simple, accessible, multi-chain security platform that empowers users to “Know Before They Trust” in Web3**.
possible_solution:
**NioVerify proposes a multi-chain scam, rug pull, and phishing detection platform** that empowers users to verify the safety of any token, contract, or website before interacting.
Our solution includes:
- **One-click verification tool** for tokens and URLs across Ethereum, BNB Chain, Solana, Polygon, and other blockchains.
- **Automated risk assessments** combining on-chain security APIs, contract code analysis, liquidity lock checks, and blacklist databases to produce clear “Safe, Medium, High Risk, Scam” results for users.
- **Community reporting and bounty rewards**, allowing users to submit suspicious tokens or sites and earn verification rewards, creating a decentralised threat intelligence network.
- **Real-time alerts via Telegram and Discord bots**, so users, traders, and communities are notified instantly of verified scams and rug pulls.
- **Educational modules and guides** to teach users how to spot scams, phishing attempts, and fraudulent projects themselves.
By integrating automated detection, community verification, and Web3 education in a single accessible platform, **NioVerify enables crypto users to “Know Before They Trust” across all chains**, enhancing security and confidence in the Web3 ecosystem.
resources:

name: Market-Making Scripts for OpenBook
description:
Market Making is critical for infusing liquidity in orderbooks, increasing efficiency for any exchange. After the demise of FTX, OpenBook (Fork of Project Serum) has been facing a liquidity crunch due to a lack of market makers. Alameda was one of the most active market makers.
Without active market making, spreads won’t be tight, leading to inefficient prices for all market participants.
possible_solution:
Provide liquidity on OpenBook by placing bids/asks on opposite sides of the book and earning a spread.
The SDKs can be utilized to pay a 2 bps fee for taker orders and pay 0 bps to make, i.e., market-making executions will be cost-free.
A few features of the bot can be:
1\. It should be easily configurable by anyone, plug and play.
2\. It should place both ask and bid orders around some mid-price.
3\. The spread should be configurable.
4\. It should automatically rebalance and be able to maintain 24/7 liquidity
resources:
\- OpenBook Docs - <https://github.com/openbook-dex/openbook-ts>
\- Market Making Bot by Mango Markets - <https://docs.mango.markets/archived-v3/market-making-bot-python> (archived)

name: Tokenize personal carbon credits
description: Currently, carbon credit trading is primarily targeted at organizations and governments. This often leaves individual efforts unrecognized. Individuals lack an easy way to quantify and verify their carbon reduction efforts. Traditional carbon credit markets can be non-transparent and slow. This leads to difficulties in tracking the lifecycle of credits, high costs, and sometimes even fraudulent activities.
possible_solution:
Users can tokenize their carbon reduction efforts into fungible tokens. These tokens can represent a specific amount of carbon reduction, such as one ton of CO2 equivalent.
A DEX can be built specifically for trading personal carbon credits or the existing DEXes can also be used.
The critical part of the solution is verifying the efforts. There can be various ways to create a robust verification mechanism.
\- Peer Review System: Individuals and entities can submit their carbon reduction efforts for review by community members who have been recognized as trusted validators. Validators can be incentivized through rewards, reputation points, or other mechanisms
\- Community driven approach where multiple participants actively engage in the verification process
\- Third party solutions for verification
resources:
\- Personal Carbon Trading: <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Personal_carbon_trading>
\- What can Blockchain do for Carbon Markets: <https://rmi.org/what-can-blockchain-do-for-carbon-markets/>
\- A Blockchain-based Carbon Credit Ecosystem: <https://arxiv.org/ftp/arxiv/papers/2107/2107.00185.pdf>
\- Tokenization of carbon credits - an explainer: <https://blog.toucan.earth/tokenization-of-carbon-credits-explained/>
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Currently, carbon credit trading is primarily targeted at organizations and governments. This often leaves individual efforts unrecognized. Individuals lack an easy way to quantify and verify their carbon reduction efforts. Traditional carbon credit markets can be non-transparent and slow. This leads to difficulties in tracking the lifecycle of credits, high costs, and sometimes even fraudulent activities.

Possible Solution:

Users can tokenize their carbon reduction efforts into fungible tokens. These tokens can represent a specific amount of carbon reduction, such as one ton of CO2 equivalent. A DEX can be built specifically for trading personal carbon credits or the existing DEXes can also be used. The critical part of the solution is verifying the efforts. There can be various ways to create a robust verification mechanism. - Peer Review System: Individuals and entities can submit their carbon reduction efforts for review by community members who have been recognized as trusted validators. Validators can be incentivized through rewards, reputation points, or other mechanisms - Community driven approach where multiple participants actively engage in the verification process - Third party solutions for verification

- Personal Carbon Trading: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Personal_carbon_trading - What can Blockchain do for Carbon Markets: https://rmi.org/what-can-blockchain-do-for-carbon-markets/ - A Blockchain-based Carbon Credit Ecosystem: https://arxiv.org/ftp/arxiv/papers/2107/2107.00185.pdf - Tokenization of carbon credits - an explainer: https://blog.toucan.earth/tokenization-of-carbon-credits-explained/

name: AMM protocol on top of Phoenix
description: Phoenix stands as the latest and foremost on-chain order book on Solana, having already facilitated over $250 million in trading volume. Notably, it holds the distinction of being the pioneer crankless limit order book DEX. Nonetheless, like any order book, liquidity poses a significant challenge, as it hinges on active market making.
possible_solution: Propose an AMM protocol atop Phoenix, essentially establishing a market for long-tail tokens and enhancing depth for blue-chip tokens such as USDC and SOL. This AMM would enable effortless participation in Liquidity Provision (LP'ing) for anyone, thereby amplifying the initial liquidity required for these tokens.

name: Web3 GoFundMe - Transparent Donation Matching
description: Organizing donation matching campaigns can be a complex and time-consuming process, especially when reconciling matches with original donations. There is a need for a transparent, easy-to-use platform that handles donation matching automatically, allowing donors to maximize their impact without the hassle of manual reconciliation. Existing fundraising platforms do not offer a seamless, crypto-powered solution that simplifies the matching process.
possible_solution:
A Web3 version of GoFundMe is a donation matching platform that uses crypto behind the curtain. The platform is designed to be user-friendly, making it easy for matcher donors to delegate their matching amounts and for donors to see the matched amounts in the same transaction. With the help of a progress bar, the platform displays the status of all matches, providing a clear visualization of the donation campaign's progress.
The platform focuses on ease of use, allowing for credit card (CC) donations using USDC rails while minimizing the exposure of other crypto-related elements. The end addresses can utilize services like Change for a seamless donation experience. Web3 GoFundMe simplifies the matching process, making it more accessible to a wider audience.
resources:
\- Stockpile (crowdfunding platform on Solana) by 0xSavant - <https://www.stockpile.pro/>
\- An example fundraising site to help you begin can be found at - <https://github.com/AnishDe12020/sponsor>
\- CandyPay Documentation - <https://docs.candypay.fun/>

name: Product Hunt for Solana
description: There are a lot of new products and it's hard to keep up with them all.
possible_solution:
One single place to submit your new product.
1\. Verified users can vote on the products that they like, with limited # of votes per day. 
2\. Charge a small fee for new products to list themselves (like, very small) and a larger fee for existing projects to list themselves (after you get page views). 
3\. Give points and reward active users via liquidity that is contributed to via fees, advertising, and sponsorships. 
4\. Create a reputation system for users with good taste (e.g., certain milestones like raising funding, winning hackathons, or making a lot of revenue can be used to "boost" products). 


Notes:
1\. Tie it to social media accounts to reduce sybil voting.
2\. There are some interesting ideas about limited DM access to product submitters that are exclusive to high rep users. VCs can pay to buy DMs. Products are incentivized to prioritize platform DMs because there is automatic filtering. 
3\. There are a lot of interesting perk ideas for participants (beyond just token). Early beta access, priority options for capital allocation if the project raises, etc.
4\. Just keep it really really simple. Do the product discovery thing really well, and then figure out monetization and extra features later.
resources:
\- This idea was originally shared on Colosseum. Find more inspiration, team mates, resources here.
\- Code Wallet Documentation

Problem to Solve:

There are a lot of new products and it's hard to keep up with them all.

One single place to submit your new product. 1. Verified users can vote on the products that they like, with limited # of votes per day. 2. Charge a small fee for new products to list themselves (like, very small) and a larger fee for existing projects to list themselves (after you get page views). 3. Give points and reward active users via liquidity that is contributed to via fees, advertising, and sponsorships. 4. Create a reputation system for users with good taste (e.g., certain milestones like raising funding, winning hackathons, or making a lot of revenue can be used to "boost" products). Notes: 1. Tie it to social media accounts to reduce sybil voting. 2. There are some interesting ideas about limited DM access to product submitters that are exclusive to high rep users. VCs can pay to buy DMs. Products are incentivized to prioritize platform DMs because there is automatic filtering. 3. There are a lot of interesting perk ideas for participants (beyond just token). Early beta access, priority options for capital allocation if the project raises, etc. 4. Just keep it really really simple. Do the product discovery thing really well, and then figure out monetization and extra features later.

name: Intent-based cross-chain DEXs
description:
Enable users to bridge native assets from Chain A to Chain B using Intents.

Popularised by UniswapX, Intents are the hottest topic in DEXs, where one can express intent like "swap X on Chain A for Y on Chain B”

The current bridging does not have the best UX, as users need to Wrap, bridge, wait for a few minutes or hours, unwrap, and claim assets on destination chains. This also leads to gas inefficiency on expensive chains.
possible_solution: Using 'Intents,' the cross-chain swaps can be executed by anyone, like DEXs or off-chain solvers (think market makers) on connected chains. The messages between chains are passed using Wormhole.
resources:
1\. Mayan Finance / Hashflow
2\. Superteam Blog
3\. Wormhole Docs

name: Search Funds
description:
There's an entire generation of highly-capable, book-smart MBAs that are so desperate for a taste of sovereignty, that they raise capital to buy & operate a small- or medium-sized business

They call these "search funds". During the last crypto bull run in '20-'21, 125 search funds raised $775m. The median search fund acquired business operates b2b services and sells for 7x EBITDA.
possible_solution:
On the other hand, DePIN has created a new path to entrepreneurship via what we call "open franchises". 
Open Franchises are protocols that create blueprints for scaling local network-based businesses; and then partner with local entrepreneurs to build the actual business. 

Instead of buying shitty businesses from a retiree for the chance to make $1m, these searchooors should be building DePIN franchises where they can make $10m or even $100m+ on a long time horizon if they are highly competent operators.

The main challenge is that MBAs' interest in crypto has historically been highly cyclical (they all leave in bear markets..) and even the persistent ones need lots of help to speed up the process of becoming crypto-native

name: Reputation-based Slashing
description: In online marketplaces, dealing with unreliable sellers can be a poor experience for buyers.
possible_solution:
A reputation-based seller blacklisting mechanism based on several factors such as delivery issues, product misrepresentation, poor customer service, etc.

Sellers who engage in problematic behavior consistently, on the basis of a series of negative feedback or complaints from multiple buyers are flagged as repeat offenders. 

These repeat offenders are then added to a blacklist that is accessible across the network, which serves as a reference for the platform to take appropriate actions against these sellers - such as limiting their visibility.
resources:
1\. Learn About ONDC
2\. Unpacking the $80+ billion ONDC opportunity: By Antler

name: Webhook service for Solana Pay
description: Current web3 payment services use "frontend polling" which is an inefficient method of fetching from the API by repeatedly calling the endpoint to check for the availability of transaction data on the blockchain. possible_solution:
Create an event-based, webhook architecture for Solana Pay [they're currently using a frontend polling mechanism].
An efficient way to build this would be to adapt a Helius webhook, that can facilitate monitoring over 100,000 addresses and is easier to integrate into existing payment solutions.

possible_solution: There is potential for a web3 platform, similar to a clubhouse or twitter space, that rewards listeners for their engagement and participation. This could include rewards for listening streaks, sharing content with friends, or completing gamified challenges. These incentives could not only increase user engagement, but also help the platform grow. Users who reach certain thresholds could unlock special perks, and creators have the opportunity to interact with and directly reward their fans. name: AMMs for Sports Betting Platforms
description: Central limit order books are a great platform for high liquidity binary outcome wagers, yet they are limited in their ability to provide accumulator or parlay bets - an integral part of any sports betting platform.
possible_solution:
An automated market maker protocol which allows for long-tail markets and parlays, all while maintaining high liquidity to ensure users can get their bet matched timely, and the pricing reflects the true odds of the outcome. This could be used in a variety of manners including: 

Bringing accumulators/parlays to on-chain betting exchanges has long been considered the holy grail of web3 betting platforms. The margin on a parlay/accumulator can get up to 40%, the protocol which solves this problem could take in significant revenue.  (Note - exposure could be hedged by individual underlying markets on Monaco)

AMM’s could also be used to augment liquidity on regular markets. For example, if an AMM was built on top of a Monaco match winner CLOB market for Liverpool vs Real Madrid, users could theoretically have a much deeper and more efficient market to trade on. 

What does the smart contract and economic incentive design look like for these type of solutions?
resources: \- Raydium has built an AMM on top of Serum/Openbook for crypto token markets, something similar could potentially be built for sports betting - <https://docs.raydium.io>

name: Mercor for Web3 Talent on Solana
description:
The inflow of developer and builder talent into Solana is at its highest ever, thanks to programs like **100x, Turbine, Blueshift**, and various accelerators that are onboarding new contributors. However, there are **three major gaps** in the ecosystem today:
1. **No standardized ranking of talent** → Developers graduate from different schools and programs, but companies have no unified way to assess their skill levels. This leads to inconsistent quality checks across the ecosystem.
2. **Inefficient hiring** → Companies and protocols need to conduct multiple interviews, coding challenges, and repeated tests to filter talent. This slows down hiring and creates friction for both sides.
3. **Fragmentation across platforms** → While Solana has job boards and Superteam has its own Talent Engine, these tools work in isolation. There is no **single layer of verified talent ranking** that these boards and hiring platforms can plug into.
As a result, the demand for Solana talent is not being efficiently matched with the supply, leading to missed opportunities and slower project growth.
possible_solution:
A **"Mercor for Web3"** — a **talent ranking and validation layer** built specifically for Solana.https://mercor.com/
- **Unified ranking system** → Talent from schools (100x, Turbine, Blueshift, etc.) can be benchmarked against one another in a standardized, verifiable way.
- **API + frontend** → Instead of being another siloed job board, this ranking system can integrate as an **API layer** for existing Solana job boards, Superteam Talent Engine, DAOs, and launchpads. At the same time, we’ll offer our own **frontend marketplace** where companies can discover, filter, and hire top-ranked developers quickly.
- **Client-first model** → Every hiring platform, job board, or studio can become our client, while developers get one single profile that travels with them across the ecosystem.
resources: https://mercor.com/
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

The inflow of developer and builder talent into Solana is at its highest ever, thanks to programs like 100x, Turbine, Blueshift, and various accelerators that are onboarding new contributors. However, there are three major gaps in the ecosystem today: 1. No standardized ranking of talent → Developers graduate from different schools and programs, but companies have no unified way to assess their skill levels. This leads to inconsistent quality checks across the ecosystem. 2. Inefficient hiring → Companies and protocols need to conduct multiple interviews, coding challenges, and repeated tests to filter talent. This slows down hiring and creates friction for both sides. 3. Fragmentation across platforms → While Solana has job boards and Superteam has its own Talent Engine, these tools work in isolation. There is no single layer of verified talent ranking that these boards and hiring platforms can plug into. As a result, the demand for Solana talent is not being efficiently matched with the supply, leading to missed opportunities and slower project growth.

Possible Solution:

A "Mercor for Web3" — a talent ranking and validation layer built specifically for Solana.https://mercor.com/ - Unified ranking system → Talent from schools (100x, Turbine, Blueshift, etc.) can be benchmarked against one another in a standardized, verifiable way. - API + frontend → Instead of being another siloed job board, this ranking system can integrate as an API layer for existing Solana job boards, Superteam Talent Engine, DAOs, and launchpads. At the same time, we’ll offer our own frontend marketplace where companies can discover, filter, and hire top-ranked developers quickly. - Client-first model → Every hiring platform, job board, or studio can become our client, while developers get one single profile that travels with them across the ecosystem.

name: Social Media Aggregator
description:
With the rise of social media networks and protocols on Solana like Spling, Gum, Dispatch, and subsequent front-ends getting built on them - there is a high chance of fragmented social media content, making it difficult for the user to navigate and keep up with the latest content. Fragmentation can also lead to poor network effects for the platforms as users will get divided between these protocols, ultimately leading to the death of many dApps.
A similar problem existed with Decentralised Exchanges, where liquidity for swapping assets was fragmented between different projects like OpenBook, Raydium, Orca, and so on - jup.ag solved it by aggregating all of them. This gave users a unified UX while helping them with the best routes and lowest fees.
possible_solution:
Think of this as Jupiter Aggregator for social media content instead of asset swaps. The content of all these protocols will live on the Solana blockchain, so users should be able to link all their social frontends and get a unified feed to interact with those posts. Composability will power collaboration over competition.
The best UX aggregator app will ultimately win the user’s mobile screen time and hence, there are higher chances of monetization over the long term. The algorithms of the social media feeds can also be a strong differentiator. For sample frontends building on such protocols, check out Wordcel and Solarplex.
resources:
\- Gum's open source SDK - <https://github.com/gumhq>
\- Gum's documentation - <https://docs.gum.fun/>

3\. destake from an NFT validator if their floor price falls below x SOL or if they've burned more than 50% of their supply bridging to another chain

name: Enabling Merchants to use Solana Pay
description: When merchants receive payments from customers, they pay a transaction fee to the involved intermediaries like banks or Visa and Mastercard. However, the processing fees paid by merchants is around 1.3% to 3.5% of each credit card transaction depending on the payment network. If both merchants and the customer are involved in the transaction process, this fee can be reduced.
possible_solution: Create an easy onboarding for merchants that want to accept Solana Pay. Features may include but are not limited to - Custodial / non-custodial wallets made for Merchants (dedicated wallets for merchants which has customizable interfaces based on the size, category & need of each merchant)‍ and tools/Services helping devs to integrate the SDK with the current products being used by the merchant like existing POS solutions and integration into inventory and accounting systems, etc.
resources:
\- Solana Pay SDK - <https://github.com/solana-labs/solana-pay>
\- Helius Docs - <https://docs.helius.xyz/>

author: Jon Wong

name: Security Analysis Tool
description:
An average on-chain user who deals with crypto tokens and NFTs on a day-to-day basis can easily forget or neglect the importance of checking the behavior of wallets they interact with, their delegated NFTs, etc. 
As web3 gets to the masses, we need a security analysis tool that keeps checking these parameters under the hood every time a transaction or an activity is made bya user, and alert them about a possible suspicious engagement they have made, thus allowing them to safeguard their digital assets, before a malicious actor takes over.

Some of the recent hacks on Solana were merely due to the wallet interactions prove the need for security analysis tools like these in current situations.
possible_solution:
A security analysis tool that
1\. checks a hot wallet, creates a graph of associated wallets, and you mark which ones are supposed to be “cold”. sends an alert if there’s any violation of certain parameters (e.g., cold wallet interacting with programs that are not the hot wallet)
2\. making sure that delegation for NFTs is locked down and those NFTs are frozen if possible to avoid drains
3\. when creating a pseudonymous wallet with a different seed phrase, send an alert if you’ve “compromised” your identity by sending transactions across seed phrases
resources:
\- Helius SDK - <https://helius.xyz/>
\- Explore on-chain indexed data - <https://solana.fm/>
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

An average on-chain user who deals with crypto tokens and NFTs on a day-to-day basis can easily forget or neglect the importance of checking the behavior of wallets they interact with, their delegated NFTs, etc. As web3 gets to the masses, we need a security analysis tool that keeps checking these parameters under the hood every time a transaction or an activity is made bya user, and alert them about a possible suspicious engagement they have made, thus allowing them to safeguard their digital assets, before a malicious actor takes over. Some of the recent hacks on Solana were merely due to the wallet interactions prove the need for security analysis tools like these in current situations.

Possible Solution:

A security analysis tool that 1. checks a hot wallet, creates a graph of associated wallets, and you mark which ones are supposed to be “cold”. sends an alert if there’s any violation of certain parameters (e.g., cold wallet interacting with programs that are not the hot wallet) 2. making sure that delegation for NFTs is locked down and those NFTs are frozen if possible to avoid drains 3. when creating a pseudonymous wallet with a different seed phrase, send an alert if you’ve “compromised” your identity by sending transactions across seed phrases

- Helius SDK - https://helius.xyz/ - Explore on-chain indexed data - https://solana.fm/

name: ARChain: Decentralized Augmented Reality Experience Network on Solana
description: Today’s AR experiences are siloed in closed‑garden apps (Niantic, Snap, Meta), limiting discoverability, interoperability, and ownership of location‑based digital content. Creators can’t port AR assets between platforms, audiences can’t verify provenance, and community‑driven city‑wide or global AR layers never persist when an app disappears. As a result, AR remains a walled‑garden novelty rather than a persistent, community‑owned medium.
possible_solution:
Build **ARChain**, an on‑chain layer for publishing, discovering, and monetizing geolocated AR content—fully decentralized, permissionless, and interoperable:
1. **Geo‑Anchored AR NFTs (xNFTs)**
    - Artists mint AR assets as compressed NFTs with embedded 3D models, audio clips, or interactive scripts.
    - Each xNFT includes geo‑coordinates, orientation, scale, and optional time‑windows.
2. **Location Index & Discovery**
    - A Solana program maintains a spatial index of AR xNFTs.
    - Mobile/Web3 apps query this index to render AR overlays as users move.
3. **Creator & Viewer Tokens**
    - $ARX rewards creators for community ratings, view counts, and on‑chain “checks‑in.”
    - Viewers earn $ARX for first‑time scans, sharing experiences, or reporting asset health.
4. **Marketplace & Royalties**
    - Integrates with Metaplex for primary sales and built‑in royalty enforcement on secondary trades.
    - Gate certain AR layers behind staking (e.g., unlock a hidden AR scavenger hunt).
5. **Open SDK & Wallet Integration**
    - Provide an open‑source SDK (Anchor + WebGPU) for building ARChain‑compatible apps on iOS/Android/Saga.
    - Out‑of‑the‑box support for Phantom Mobile’s AR view component.
6. **Governance & Community Curation**
    - A DAO treasury funds public‑good AR projects (historical tours, art installations).
    - Token‑weighted votes decide featured AR trails and curation guidelines.
resources:
- Solana xNFT Standard: <https://github.com/metaplex-foundation/metaplex-program-library/tree/master/xnft>
- Anchor Framework: <https://www.anchor-lang.com/>
- Mapbox AR SDK (for geolocation): https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/overview/
- Three.js / WebGPU for rendering: <https://threejs.org/>
- Metaplex Candy Machine & Auction House: https://docs.metaplex.com/
- Solana Mobile Stack & AR reference: https://solana.com/mobile
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Today’s AR experiences are siloed in closed‑garden apps (Niantic, Snap, Meta), limiting discoverability, interoperability, and ownership of location‑based digital content. Creators can’t port AR assets between platforms, audiences can’t verify provenance, and community‑driven city‑wide or global AR layers never persist when an app disappears. As a result, AR remains a walled‑garden novelty rather than a persistent, community‑owned medium.

Possible Solution:

Build ARChain, an on‑chain layer for publishing, discovering, and monetizing geolocated AR content—fully decentralized, permissionless, and interoperable: 1. Geo‑Anchored AR NFTs (xNFTs) - Artists mint AR assets as compressed NFTs with embedded 3D models, audio clips, or interactive scripts. - Each xNFT includes geo‑coordinates, orientation, scale, and optional time‑windows. 1. Location Index & Discovery - A Solana program maintains a spatial index of AR xNFTs. - Mobile/Web3 apps query this index to render AR overlays as users move. 1. Creator & Viewer Tokens - $ARX rewards creators for community ratings, view counts, and on‑chain “checks‑in.” - Viewers earn $ARX for first‑time scans, sharing experiences, or reporting asset health. 1. Marketplace & Royalties - Integrates with Metaplex for primary sales and built‑in royalty enforcement on secondary trades. - Gate certain AR layers behind staking (e.g., unlock a hidden AR scavenger hunt). 1. Open SDK & Wallet Integration - Provide an open‑source SDK (Anchor + WebGPU) for building ARChain‑compatible apps on iOS/Android/Saga. - Out‑of‑the‑box support for Phantom Mobile’s AR view component. 1. Governance & Community Curation - A DAO treasury funds public‑good AR projects (historical tours, art installations). - Token‑weighted votes decide featured AR trails and curation guidelines.

name: Recruitment with Skin in the Game
description: Traditional hiring methods are resource intensive.
possible_solution:
Create a 2-sided platform with a $RECRUIT token. To participate both recruiters and clients have to acquire some amount of $RECRUIT. When signing a client, both recruiter and client contribute X tokens to a pot.
Hire candidates → work out → tokens migrate from client to recruiter. If don’t work out tokens migrate from recruiter to client.
Bonus:
1\. Can agree if within timeframe Y no hires, then tokens also move from recruiter to client, since time has been lost.
2\. If you can find a way to measure the client being “difficult” (aka rejecting all hires), then potentially could move tokens from client to recruiter.
End result: better aligned incentives. Recruiter is incentivized to choose fewer clients and work harder for them (what the client wants). Clients are incentivized to choose fewer recruiters or they will get slashed if they reject all hires.
resources: 1\. Full Essay with GTM and Biz Model Ideas

If don’t work out tokens migrate from recruiter to client. Bonus: 1. Can agree if within timeframe Y no hires, then tokens also move from recruiter to client, since time has been lost. 2. If you can find a way to measure the client being “difficult” (aka rejecting all hires), then potentially could move tokens from client to recruiter. End result: better aligned incentives. Recruiter is incentivized to choose fewer clients and work harder for them (what the client wants). Clients are incentivized to choose fewer recruiters or they will get slashed if they reject all hires.

name: Fiat-backed Stablecoins
description: Stablecoins are inherently centralized products that need higher control while retaining composability throughout the ecosystem. Token Extensions enable exactly that.
possible_solution:
A few features, which can be enabled are:
1\. Using Permanent delegate token extensions, projects can clawback or revoke Tokens, when required: Teams, such as those behind stablecoins or CBDCs, could appoint permanent delegates with the power to freeze accounts or remove tokens in cases of fraud or criminal activity. It can also be used for revocable membership. The Solana token program has always had "freeze", this extension now enables "seize" as well.
2\. Using transfer hooks, stablecoins could enforce full KYC for all users or those holding amounts above a certain threshold. The RWA token could be made transferable only after certain conditions are met, such as completing KYC, ensuring non-violation of the OFAC list, or agreeing to Terms of Service.


Two stablecoin Issuers are already using TEs like GMO to utilize transfer hooks, Metadata pointers, and permanent delegate authority extensions. Paxos with these token extensions can transfer or remove tokens from the accounts of USDP token holders. The ‘Permanent delegate’ will be used by all NYDFS-based stablecoin issuers (potentially PayPal’s PYUSD too) due to regulatory requirements.
resources:
1\. A Primer on Solana's Token Extensions: The Superteam Blog
2\. Token Extensions Docs
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Stablecoins are inherently centralized products that need higher control while retaining composability throughout the ecosystem. Token Extensions enable exactly that.

Possible Solution:

A few features, which can be enabled are: 1. Using Permanent delegate token extensions, projects can clawback or revoke Tokens, when required: Teams, such as those behind stablecoins or CBDCs, could appoint permanent delegates with the power to freeze accounts or remove tokens in cases of fraud or criminal activity. It can also be used for revocable membership. The Solana token program has always had "freeze", this extension now enables "seize" as well. 2. Using transfer hooks, stablecoins could enforce full KYC for all users or those holding amounts above a certain threshold. The RWA token could be made transferable only after certain conditions are met, such as completing KYC, ensuring non-violation of the OFAC list, or agreeing to Terms of Service. Two stablecoin Issuers are already using TEs like GMO to utilize transfer hooks, Metadata pointers, and permanent delegate authority extensions. Paxos with these token extensions can transfer or remove tokens from the accounts of USDP token holders. The ‘Permanent delegate’ will be used by all NYDFS-based stablecoin issuers (potentially PayPal’s PYUSD too) due to regulatory requirements. 

1. A Primer on Solana's Token Extensions: The Superteam Blog 2. Token Extensions Docs

name: Sports betting market making tools
description: Market makers add value by injecting liquidity into a market - they are essential to any healthy exchange. However, many of the tools today available to market makers are burdensome, manual, and often time consuming.
possible_solution: A market making tool that allows users to inject liquidity in a programmatic manner onto a market. These tools would allow a market marker to set unique, personalised parameters such as depth, volume, etc. They should also be able to adapt instantly to new market information and overall price movements. A tool this powerful would allow market makers to participate in more markets, making their capital more efficient, and by extension, the market more efficient for end users.
resources:
\- A basic market seeder tool has been outsourced in the Monaco github: <https://github.com/MonacoProtocol/market-seeder>

\- Bet Angel is a common tool used in the sports betting industry to trade programmatically on Betfair: <https://www.betangel.com/>

name: LivePhrass Wallet On Solana
description:
Crypto is supposed to give freedom. But today, losing your wallet passphrase means losing everything money, identity, access. Scams are rampant. Seed phrase theft is a daily nightmare. Crypto is still stuck in 2014 security.
-$1.7 Billion lost to crypto hacks & scams in 2023 alone.
\- 40% of users avoid crypto because they fear losing access (source: Coinbase study).
No recovery. No second chance. No security net.
possible_solution:
**LivePhrass Wallet Solution**
 A secure crypto wallet that uses Face ID, fingerprint, and identity verification  all encrypted and stored in the cloud. Once registered, users can access their wallet from any device.
If an account is lost or access is compromised, the user can recover it using Face ID, liveness check, and fingerprint scan.
To enhance security, we require email verification before granting access. Even if someone has the passphrase, they must still pass both email verification and LivePhrass biometric checks.
Users can also set a custom password and email backup. In cases where the user can no longer access the account, a trusted loved one can recover it using a predefined secret word that both parties know.
Users can choose a timeline (e.g., 1 to 20 years), after which an automated message or email will be sent to their designated loved one. This will include a set of verification questions. Once answered correctly, a one-time password is issued to access and transfer the user’s funds securely.
resources:
LivePhrass is the world’s first biometric-powered wallet security and recovery layer for Web3.
Combining Face ID, fingerprint, and liveness checks, LivePhrass makes wallet access and recovery as simple and safe as your iPhone.
- Lose your seed phrase? No problem recover with face + fingerprint + secret word.
- Scam detected? We lock the wallet, verify you via LivePhrass within 24hrs.
- New device? Just re-authenticate with biometrics, no stress.

Key Features:
-User Choice: Passphrase / LivePhrass / Both.
-Scam Activity Lock: 24-hour verification shield.
-Universal Device Access: Login from any device.
-Email + Password Recovery: Traditional fallback.
-Next-of-Kin Access: Secret word for loved ones.

How LivePhrass will Stays Secure
- Encrypted Biometric Templates, No raw images stored.
- Cloud Vault Storage, Biometric data encrypted & stored securely.
- AI + Human Verification, Scans user biometrics during scam checks.
- Universal Access , Biometric data lets users log in from any device.
- 24-Hour Scam Shield, Suspicious activity? Lock triggers real-user verification before
restoring access.

Security Architecture
- Privacy-Safe Biometrics (Face ID, fingerprint, liveness checks).
- AI-Driven Scam Detection engine monitors access patterns.
- Multi-Factor Access System: Biometrics + Email + Secret Word.
- Zero-Knowledge Storage: Data encrypted and privacy-preserving.

It’s Web3 security with Web2 smoothness.
It’s privacy-respecting, encrypted, and scalable for mass adoption.
