name: P2P Lending with Customised Interest Rate
description:
In DeFi lending markets, we get used to with a pool system where it's the one establishing the APR/ APY for both lenders and borrowers

This creates issues when there is a significant gap between the APR/APY set for lending and borrowing. This issue results in lenders earning less while borrowers are burdened with higher costs. Consequently, this leads to a significant drop in capital efficiency within lending markets.
possible_solution:
A peer to peer lending platform that allows users to customise interest rate

Lenders can create offers with customised APR. Meanwhile, borrowers have the option to choose from various offers, ultimately selecting the one with the lowest APR as determined by the lender.
resources: This idea was originally shared on Colosseum. Find more inspiration, team mates, resources here.

Possible Solution:

name: Phygital product with TipLinks as NFCs or QR codes
description: Events and NFTs are a perfect match; however, the current NFT experience falls short of expectations. Existing NFT minting solutions are excessively complex and cost-prohibitive, rendering them inaccessible to retail users interested in NFTs. The emergence of compressed NFTs on Solana addresses the cost concern by enabling the creation of 1 million NFTs for just 5 SOL.
possible_solution:
The current events experience can be transitioned to the blockchain through an NFT-based whitelist system. The experience can be either QR-code based or NFC-based bracelets. The process would operate as follows:

\- Users claim an NFT via TipLink with a single click.
\- This NFT becomes associated with their NFC tag-based bracelets.
\- The NFC tag detector validates the on-chain NFT and grants entry based on the whitelist.

Additionally, users should be able to claim NFTs seamlessly, utilizing a single click, and without requiring a wallet (made possible through TipLink). This streamlined process aims to provide users with an experience reminiscent of interacting with conventional web2 applications.

name: An Interactive NFT State Compression Visualizer
description: As the popularity of NFTs grows, so does the need for efficient ways to manage and verify their underlying data. Developers and users alike require tools to better understand the structure and relationships of hash information, and to generate proofs in real-time. Current visualizers lack the ability to display this crucial information, making it harder for users to grasp the intricacies of their NFTs.
possible_solution:
NFTree is an interactive NFT state compression visualizer that presents hash information as nodes in a tree-like structure. Users can provide their tree data, which the platform will use to map out the entire structure, complete with roots and canopy. Through a user-friendly interface, NFTree connects to the RPC, allowing for live proof generation and verification. To increase accessibility, the platform will be hosted on a domain such as Helius.

Key Features:
\- Interactive tree-like visualization of NFT hash information
\- Real-time proof generation and verification through RPC integration
\- User-friendly interface for easy navigation and understanding of NFT structures
\- Hosting on a reliable domain for easy access and increased visibility
resources: \- Merkle Tree Demo - <https://prathamudeshmukh.github.io/merkle-tree-demo/>

name: The post claim experience for TipLinks
description: Thousands of POAPs, gift cards and product NFTs have been issued across a range of events like Solana Breakpoint, The Network State Conference, Miami Art Basel, IRL Ecosystem Calls, technical workshops, etc.
possible_solution:
Allowing teams to distribute exclusive content & custom products to people who hold these NFTs will incentivise them to build their distribution. For example,
1\. The 5000+ people who claimed a POAP at Breakpoint 2023 could get targeted offers, airdrops and product access from Ecosystem projects who are showing up at the next edition.  
2\. The hundreds of people who attended the TNS Conference can get invited to a special premier of the upcoming movie and early access to start-up cities and related products.  
3\. The 1000+ people who attend the 50+ IRL Ecosystem Call events across the world could get product credits and airdrops from those who are presenting and want to amplify their launch announcements. 
4\. Artists who displayed work at Art Basel could offer special pricing to the 500+ people who showed up and collected their work IRL.  

The insight here is that allowing brands, events, artists, etc to pass on their performance marketing costs as a discount to qualified leads gives them a direct financial incentive to build an audience on Farcaster. 
There is organic product synergy here as well – because while TipLink has built a seamless distribution experience, it is up to each brand or event to build and attract users for their post claim experience.
With Blinks, hosts and sponsors can now direct their audience to their Twitter (soon, any platform of their choice) from their POAP.
resources:
1\. Blinks Docs
2\. List of all Blinks built so far
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Thousands of POAPs, gift cards and product NFTs have been issued across a range of events like Solana Breakpoint, The Network State Conference, Miami Art Basel, IRL Ecosystem Calls, technical workshops, etc. 

Possible Solution:

Allowing teams to distribute exclusive content & custom products to people who hold these NFTs will incentivise them to build their distribution. For example, 1. The 5000+ people who claimed a POAP at Breakpoint 2023 could get targeted offers, airdrops and product access from Ecosystem projects who are showing up at the next edition.   2. The hundreds of people who attended the TNS Conference can get invited to a special premier of the upcoming movie and early access to start-up cities and related products.   3. The 1000+ people who attend the 50+ IRL Ecosystem Call events across the world could get product credits and airdrops from those who are presenting and want to amplify their launch announcements.  4. Artists who displayed work at Art Basel could offer special pricing to the 500+ people who showed up and collected their work IRL.   The insight here is that allowing brands, events, artists, etc to pass on their performance marketing costs as a discount to qualified leads gives them a direct financial incentive to build an audience on Farcaster.  There is organic product synergy here as well – because while TipLink has built a seamless distribution experience, it is up to each brand or event to build and attract users for their post claim experience. With Blinks, hosts and sponsors can now direct their audience to their Twitter (soon, any platform of their choice) from their POAP.

author: Aditya Shetty

name: Decentralized identity (DIDs) on Solana
description:
\- DeFi Protocols, particularly Real World Asset players on Solana like Credix still rely on centralized identity services.
\- The absence of an identity model poses its own problems and stunts the growth of crypto finance: it prevents broader commercial and institutional adoption, blocks key use cases and market opportunities, and risks legal non-compliance.

Addressing these issues requires a new privacy-preserving decentralized identity model. Although there is Civic on Solana, however, it's not institutional-friendly and is more focussed on gating and permissioning for communities, instead of real-world financial use cases.
possible_solution:
An open-source framework for proving identity claims in Web3 without exposing sensitive personal information. Web apps, mobile apps, and smart contracts get the benefit of identity-verified participants from the simple check of the on-chain credentials. The use of ZKPs (Zero Knowledge Proofs) can be explored to verify the talent’s identity - ensuring that neither identity protocol nor the company is ever able to view their personal information

The following can be the potential use cases:
1\.  Institutions can engage with the DeFi ecosystem using identity protocols and meet regulatory requirements
2\. Marketplaces, exchanges, and smart contracts can validate customer identity without storing personal identity data
3\. Lending protocols can use decentralized credit and risk scoring to reduce collateralization requirements. This can unlock much-needed uncollateralized lending on-chain
4\. A payroll and compliance tool that enables Solana-based companies to hire pseudonymous talent, without compromising legal obligations.
resources:
1\. Solana ID
2\. A reference for the architecture - <https://docs.centre.io/verite/overview/architecture-overview>

Problem to Solve:

- DeFi Protocols, particularly Real World Asset players on Solana like Credix still rely on centralized identity services. - The absence of an identity model poses its own problems and stunts the growth of crypto finance: it prevents broader commercial and institutional adoption, blocks key use cases and market opportunities, and risks legal non-compliance. Addressing these issues requires a new privacy-preserving decentralized identity model. Although there is Civic on Solana, however, it's not institutional-friendly and is more focussed on gating and permissioning for communities, instead of real-world financial use cases.

Possible Solution:

Web apps, mobile apps, and smart contracts get the benefit of identity-verified participants from the simple check of the on-chain credentials. The use of ZKPs (Zero Knowledge Proofs) can be explored to verify the talent’s identity - ensuring that neither identity protocol nor the company is ever able to view their personal information The following can be the potential use cases: 1. Institutions can engage with the DeFi ecosystem using identity protocols and meet regulatory requirements 2. Marketplaces, exchanges, and smart contracts can validate customer identity without storing personal identity data 3. name: Community-based Learning Apps
description:
Duo Lingo was able to blitz scale as a free learning app because thousands of volunteers contributed to creating its coursework. 

And while there is a thriving Duo Lingo community on Reddit, Discord, Twitch, etc multiple past attempts at incorporating community into the product have failed.
possible_solution:
This is a problem because language is inherently social. If there’s one thing you want to learn from another human - it’s how to speak with them. 

A crypto-powered learning app would include: 
\- an in-app wallet
\- micropayments via a native token / USDC
\- an incentives mechanism: bounties, quests, rewards for helping other users, etc.
\- simple on and off-ramps

name: Streamflow PDF Generator
description:
Streamflow supports these wallets - Phantom, Sollet, Solflare and Slope. If a recipient is using a different non-custodial wallet or using a centralized exchange wallet address to receive the payments, they can’t see the stream details on request. They can only get the streaming details if the sender has added their email address while setting up the stream. The recipient gets notified when the stream is set-up or the funds are auto-withdrawn to their SPL wallet.

in case the sender forgets to add email, the employee would not receive any notification(s).
possible_solution: An on-request tool that generates a PDF document with all the details about the stream created with Streamflow for both senders & receivers. Senders can create a PDF with stream details to be shared with the recipients as soon as the stream is created. Recipients can also check the stream details as soon as it is created.
resources:

name: Accept any SPL token using Jupiter
description: When a merchant is set up to receive payments, users might possess various SPL tokens like BONK or SOL. However, the merchant's preference is to accept USDC due to their expenses being denominated in USD. This creates the inconvenience of needing to convert these tokens or limiting the range of acceptable payment options.
possible_solution:
Introducing a novel payments service that empowers retailers to accept any SPL token as payment, utilizing Jupiter's pricing and swap APIs. The proposed flow would be as follows:
\- The user initiates payment using any SPL token, such as BONK.
\- Behind the scenes, the SPL token is seamlessly swapped to the merchant's desired token, such as USDC.
\- The merchant receives settlement in USDC, aligning with their preferred currency.

To power these, Jupiter APIs like Pricing APIs and Payment APIs will be critical.
resources: \- Jupiter APIs - <https://station.jup.ag/docs/apis>

name: Compressed NFTs in Gaming
description: Web3 gaming can be a mammoth task, but representing in-game assets as NFTs can become expensive when scaled up. Compressed NFTs on Solana, however, can significantly reduce the costs and enable the minting of 1 million NFTs for just 5 SOL.
possible_solution:
There are two potential ideas in this direction:
\- Develop an open-source smart contract plugin that allows any Solana game to enable NFT characters capable of storing in-game information within the NFT. These contracts should facilitate NFT trading on marketplaces like Magic Eden & Tensor, with all the in-game info stored. This would allow players to buy an NFT, play the game, and then resell the NFT with more in-game achievements already completed.
\- Create a tool that allows gaming projects to incorporate a world-building mechanism into their game. Players can choose to add new buildings to their virtual world, and all these changes are stored within a compressed NFT.
resources:
\- Metaplex Compressed NFTs Documentation - <https://docs.metaplex.com/programs/compression/>
\- Underdog Documentation - <https://underdog.readme.io/reference/getting-started-1>

name: One-Stop-Shop for On and Off-Ramp
description:
On/Off-ramping can still be tedious and expensive to most people. There are still KYCs that take days to complete to transact via a CEX and still risk losing those funds if mistakenly sent to a non-custodial wallet. 

This whole process can, on average, take an hour or more even for a savvy user taking 5-10 steps to do so. It might take days for a normie or my old parent to do that and they can get confused with it. Not to mention the 1-5% fee rates and credit card fees which can be really expensive for users from the third world countries.

Some users might go for cheaper rates from exchanges but opt to bridge to Solana via a chain that is only authorized by the CEX. This subjects the user to bridging risks and extra transaction fees. 

The process of creating your own crypto exchange can also take a long time, require more capital, and pose local legislation hindrance. 

Negative factors to consider:
\- more steps to take 
\- risk of losing funds on the way
\- more expensive fee structures
\- daunting task to create your own exchange
\- UI/UX for using CEX and trading
\- regulatory challenges
\- bridging risks
\- security risks

On/Off-ramping is crucial in onboarding more people into blockchain as serves as an entry point for most of the users.
possible_solution:
Probable solutions and steps:
\- Possibly connect non-custodial crypto wallets like Phantom to directly purchase USDC or SOL from your e-wallet or local bank like just swapping via Jupiter. It should work for debit/credit cards like Decaf.so, Ottr Finance, or Ultimate App is doing, but:
    \- massively takes SHORTER steps
    \- requires LESS INTERACTION with centralized entities
    \- users deposit crypto directly to their non-custodial wallets
    \- available to more countries in the world
    \- easier for remittances from a first-world to a third-world country

This might replace CEX merchants, or possibly attract more of them depending on how this solution is addressed.

\- Solana currently has $600M+ TVL - maybe we can make a protocol that will possibly use this liquidity to market-make for this on/off-ramping problem. This will require different fiat-to-crypto systems to work and multi-national coordination with local banks and e-wallets.

This in a way makes it peer-to-peer or protocol-to-user, which I myself don't completely understand how it will work.

What are we disrupting here?
\- We will be disrupting on/off-ramp players with high fees
\- Local banks that charge a high % when using debit/credit cards for crypto purchases
resources:
\- Innovation in fiat on-ramps can overcome crypto's expensive card fees - <https://cointelegraph.com/news/innovation-in-fiat-on-ramps-can-overcome-crypto-s-expensive-card-fees>

\- Crypto On/Off-Ramp Pros/Cons: <https://prestmit.com/blog/crypto-on-ramp-off-ramp-whats-the-difference/>

\- Best Fiat-to-Crypto On/Off-Ramp Companies: <https://www.cbinsights.com/esp/financial-services/payments-tech/fiat-to-crypto-on%2Foff-ramps>

This whole process can, on average, take an hour or more even for a savvy user taking 5-10 steps to do so. author: Solana Foundation

name: Pump.fun, but for X
description:
Pump isn’t one-size fit all; we need custom market structures for different use cases.

Memecoins (or Attention Assets) are here to stay:

Memecoins on Solana were a glimpse of an incredible phenomenon. Yes, like with every market structure, there were insiders, and many tokens reached highly bloated valuations — pumped by cabals. But we can’t discredit the tech (Solana DEXs) that powered $500B+ in volume in just one month!
Cabals and insiders are clear signs of a highly inefficient market — but the best part is, information travels much faster on the internet and on-chain (especially on Solana). Now’s the time to design and experiment with new market structures — pushing us closer to efficient markets.
possible_solution:
**Experimentations around Vertical Pump** i.e Pump but with either or both curation and mechanism:

— **Niche curations** like music, dating, time, and so on. Specific verticals will allow for better feed curation (FYPs) and hence, better consumer UX. Further, a few more 'AttentionFi' products will emerge tapping existing social graphs. For instance, Kaito is tapping the Twitter social graph — mass popular platforms like TikTok, Discord, and YouTube are still underexplored.

— **Specific Mechanism**: Pump isn’t one-size fit all; we need custom market structures for different use cases. For instance, pre-sale or dutch auction is better suited for project-based token launches. This also involves experimentation around creator-fee sharing, niche AMM design, tokenomics/liquidity locking, pricing curves, and more.
resources: 1. Attention Capital Markets

name: Corporate Bond Market on Solana
description:
In the traditional world, the current process of issuing a corporate bond market is as follows: Planning & Structuring -> Underwriting -> Registration -> Marketing -> Pricing -> Closing -> Secondary Trading.

The notional value of the bond market is $128 Trillion, but the process is slow and inefficient, limiting the accessibility to a large number of retail as well as institutional investors.

Blockchains like Solana enable the automatic creation of efficient markets, solving for liquidity and accessibility.
possible_solution:
There can be a few ways to solve this:
1\. Issuance and tracking of Bond On-chain, Payments Off-chain:
\- No Regulatory Hassle as an only ledger entry.
\- Opportunity to onboard all existing corporate bond issuers to issue SPL bond tokens for greater transparency.
\- The payments are done via Fiat, and no crypto is involved
\- This model is easy to Implement and attract clients -> Later monetize

2\. Both Issuance and Payments On-chain [Tokenisation of existing Bonds] 
\- The Investors can invest via USDC; it can be made permissioned to allow for KYC or accredited investors
\- Utilizing existing protocols/Infra on Solana: Pricing of Bonds via AMMs, Streaming payments to bond investors, Multi-sig  -> Power of Composability
\- Target clients can be: Large companies in tokenization-friendly jurisdictions looking to finance massive projects.
\- This model will take time, but a massive opportunity.

The Platform: Bond as a Service
It should enable the creation of Bond Markets for anyone, and they should be able to configure based on the type of bonds, like vesting schedule, payment terms, and so on. The platform should be able to plug in or simply fork existing projects on Solana to leverage the already built markets Infra.

The marketplace model (Amazon for bonds) can also be adopted, where the issuers can issue and sell their bonds to investors, but bootstrapping liquidity and regulation can be a challenge.

---

In case you are interested in building or supporting this - DM me at @yashhsm on Twitter/Telegram - happy to help/introduce.
resources:
\- Siemens issues corporate bond of $65M on Public chain -  <https://www.ledgerinsights.com/siemens-digital-bond-blockchain/>
\- Understanding On-chain securitisation - <https://www.notboring.co/p/everything-is-broken>
\- Similar Projects
    \- Bridgesplit - <https://www.bridgesplit.com/>
    \- Bond Protocol - <https://www.bondprotocol.finance/>
    \- Ondo Finance - <https://ondo.finance/>
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

In the traditional world, the current process of issuing a corporate bond market is as follows: Planning & Structuring -> Underwriting -> Registration -> Marketing -> Pricing -> Closing -> Secondary Trading. The notional value of the bond market is $128 Trillion, but the process is slow and inefficient, limiting the accessibility to a large number of retail as well as institutional investors. Blockchains like Solana enable the automatic creation of efficient markets, solving for liquidity and accessibility.

Possible Solution:

There can be a few ways to solve this: 1. Issuance and tracking of Bond On-chain, Payments Off-chain: - No Regulatory Hassle as an only ledger entry. - Opportunity to onboard all existing corporate bond issuers to issue SPL bond tokens for greater transparency. - The payments are done via Fiat, and no crypto is involved - This model is easy to Implement and attract clients -> Later monetize 2. Both Issuance and Payments On-chain [Tokenisation of existing Bonds] - The Investors can invest via USDC; it can be made permissioned to allow for KYC or accredited investors - Utilizing existing protocols/Infra on Solana: Pricing of Bonds via AMMs, Streaming payments to bond investors, Multi-sig -> Power of Composability - Target clients can be: Large companies in tokenization-friendly jurisdictions looking to finance massive projects. - This model will take time, but a massive opportunity. The Platform: Bond as a Service It should enable the creation of Bond Markets for anyone, and they should be able to configure based on the type of bonds, like vesting schedule, payment terms, and so on. The platform should be able to plug in or simply fork existing projects on Solana to leverage the already built markets Infra. The marketplace model (Amazon for bonds) can also be adopted, where the issuers can issue and sell their bonds to investors, but bootstrapping liquidity and regulation can be a challenge. --- In case you are interested in building or supporting this - DM me at @yashhsm on Twitter/Telegram - happy to help/introduce.

- Siemens issues corporate bond of $65M on Public chain - https://www.ledgerinsights.com/siemens-digital-bond-blockchain/ - Understanding On-chain securitisation - https://www.notboring.co/p/everything-is-broken - Similar Projects - Bridgesplit - https://www.bridgesplit.com/ - Bond Protocol - https://www.bondprotocol.finance/ - Ondo Finance - https://ondo.finance/

name: Crypto Tiktok
description:
Solana truly loves Betting and Prediction Markets. 

Drift BET did $20m in 24-hour volume after one week of being live.
possible_solution:
Every creator gets a ticker and a bonding curve. Degens can bet on the creator early if they think they are good and will become more popular.

Additional benefits of holding a creator’s coin could be early access to new content and / or private chat (so yes, like FT).

To push this idea even further, every video could have a memecoin associated with it. Like the video? Buy the coin, assuming others will follow. It’s a pure attention asset, the coin has no intrinsic value, but neither do the 100,000s of coins created daily on pump.fun and people seem okay with that. Again, it’s just about aggregating attention.

For safety reasons could prevent bets above X size, and force users to KYC to ensure they don’t create multiple accounts (probably a later stage problem).
resources: 1\. Full Essay with GTM and Biz Model Ideas

name: Solana Jobs-to-be-Done
description:
In the Solana ecosystem, the challenge lies in the need for custom and sovereign blockspace rather than generalized L2 solutions. Solana’s unique strength is its composability, making it ideal for hardware-specific or hyper-optimized tasks. 

This specificity is crucial, as it ensures that Solana’s L2 solutions can handle scaling, upgrades, reliability, and security automatically while also managing the operational work associated with running a rollup in production on the mainnet, such as accounting and compliance. 

We believe an optimal solution here is Solana-aligned.
possible_solution:
As the Solana ecosystem continues to evolve, balancing competition and collaboration will be essential for the sustainable growth of rollup frameworks and RaaS providers. 

Increasing activity on Solana leads to enhanced liquidity, capital, and volumes due to its composability, though it also introduces challenges like high infrastructure costs and congestion. 

However, with the right tools and support, Solana’s ecosystem can harness its unique strengths, providing specialized L2 solutions that meet the specific needs of its developers and users.
resources:
1. Example companies (existing and potential): MagicBlock (Ephemeral Rollups), Solforge, Sovereign Labs
2. Further readings:

name: GPT Arbitration
description: Disputes in a startup society can be challenging to resolve due to subjective biases, lack of expertise, or inefficiencies in traditional arbitration methods.
possible_solution:
A GPT bot for online arbitration of conflict. Train an AI on legal documents over the years. The documents used to train should have been retrieved from trust worthy sources. The startup society approves amends to the constitution via onchain voting. When a dispute occurs, the GPT is asked to provide a judgement based on the data it was trained on, the constitution that has been created by the startup society and evidences submitted by the involved parties.

The data used to train the model, and the evidences submitted by the parties must be from authentic sources as proven by Reclaim Protocol.
resources:
\- Reclaim Protocol Docs: <https://docs.reclaimprotocol.org>
\- Reclaim Protocol Blog: <https://blog.reclaimprotocol.org>
\- The Network State Book: <https://thenetworkstate.com>
\- Reclaim Protocol’s Discord: <https://discord.com/invite/Y9sC929gNS>

name: Coupon Marketplace
description: Buyers often miss out on great deals and discounts because they either don't know about them or can't access them easily. Sellers, on the other hand, struggle to attract customers despite having enticing offers. Additionally, buyers might not always have the funds available to take advantage of these deals when they arise.
possible_solution: Introducing a dynamic marketplace powered by ONDC that connects buyers and sellers through a seamless coupon and offer-sharing platform. This marketplace not only showcases a variety of deals but also incorporates a funding mechanism. This way, buyers can pool resources to unlock even bigger discounts, and sellers can attract a larger customer base.

name: Generative Crypto Agents
description: Build a simulation in which autonomous agents are prompted to interact with other autonomous agents and trade tokens with each other.
possible_solution: For example, mint a fixed supply of a new token called SIM. Initialize 10 agents where each agent controls a private key with a fixed amount of SIM. Give each agent a specific role, such as farmer, baker, and grocer. Instruct each agent to live their day-to-day lives and use their SIM tokens to barter with each other. Run the simulation and observe results.
resources: \- Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior - <https://arxiv.org/abs/2304.03442>
article_content:

name: Wallet integration to Meteora Dynamic Vaults
description: Wallets serve as the gateways for crypto users, housing their assets. Users are keen on earning yield from their idle assets. These wallets could be seamlessly integrated to allow users to deposit their idle assets with a single click and start earning a yield.
possible_solution:
Meteora facilitates users and integrated protocols in depositing their funds into the Dynamic Vaults Pool. These funds are subsequently allocated to top lending protocols like Marginfi and Solend, generating yield. A wallet integration would automatically direct a user's idle funds to Meteora to earn yield, with automatic withdrawal when the user needs to take action, such as transferring to another wallet or swapping for a different token.

The target wallets for integration could include Phantom, Backpack, Glow, Ottr Finance, Ultimate, and Solflare.

name: Fiat to any SPL token Widget
description: Most of the on-ramp/off-ramp (fiat <> crypto) players have only three major SPL tokens listed: USDC, USDT, and SOL, as these are the most traded and liquid assets on Solana. However, this prevents the exposure of other Solana tokens to first-time users that are not listed on these on/off-ramps.
possible_solution:
\- Develop a widget similar to the Jup.ag widget.
\- Users should have the option to select an on/off-ramp provider, such as Moonpay or Ramp, along with any token listed by Jupiter.
\- Upon clicking the "swap" button, the selected token should be received in the user's wallet.
\- In the backend, fiat should be converted to the most liquid asset, such as USDC, which can then be automatically converted to the chosen token using Jupiter APIs.
\- To achieve comprehensive on/off-ramp coverage, it's advisable to utilize an on/off-ramp aggregator like Worldline or Onramper.
resources: \- Jupiter APIs - <https://station.jup.ag/docs/apis>

description: Games have incredible use cases for Token Extensions as all games want flexibility in representing different gaming assets while having composability with DEXs for higher liquidity.
possible_solution:
For instance, Blessed Burgers — A Solana-based Squid Game, powered by Token Extensions-based protocol called epPlex, which are ephemeral NFTs, programmed to self-destruct. It makes use of TokenMetadata Extension (On-chain voting), TransferHook Extension, Permanent Delegate Extension: Purge & burn NFT, and CloseAuthority (Closing Mint accounts).

Fluxbeam is developing another funny on-chain strategy game, called Chronokingdoms using Token Extensions.

More token extensions can be mixed and matched to allow a higher feature set to tokens.

name: Web3 Discord for Communities
description:
Discord is facing significant challenges, especially among web3 communities that hinder secure and meaningful community interactions. These challenges include:
\- Insecurity and Lack of Accountability: Discord lacks robust security measures, making it susceptible to malicious activities and impersonation.
\- Difficulty in Controlling Channel Access: Discord lacks efficient gating mechanisms for channels, leading to difficulties in managing access and participation.
\- Lack of User Reputation Validation: Discord lacks an on-chain reputation system to assess user credibility and contribution within the community.
\- Absence of Native Rewards for Positive Actions: Users who actively contribute and help others are not recognized or rewarded, resulting in a lack of motivation and community growth.
possible_solution: The ultimate web3 Discord platform should be leveraging an open social tech stack such as Orbis to create a secure, transparent, and engaging web3 community platform. By introducing transparent action (using an open network to verify user social action based on their onchain Solana identity), effective gating mechanisms, leveraging on-chain Solana activity for user reputation, and implementing native rewards, the platform will foster trust, incentivization, and recognition among community members.
resources:
\- Orbis general overview: <https://useorbis.com>
\- Orbis documentation: <https://docs.useorbis.com>

Possible Solution:

The ultimate web3 Discord platform should be leveraging an open social tech stack such as Orbis to create a secure, transparent, and engaging web3 community platform. By introducing transparent action (using an open network to verify user social action based on their onchain Solana identity), effective gating mechanisms, leveraging on-chain Solana activity for user reputation, and implementing native rewards, the platform will foster trust, incentivization, and recognition among community members.

name: A Better Ticketmaster
description:
You have been buying tickets to your favorite artists' concerts since ages. Despite your dedication, you are not rewarded for your loyalty by the ticketing platforms. Ticket fraud and ticket scalping (the practice of buying tickets and reselling them at a higher price) can also be major challenges for ticketing companies.

Platforms like Ticketmaster are ripe for disruption.
possible_solution:
An opportunity for artists to engage with their audience directly. Give purchasers exclusive collectibles that can serve as POAPs as well as a reward for loyalty thorough attendance.
If an event sells out, artists can put a clause in their contracts in order to receive a percentage of the proceeds if someone resells the ticket. This helps to prevent ticket scalping and increase revenue for the artist.
resources:
\- Leading Web3 Ticketing Projects - <https://mirror.xyz/0x75D83199fDD34a628C34B83995bE2a32523A38e3/4H8Nq9BGbwfZSPkZ-K0aQjF03vsSWjcy5Wh_8QnMw74>
\- Creating NFTs using Metaplex - <https://www.soldev.app/course/nfts-with-metaplex>
\- Metaplex Docs - <https://docs.metaplex.com/>

name: Onchain Cap Table
description:
For twenty years, we’ve been liking, poking, and tweeting at each other on social media. But man does not live by threads alone. There’s an entire dimension of human existence that has been missing from social media — the economic plane.


Yes, yes, of course you are pestered with ads. But that’s a one way thing. The platforms are getting the economics out of you. Your economics are missing. Your property rights are too. You aren’t really able to create wealth directly on social media, aside from maybe getting a small share of ad revenue.

You can create information, though. You can send anything from the tiniest text file to the largest dataset to anyone online. Or many anyones. And you can collaboratively edit with them, anywhere, at any time, in no time. Indeed, we now take for granted that any person on the internet can create and send information across borders in many ways — from writing simple emails to running sophisticated peer-to-peer programs.

But you still can’t easily create value. It takes days to open bank accounts, send international wires, or start companies. You can’t gather a group of friends from around the world into a group chat, hit a button to start an organization, hit another button to accept international wires, and start selling software within seconds.

Yet.

Cryptocurrency is changing that. Anyone with a Solana address can now send and receive funds to anyone else with a Solana address, within seconds, anywhere in the world, without any previous setup. So at least the transaction part is there. More than there, in fact, because very complicated financial transactions are now routinely facilitated on chain by smart contracts.

The next step is to move from individual transactions and smart contracts to fully on-chain organizations, which are in a sense collections of many contracts. And that brings us to DAOs and network states.
possible_solution:
Mirror a cap table on-chain, then use that as input to calculate a liquidation waterfall, and then use that as input to a contract that handles payouts via SNS.

You could even show a proof of concept for “automated M&A” by having one mocked-up on-chain entity acquire another and distribute proceeds according to that waterfall.
resources:
\- Echo: <https://x.com/echodotxyz>
\- Mirror Table: <https://balajis.com/p/mirrortable>
\- DAOs and Network States: <https://superteam.substack.com/p/daos-and-network-states>
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

For twenty years, we’ve been liking, poking, and tweeting at each other on social media. But man does not live by threads alone. There’s an entire dimension of human existence that has been missing from social media — the economic plane. Yes, yes, of course you are pestered with ads. But that’s a one way thing. The platforms are getting the economics out of you. Your economics are missing. Your property rights are too. You aren’t really able to create wealth directly on social media, aside from maybe getting a small share of ad revenue. You can create information, though. You can send anything from the tiniest text file to the largest dataset to anyone online. Or many anyones. And you can collaboratively edit with them, anywhere, at any time, in no time. Indeed, we now take for granted that any person on the internet can create and send information across borders in many ways — from writing simple emails to running sophisticated peer-to-peer programs. But you still can’t easily create value. It takes days to open bank accounts, send international wires, or start companies. You can’t gather a group of friends from around the world into a group chat, hit a button to start an organization, hit another button to accept international wires, and start selling software within seconds. Yet. Cryptocurrency is changing that. Anyone with a Solana address can now send and receive funds to anyone else with a Solana address, within seconds, anywhere in the world, without any previous setup. So at least the transaction part is there. More than there, in fact, because very complicated financial transactions are now routinely facilitated on chain by smart contracts. The next step is to move from individual transactions and smart contracts to fully on-chain organizations, which are in a sense collections of many contracts. And that brings us to DAOs and network states.

Possible Solution:

Mirror a cap table on-chain, then use that as input to calculate a liquidation waterfall, and then use that as input to a contract that handles payouts via SNS. You could even show a proof of concept for “automated M&A” by having one mocked-up on-chain entity acquire another and distribute proceeds according to that waterfall.

author: Akshay BD

name: Content Authenticity
description: ChatGPT may have stolen the spot light from generative AI models that focus on generating pictures, audio, and videos. However, these models are currently capable of generating realistic deepfakes. The recent AI-generated Drake song is a good example of what these models can achieve. As humans are programmed to believe what they see and hear, these deepfakes represent a significant threat.
possible_solution: In Web3, transactions are signed by the user’s private key to prove their validity. Similarly, content whether it be text, pictures, audio, or video can also be signed by the creator’s private key to prove its authenticity. Anyone can verify the signatures against the creator’s public address which is provided on the creator’s website or social media accounts. Web3 networks have already built all the infrastructure needed for this use case. Many reputable VCs and hackers are already linking their existing social media profiles to a cryptographic public address which gives credibility to the use of digital signatures as a method of content authentication.
resources: N/A
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

ChatGPT may have stolen the spot light from generative AI models that focus on generating pictures, audio, and videos. However, these models are currently capable of generating realistic deepfakes. The recent AI-generated Drake song is a good example of what these models can achieve. As humans are programmed to believe what they see and hear, these deepfakes represent a significant threat.

Possible Solution:

In Web3, transactions are signed by the user’s private key to prove their validity. Similarly, content whether it be text, pictures, audio, or video can also be signed by the creator’s private key to prove its authenticity. Anyone can verify the signatures against the creator’s public address which is provided on the creator’s website or social media accounts. Web3 networks have already built all the infrastructure needed for this use case. Many reputable VCs and hackers are already linking their existing social media profiles to a cryptographic public address which gives credibility to the use of digital signatures as a method of content authentication.

N/A
author: AllianceDAO

name: RWA Collateral Lending
description: A different spin on RWAs. Allowing off-chain assets, such as real-estate, treasury bills, or other RWAs to to serve as collateral.
possible_solution:
The obvious way is to tokenize the asset and treat it as collateral on-chain. An example is 4K tokenizing a watch, and using the watch NFT on Arcade to take out a USDC loan. With all asset classes in the world on a single ledger, we can dramatically improve lending/borrowing experience. Any asset can be collateralized to borrow any other asset. This is something not possible before crypto.

But there is also a possibility to forego the tokenization. Maybe just keep an oracle, or maybe even less. An example is Maker. They've effectively created a collateralized debt position, DAI, partially backed by RWAs (treasury custodians), but their RWA positions are not tokenized.

Another potential example is unlocking a fund's RWA capital efficiency by making use of it in crypto, without having to fully tokenize it.
resources: 1\. State of RWAs on Solana, 2023 - by Yash Agarwal
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

A different spin on RWAs. Allowing off-chain assets, such as real-estate, treasury bills, or other RWAs to to serve as collateral.

Possible Solution:

The obvious way is to tokenize the asset and treat it as collateral on-chain. An example is 4K tokenizing a watch, and using the watch NFT on Arcade to take out a USDC loan. With all asset classes in the world on a single ledger, we can dramatically improve lending/borrowing experience. Any asset can be collateralized to borrow any other asset. This is something not possible before crypto. But there is also a possibility to forego the tokenization. Maybe just keep an oracle, or maybe even less. An example is Maker. They've effectively created a collateralized debt position, DAI, partially backed by RWAs (treasury custodians), but their RWA positions are not tokenized. Another potential example is unlocking a fund's RWA capital efficiency by making use of it in crypto, without having to fully tokenize it.

1. State of RWAs on Solana, 2023 - by Yash Agarwal

name: Composable NFT Loyalty
description:
Be it Web3-native or Web2 brands, enabling them to launch a tokenized loyalty program powered by NFTs, easily transferrable across all ecosystems, is the next big viable consumer opportunity. This can onboard millions and even billions into the crypto via a non-speculative use case!

Why should composable NFT loyalty be built?

The underlying technology of NFTs unlock:
1\. Earnabilty: Customers can earn points by completing activities beyond spending to deepen their relationship with the brand.
2\. Utility: Customers can unlock more valuable rewards, products, and experiences as all actions are tied to on-chain information.
3\. Incentive Alignment: NFTs allow customers to own their loyalty status as a unique asset that appreciates value as they interact with the brand.
possible_solution:
1\. NFT-gated commerce: Exclusive access via NFTs for a premium cohort of users
2\. Tiered loyalty levels and leaderboards based on the volume of transactions
3\. Incentivise user actions like referrals and sign-ups with loyalty tokens
resources:
\- ASICS x Solana Loyalty Program - <https://solana.com/news/case-study-asics-solana-pay>
\- Starbucks x Polygon Loyalty Program - <https://techcrunch.com/2022/12/08/starbucks-opens-up-its-blockchain-based-loyalty-program-and-nft-community-to-first-beta-testers/>
\- Salesforce x Polygon - <https://www.coindesk.com/business/2023/03/17/polygon-partners-with-salesforce-for-nft-based-loyalty-program/>
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Be it Web3-native or Web2 brands, enabling them to launch a tokenized loyalty program powered by NFTs, easily transferrable across all ecosystems, is the next big viable consumer opportunity. This can onboard millions and even billions into the crypto via a non-speculative use case! Why should composable NFT loyalty be built? The underlying technology of NFTs unlock: 1. Earnabilty: Customers can earn points by completing activities beyond spending to deepen their relationship with the brand. 2. Utility: Customers can unlock more valuable rewards, products, and experiences as all actions are tied to on-chain information. 3. Incentive Alignment: NFTs allow customers to own their loyalty status as a unique asset that appreciates value as they interact with the brand.

Possible Solution:

1. NFT-gated commerce: Exclusive access via NFTs for a premium cohort of users 2. Tiered loyalty levels and leaderboards based on the volume of transactions 3. Incentivise user actions like referrals and sign-ups with loyalty tokens

name: Tokenizing Precious Metals
description: Buying precious metals like gold in traditional forms often requires a significant upfront investment, limiting accessibility for many people.
possible_solution: These metals can be tokenized, breaking them into smaller, more affordable fractions. Each token represents a share of the actual metal, making it easier for users to invest in increments that suit their budget.

name: Identicore: Decentralized Identity for Physical AI
description: To ensure Identicore succeeds in solving the problems of insecure, non-interoperable, and untrusted identities for Physical AI while fostering fairness and transparency, the following critical considerations must be addressed.
possible_solution: Identicore addresses the challenges of insecure, non-interoperable, and untrusted identities for Physical AI (e.g., robots, drones, IoT devices) by providing a Decentralized Identifier (DID) framework integrated with blockchain, AI, and IoT technologies. This solution ensures secure, interoperable, and trusted identities, enabling privacy-preserving data sharing, seamless cross-platform interactions, and autonomous transactions in Web3, DePIN, and IoT ecosystems.
resources: DocumentationW3C Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs) v1.0Description: Official W3C standard for DIDs, detailing how to create, manage, and verify decentralized identifiers for entities like IoT devices. Essential for ensuring Identicore’s interoperability.Link: https://www.w3.org/TR/did-core/Why: Provides the foundational standard for Identicore’s DID system, ensuring global compatibility.W3C Verifiable Credentials Data ModelDescription: Specifies how to issue, store, and verify credentials (e.g., a drone’s certification) using DIDs, critical for trust in Physical AI interactions.Link: https://www.w3.org/TR/vc-data-model/Why: Guides Identicore’s credential system for secure, privacy-preserving verification.IOTA Identity FrameworkDescription: Documentation for IOTA’s DID implementation using Tangle, optimized for IoT with low-cost, energy-efficient transactions.Link: https://wiki.iota.org/identity.rs/introduction/Why: Ideal for Identicore’s IoT use cases (e.g., agricultural drones), aligning with your focus on scalable, low-cost solutions.Polygon ID DocumentationDescription: Guides for building zero-knowledge proof (ZKP)-based DID systems on Polygon, including SDKs and APIs for privacy-preserving identity.Link: https://docs.polygonid.com/Why: Supports Identicore’s ZKP integration for privacy, relevant for global markets like Europe (GDPR).Hyperledger Aries DocumentationDescription: Framework for DID-based identity management, with tools for secure communication and credential exchange.Link: https://www.hyperledger.org/projects/ariesWhy: Useful for enterprise-grade applications (e.g., logistics, smart cities), aligning with your global ambitions.GitHub RepositoriesIoTeX Network (iotexproject)Description: Full-stack infrastructure for DePIN, including ioID for DID-based identity management and encrypted communication for IoT devices.Link: https://github.com/iotexprojectWhy: Provides code and examples for Identicore’s DePIN use cases (e.g., logistics robots), with a focus on scalability (1000 TPS).Veramo (veramo/veramo)Description: Modular APIs for DID creation, credential issuance, and SSI (Self-Sovereign Identity) management, supporting multiple blockchains.Link: https://github.com/veramo/veramoWhy: Simplifies Identicore’s DID implementation, offering flexibility for cross-chain interoperability.IOTA Identity (iotaledger/identity.rs)Description: Rust-based implementation of DIDs and verifiable credentials on IOTA’s Tangle, optimized for IoT devices.Link: https://github.com/iotaledger/identity.rsWhy: Perfect for Identicore’s low-resource IoT applications (e.g., Indonesian agricultural drones).Web3.js (web3/web3.js)Description: JavaScript library for interacting with Ethereum and EVM-compatible blockchains, useful for smart contract integration.Link: https://github.com/web3/web3.jsWhy: Enables Identicore’s blockchain interactions (e.g., wallet transactions), aligning with your experience in EVM ecosystems (Tacitus, 8 March 2025).Hardhat (nomiclabs/hardhat)Description: Development environment for Ethereum smart contracts, with testing and deployment tools.Link: https://github.com/nomiclabs/hardhatWhy: Streamlines Identicore’s smart contract development, leveraging your familiarity with Hardhat (Tacitus, 8 March 2025).Developer ToolsRemix IDEDescription: Browser-based IDE for writing, testing, and deploying Solidity smart contracts for Identicore’s DID and credential systems.Link: https://remix.ethereum.org/Why: User-friendly for rapid prototyping, especially for your Ethereum-based experiments (CogniHash, 11 April 2025).Truffle SuiteDescription: Development framework for Ethereum, offering testing, debugging, and deployment tools for smart contracts.Link: https://trufflesuite.com/Why: Complements Hardhat for robust contract development, ensuring security for Identicore’s blockchain components.MetamaskDescription: Browser extension for managing crypto wallets, enabling device wallets for Identicore’s machine economy transactions.Link: https://metamask.io/Why: Simplifies wallet integration, aligning with your interest in user-friendly Web3 tools (13 April 2025).IPFSDescription: Decentralized storage protocol for storing DID documents and verifiable credentials, ensuring data availability.Link: https://ipfs.io/Why: Supports Identicore’s decentralized data storage, critical for privacy and scalability.MQTT.jsDescription: JavaScript library for MQTT protocol, enabling lightweight IoT communication for Identicore’s devices.Link: https://github.com/mqttjs/MQTT.jsWhy: Ideal for real-time data exchange in IoT use cases (e.g., drone sensor data), fitting your agrikultur focus.Essays and Research Papers“Decentralized Identifiers for IoT Systems: Current Status and Future Vision” (SpringerLink, 2023)Description: Explores IOTA’s Tangle-based DID system for IoT, addressing scalability and energy efficiency challenges in traditional blockchains.Link: https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11277-023-10387-xWhy: Provides insights into low-cost, IoT-optimized DIDs, relevant for Identicore’s agricultural drone use case in Indonesia.“A Survey on Decentralized Identifiers and Verifiable Credentials” (arXiv, 2024)Description: Comprehensive overview of DID and VC applications, including IoT and supply chain use cases, with a focus on SSI and blockchain.Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.02698Why: Offers a taxonomy of smart applications, guiding Identicore’s logistics and agrikultur implementations.“Decentralized Identity: The Ultimate Guide 2025” (Dock.io, 2025)Description: Explains decentralized identity models, including SSI, verifiable credentials, and blockchain, with real-world applications.Link: https://www.dock.io/decentralized-identity-guideWhy: Clarifies user-centric identity management, aligning with your community-driven vision (13 April 2025).“Blockchain-Based AI Methods for Managing Industrial IoT” (arXiv, 2024)Description: Surveys AI and blockchain integration for IoT, focusing on security, privacy, and decentralization challenges.Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.14414Why: Relevant for Identicore’s AI-driven Physical AI (e.g., drone navigation), building on your AI interests (Promotheus AI, 12 March 2025).“Leveraging Blockchain Technology for Ensuring Security and Privacy Aspects in IoT” (MDPI, 2023)Description: Systematic review of blockchain-IoT integration, highlighting security and privacy solutions for heterogeneous devices.Link: https://www.mdpi.com/1424-8220/23/2/1093Why: Guides Identicore’s approach to securing IoT devices, critical for trust in global markets.

Problem to Solve:

To ensure Identicore succeeds in solving the problems of insecure, non-interoperable, and untrusted identities for Physical AI while fostering fairness and transparency, the following critical considerations must be addressed.

Possible Solution:

Identicore addresses the challenges of insecure, non-interoperable, and untrusted identities for Physical AI (e.g., robots, drones, IoT devices) by providing a Decentralized Identifier (DID) framework integrated with blockchain, AI, and IoT technologies. name: Access crypto without a smart phone
description: One of the main challenges in accessing the web3 ecosystem is the need for consistent access to a personal computer or smart phone. However, not everyone has access to these types of devices, which can limit their ability to participate.
possible_solution:
We’re interested in projects that enable people with limited access to technology to be able to access the ecosystem and enjoy its benefits. Possible projects might include accessing web3 with:
\- Flip phones
\- Public computers (for example, safe and secure onramps in public libraries)
resources: \- Solana Docs - <https://docs.solana.com/>

Possible Solution:

We’re interested in projects that enable people with limited access to technology to be able to access the ecosystem and enjoy its benefits. Possible projects might include accessing web3 with: - Flip phones - Public computers (for example, safe and secure onramps in public libraries)

name: Social sports betting
description: Sports watching is at its most fun when done with & around friends. However, there is no easy way to bet on sports alongside your friends, especially if everyone is located in a different city or country.
possible_solution: One solution would be a product that allows you and your friends to form a ‘syndicate’ and bet in a transparent manner. Perhaps a solution that allows multiple users to share one account, and make betting decisions collectively. Another solution would be to allow users to "tag along" and automatically place bets on their behalf, mirroring the selections of the "syndicate lead"  gambler. The goal is to make sports betting more social, and easier to do collectively amongst friends.
resources:
\- Monaco Protocol - <https://github.com/MonacoProtocol>
\- Monaco Protocol Docs - <https://docs.monacoprotocol.xyz/>
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Sports watching is at its most fun when done with & around friends. However, there is no easy way to bet on sports alongside your friends, especially if everyone is located in a different city or country.

Possible Solution:

One solution would be a product that allows you and your friends to form a ‘syndicate’ and bet in a transparent manner. Perhaps a solution that allows multiple users to share one account, and make betting decisions collectively. Another solution would be to allow users to "tag along" and automatically place bets on their behalf, mirroring the selections of the "syndicate lead" gambler. The goal is to make sports betting more social, and easier to do collectively amongst friends.

name: Stablecoin clearing house
description: With the advent of more stablecoins on Solana, we will need a clearing house to allow interoperability between all these stablecoins
possible_solution: Ability to swap stables at fixed cost on a 1:1 basis
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

With the advent of more stablecoins on Solana, we will need a clearing house to allow interoperability between all these stablecoins

Possible Solution:

Ability to swap stables at fixed cost on a 1:1 basis

name: Decentralized Scale AI
description: Scraped data and other data collected in the wild is of no use, if they can’t be cleaned up and annotated for specific needs. Labeled and annotated datasets are the bread and butter for any supervised AI models.
possible_solution:
AI companies use a variety of solutions like scaleAI, SnorkelAI, DataloopAI, Amazon Mechanical Turk -- to label their datasets. All of these are valued at ~$1 Billion. 

The major blocker to scaling something like this is building up the supply of labelers and handling hundreds of thousands of daily payments and Solana is uniquely suited for this use case.
resources: 1\. AI x Web3, by AllianceDAO

name: Egocentric Vision Data Collection
description:
Quality data is a bottleneck in designing general purpose robots — not to mention task-specific robots. Egocentric vision data, in particular, is one relatively new approach.

While it is part of computational intelligence, it can meaningfully augment physical intelligence by providing the necessary sensory input to guide physical actions.
possible_solution: We see an opportunity to crowdsource egocentric vision data which can be coordinated and incentivized via crypto platforms.
resources:
1. Example companies: [Mecka.ai](http://mecka.ai/)
2. Further reading: [Learning Robot Soccer from Egocentric Vision with Deep Reinforcement Learning](https://capital.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6f895fcc6df9ca0df180735e3&id=3ffafeaf0c&e=5c51df9638)

name: Linkedin version of friend.tech
description: Twitter (X), LinkedIn, or any social media platform has been grappling with the issue of spam-filled direct messages (DMs), leading to the risk of important messages being overlooked. To address this, a feature could be created that requires individuals to make a payment before sending a DM to another user.
possible_solution:
There are several potential solutions to the spamming issue:
1\. For instance, consider a scenario where one must pay 100 USDC to send a DM to Elon Musk. If Elon Musk responds, the 100 USDC is refunded to the user. The bidirectional nature of cryptocurrency makes this easily programmable. This approach could potentially make Twitter free of spam by discouraging spammy behavior.
2\. Think of this as the LinkedIn version of the friend request. An email inbox that prioritizes messages based on the amount of $USDC sent to me, and users could set a minimum price that guarantees a response.
resources:
\- Idea by Anatoly - <https://twitter.com/aeyakovenko/status/1693656808865546300?s=20>
\- Sollinked - <https://x.com/Sollinked_com>
\- time.fun - <https://x.com/timedotfun>
\- Orb Land - <https://orb.land/>
\- TipLink docs - <https://docs.tiplink.io/>
article_content:
Problem to Solve:

Twitter (X), LinkedIn, or any social media platform has been grappling with the issue of spam-filled direct messages (DMs), leading to the risk of important messages being overlooked. To address this, a feature could be created that requires individuals to make a payment before sending a DM to another user.

Possible Solution:

There are several potential solutions to the spamming issue: 1. For instance, consider a scenario where one must pay 100 USDC to send a DM to Elon Musk. If Elon Musk responds, the 100 USDC is refunded to the user. The bidirectional nature of cryptocurrency makes this easily programmable. This approach could potentially make Twitter free of spam by discouraging spammy behavior. 2. Think of this as the LinkedIn version of the friend request. An email inbox that prioritizes messages based on the amount of $USDC sent to me, and users could set a minimum price that guarantees a response.
